脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sandra G脙鲁mez L脙鲁pez (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, en la madrugada del 16 de noviembre se lleg脙鲁 al acuerdo de pactar y cerrar un presupuesto de casi 200脗聽000 millones de euros; un acuerdo que no fue f脙隆cil de obtener, en primer lugar, porque era el primer presupuesto que pact脙隆bamos y cerr脙隆bamos tras la revisi脙鲁n del marco financiero a largo plazo. Y, en segundo lugar, porque tambi脙漏n era el primer presupuesto en el que los costes de los intereses del programa NextGenerationEU eran superiores a los previstos.

Como Parlamento, ten脙颅amos absolutamente claro que eso no pod脙颅a afectar a iniciativas clave como Erasmus+ u Horizonte Europa. Por eso, pactamos llevar a cabo el 脗芦mecanismo en cascada脗禄 del Instrumento de Recuperaci脙鲁n de la UE (IRUE) para, precisamente, poder gestionar esos costes de endeudamiento de los NextGenerationEU sin afectar a ninguna de estas iniciativas clave, mientras segu脙颅amos teniendo, como presupuesto, flexibilidad y capacidad de respuesta a cualquier imprevisto.

Hemos hecho y hemos pactado un presupuesto que pretende dar respuesta a cuestiones que hoy son desaf脙颅os clave para la Uni脙鲁n Europea 芒聙聲cuestiones como el cambio clim脙隆tico o los retos sanitarios芒聙聲, dar una soluci脙鲁n y una alternativa a todas las zonas rurales, a la agricultura y a nuestros j脙鲁venes, e impulsar la competitividad en Europa.

Pero, adem脙隆s, pretende dar respuesta a la emergencia de la crisis que estamos viviendo pa脙颅ses como el m脙颅o, Espa脙卤a 芒聙聲pensemos en Valencia芒聙聲, pactando, por primera vez, un presupuesto que incluye una reserva de m脙隆s de 3脗聽000 millones de euros para que, desde ya, cualquier pa脙颅s que tenga un problema y una cat脙隆strofe natural pueda, a partir del a脙卤o 2025, tener recursos suficientes para poder atender la emergencia...

(la presidenta retira la palabra a la oradora)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Auke Zijlstra (PfE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, ik was schaduwrapporteur namens de Patriots, de derde partij in dit Parlement. Dat was een interessante ervaring. De rapporteur gooide ongelezen mijn zorgvuldig opgestelde amendementen in de prullenbak en beweerde later bij de stemming in de Begrotingscommissie dat hij met alle partijen succesvol had overlegd. Dat succes bleek vervolgens niet uit de uitslag op de plenaire vergadering, waar de resolutie juist werd weggestemd. Een verdeeld Parlement.

In de discussie met de Raad en de Commissie werd een sterk gegroeide begroting vastgesteld, bijna 200脗聽miljard. In de Raad onthielden vijf landen zich vervolgens van stemming en stemde Zweden zelfs tegen. Ook een verdeelde Raad dus.

Vervolgens wordt het eindresultaat hier als een overwinning gevierd. Zoals de rapporteur zelf stelde: het Parlement heeft de traditie en de plicht om begrotingen altijd te verhogen. Dat dit ten koste gaat van de belastingbetalers is geen onderdeel van de discussie. Dat de Europese Commissie geen rekening had gehouden met het feit dat geld lenen geld kost, is ook belachelijk. En ook die rekening gaat nu naar de belastingbetaler. De Raad wilde graag prudent begroten en dat is duidelijk niet gelukt. De Europese Commissie plant nu zelfs 3脗聽miljard aan boetes in om de kosten te dekken. Boetes die nog helemaal niet bestaan!

Volgend jaar start het overleg over de nieuwe meerjarenbegroting, met een verdeelde Raad en een verdeeld Parlement en een Commissie die niet met schulden kan omgaan. Dat is een treurig beeld. Bijna zo treurig als de economische vooruitzichten van de Europese Unie, die met deze begroting niet zullen veranderen. Als de rapporteur de uitgangspunten van de Raad had gevolgd, zouden wij voor hebben gestemd. U begrijpt dat wij nu tegen zullen stemmen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ruggero Razza (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, il 22 novembre, qualche giorno fam a Parigi si sono riunite le principali associazioni imprenditoriali di Francia, Germania e Italia, che da sole rappresentano il 53% del prodotto interno lordo dell'Unione.

Hanno chiesto all'Europa un intervento straordinario in competizione, hanno chiesto di recuperare i tempi perduti, hanno chiesto di guardare al futuro della competitivit脙聽 del nostro sistema imprenditoriale.

E a loro dobbiamo dire, mentre ci apprestiamo a votare questo bilancio, che la risposta non arriva da questo bilancio. Certo, ci sono dei significativi miglioramenti: c'脙篓 un lavoro che ha fatto il Parlamento, c'脙篓 un recupero dei tagli che il Consiglio aveva proposto alla Commissione. Ma non c'脙篓 quell'intervento straordinario anche inserito nella relazione Draghi.

Bastano i numeri: 200 miliardi il bilancio, 800 miliardi le attese. Dobbiamo lavorare molto e bisogner脙聽 fare molto di pi脙鹿 nel futuro.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lucia Yar (Renew). 芒聙聯 V脙隆脜戮en脙隆 pani predsedaj脙潞ca, pr脙隆ve som sa vr脙隆tila z v脙陆chodn脙漏ho Slovenska 芒聙聯 regi脙鲁nu, ktor脙漏mu Eur脙鲁pska 脙潞nia garantuje podporu prostredn脙颅ctvom koh脙漏znej politiky. Politiky na vyrovn脙隆vanie rozdielov medzi regi脙鲁nmi, v脛聫aka ktorej m脙隆 aj tento kraj dobehn脙潞脜楼 z脙隆pad. Namiesto toho som videla odliv mlad脙陆ch 脛戮ud脙颅 do zahrani脛聧ia, nedostatok pracovn脙陆ch miest aj chab脙潞 infra脜隆trukt脙潞ru. Kapitola tejto politiky m脙隆 pritom v bud脙潞coro脛聧nom rozpo脛聧te najvy脜隆脜隆ie pridelen脙漏 zdroje 芒聙聯 a脜戮 40 % z 200 mili脙隆rd.

No realita v ter脙漏ne je in脙隆, niekedy alarmuj脙潞ca. Napriek mno脜戮stvu vy脛聧lenen脙陆ch eurofondov v mnoh脙陆ch obciach st脙隆le ch脙陆ba kanaliz脙隆cia aj pr脙颅stup k vode. A my vieme, 脜戮e toto je probl脙漏m na n脙隆rodnej 脙潞rovni, no pokia脛戮 ide o eur脙鲁pske invest脙颅cie a s脛戮uby, 脛戮udia ot脙隆脛聧aj脙潞 hlavu smerom k Bruselu a 脛戮ud脙颅 nem脙麓脜戮eme tresta脜楼 za to, 脜戮e maj脙潞 neschopn脙潞 vl脙隆du. Ak m脙隆me nap脛潞脜聢a脜楼 na脜隆e spolo脛聧n脙漏 eur脙鲁pske ciele, mus脙颅me zabezpe脛聧i脜楼, aby n脙隆脜隆 rozpo脛聧et bol skuto脛聧ne tak脙陆, aby pom脙隆hal t脙陆m, ktor脙颅 to najviac potrebuj脙潞. Mus脙颅 sa prejavi脜楼 na 脜戮ivotoch 脛戮ud脙颅. A脜戮 vtedy budeme m脙麓c脜楼 naozaj hovori脜楼 o 脙潞spe脜隆nom rozpo脛聧te.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kai Tegethoff (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, under the given circumstances, this compromise for the EU budget 2025 is a very good one. It includes very important top-ups for programmes for climate, biodiversity, humanitarian aid and healthcare, for example. But we also have to be aware that we should have done a lot more.

We need an EU budget for the people, and the structure of the EU budget is completely outdated and urgently needs to be updated. We need a budget that is far more flexible. We need a budget that really follows our goals and values. Right now we are refusing to invest in fire alarms, but at the same time, we cannot afford to rebuild our house once it's burned down.

We finally need mandatory targets, for example for climate mainstreaming and climate adaptation, to finally start building resilient communities. And we need to stop financing dictators. We really need a conditionality for a rule of law in the EU budget. With a flexible budget, we need to understand where our money is spent. So we need to follow the money. Let's reform the EU budget.

脗聽脗聽Isabel Benjumea Benjumea (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, la elaboraci脙鲁n de un presupuesto es una enorme responsabilidad; se trata de decidir a qu脙漏 prioridades vamos a destinar el dinero proveniente de los impuestos de los ciudadanos europeos: 200 000 millones de euros.

La prioridad de la Uni脙鲁n Europea no puede ser financiar los intereses de la deuda NextGenerationEU a costa de recortar recursos a otros programas europeos con alta valoraci脙鲁n ciudadana. Ni mucho menos la prioridad puede ser la imposici脙鲁n de este modelo en el futuro marco financiero cuando el modelo de los fondos NextGenerationEU est脙隆 muy lejos de haber conseguido los ambiciosos objetivos para los que fue creado, con un reducido impacto econ脙鲁mico y un alto nivel de fondos sin ejecutar.

Las prioridades de la UE deben ser la competitividad de nuestras empresas, la seguridad de nuestras fronteras, la inversi脙鲁n en innovaci脙鲁n e investigaci脙鲁n, la eficiencia de la pol脙颅tica de cohesi脙鲁n, la defensa de nuestro sector primario y la reserva de recursos comunitarios para hacer frente a las cat脙隆strofes como la vivida en Valencia.

Este presupuesto que ma脙卤ana votamos las recoge y esto ha sido, en gran parte, gracias al liderazgo del Partido Popular Europeo.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Carla Tavares (S&D). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhor Comiss脙隆rio, a negocia脙搂脙拢o do Or脙搂amento 2025 representa aquilo que n脙拢o queremos para o pr脙鲁ximo quadro financeiro plurianual p脙鲁s 2027. N脙拢o queremos, no or脙搂amento anual, centrar-nos na gest脙拢o do reembolso da d脙颅vida do NextGenerationEU.

Queremos, a partir de 2028, altura em que devemos fazer o reembolso do capital do NextGenerationEU, ter uma solu脙搂脙拢o para o reembolso que se configure num instrumento seguro.

Queremos acordar at脙漏 l脙隆 um perfil do reembolso da d脙颅vida que n脙拢o afete a gest脙拢o anual de programas, que n脙拢o ponha em perigo as prioridades para os europeus, que nos permita, no or脙搂amento anual, dedicarmo-nos 脙聽quelas que s脙拢o as verdadeiras prioridades da Uni脙拢o e o seu refor脙搂o.

Queremos a Uni脙拢o Europeia mais simples e eficaz na sua capacidade de resposta. Um or脙搂amento anual mais transparente, com uma gest脙拢o mais 脙隆gil, focada naquilo que deve ser o valor acrescentado da Uni脙拢o Europeia para os europeus.

Ainda assim, o Parlamento alcan脙搂ou para 2025 importantes refor脙搂os na educa脙搂脙拢o, na sa脙潞de, nas pol脙颅ticas sociais, na ajuda humanit脙隆ria, na prote脙搂脙拢o civil, na investiga脙搂脙拢o, na agricultura e na luta contra a corrup脙搂脙拢o, ao mesmo tempo que encontr脙隆mos uma abordagem equilibrada no financiamento das d脙颅vidas geradas pelo NextGenerationEU.

Num processo negocial dif脙颅cil, o Parlamento provou mais uma vez que 脙漏 capaz de encontrar solu脙搂脙碌es claras para os desafios da Uni脙拢o Europeia e para as expectativas dos nossos cidad脙拢os.

Permitam-me uma palavra de reconhecimento aos dois colegas relatores pelo trabalho desenvolvido e pelos resultados alcan脙搂ados, bem como ao Senhor Comiss脙隆rio e 脙聽 sua equipa, pela prestimosa e sempre disponibilidade.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ang脙漏line Furet (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, la Commission europ脙漏enne continue d'imposer des contraintes budg脙漏taires qui empi脙篓tent sur la souverainet脙漏 des 脙聣tats membres et alimentent la bureaucratie inefficace de Bruxelles.

Dans le cadre du budget pour脗聽2025, les augmentations des d脙漏penses concernent des programmes tels que le pacte vert et la militarisation de l'Union europ脙漏enne, alors que nos citoyens subissent de plein fouet les cons脙漏quences de l'inflation, des crises 脙漏nerg脙漏tiques et de l'immigration massive.

Comment justifiez-vous l'allocation de ces fonds 脙聽 des projets id脙漏ologiques, alors m脙陋me que de nombreux gouvernements nationaux sont contraints de r脙漏duire leurs budgets pour des services essentiels? Est-ce que cela refl脙篓te r脙漏ellement les priorit脙漏s et les pr脙漏occupations des Europ脙漏ens?

Ne serait-il pas plus responsable de redonner aux 脙聣tats membres la ma脙庐trise de leur budget et de cesser de financer des projets qui divisent au lieu de rassembler?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Dick Erixon (ECR). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! Sverige blev nedr脙露stat i r脙楼det. I脗聽st脙陇llet gjorde de tre institutionerna upp om att h脙露ja budgeten och d脙陇rmed l脙陇gga ytterligare b脙露rda p脙楼 medborgarna 芒聙聯 medborgare som har en tuff verklighet sedan maten, bensinen och elen blivit dyrare.

Familjernas hush脙楼llsbudgetar 脙陇r anstr脙陇ngda, n脙楼got som vi borde t脙陇nka p脙楼 i denna kammare d脙楼 vi tar ytterligare resurser fr脙楼n medborgarna n脙陇r EU-budgeten blir st脙露rre.

Ett positivt inslag i 脙楼rets budget 脙陇r dock att fem regeringar i r脙楼det inte r脙露stade f脙露r den slutgiltiga uppg脙露relsen. Sveriges regering tillh脙露rde dem som markerade mot en st脙陇ndigt v脙陇xande EU-budget. Detta hoppas jag blir ett f脙露rsta steg mot en tuffare f脙露rhandling inf脙露r kommande sju脙楼rsbudget. Nu m脙楼ste det bli ett slut p脙楼 expansionen; pengarna g脙露r b脙陇st nytta i medborgarnas h脙陇nder.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Anouk Van Brug (Renew). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, het leven is de afgelopen tijd flink duurder geworden. Dit merken onze inwoners dagelijks als zij hun boodschappen doen, wanneer zij hun energierekening moeten betalen of wanneer zij aan de pomp staan. Ook onze lidstaten merken dit wanneer zij hun begroting opstellen. Geld kun je immers maar 脙漏脙漏n keer uitgeven. Om een sluitende begroting te krijgen, moeten er pijnlijke keuzes gemaakt worden waar landen hun geld wel en vooral ook niet aan uitgeven.

Wij kunnen als Europa niet achterblijven. Het roer moet om. De uitdagingen van vandaag vragen om stevige investeringen in defensie en innovatie. Om geld hiervoor vrij te maken, moeten we andere prioriteiten stellen en bezuinigen als dat nodig is. Laten we samen gezond verstand en realisme terugbrengen in onze begroting. Een begroting die zorgt voor onze veiligheid, die onze welvaart vergroot en waarmee we klaarstaan voor de toekomst.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tilly Metz (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, dear colleagues, Luxembourg, as one of the EU capitals, is home to key EU institutions.

There are strong reasons for why 12 European institutions, agencies and bodies are based in Luxembourg, such as the Court of Justice and the European Court of Auditors. Their location ensures independence away from the political centres of Brussels and Strasbourg.

However, the cost of living in Luxembourg is the highest in Europe, affecting not only residents but also 14脗聽000 EU staff working there. Ensuring proper staffing and functionality of these institutions is vital for the good functioning of the European Union.

So, negotiating a housing allowance is a welcome step. But more is needed. A correction coefficient reflecting Luxembourg's high cost would ensure fairness and protect the attractiveness of these institutions.

Colleagues, supporting staff working in our institution in Luxembourg is not just operational, it is about upholding our shared European values, such as justice and the rule of law.

(The speaker agreed to take a blue-card question)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Maria Grapini (S&D), 脙庐ntrebare adresat脛聝 conform procedurii 芒聙聻cartona脠聶ului albastru芒聙聺. 芒聙聯 Stimat脛聝 doamn脛聝, v脛聝d c脛聝 pl脙垄nge脠聸i nivelul de trai 脠聶i costul ridicat din Luxemburg. 脠聵ti脠聸i dumneavoastr脛聝 脙庐n estul Europei c脙垄脠聸i copii tr脛聝iesc 脙庐n s脛聝r脛聝cie? C脙垄脠聸i oameni au pensionari care nu au cum s脛聝 脙庐脠聶i cumpere medicamentele? Eu cred c脛聝 trebuie s脛聝 ne g脙垄ndim la acest buget, mai ales la eficien脠聸a modului 脙庐n care se cheltuie脠聶te. Nu e suficient s脛聝 dirij脛聝m ni脠聶te bani pentru ni脠聶te domenii, ci este foarte important s脛聝 vedem eficien脠聸a: dac脛聝 s-au alocat la Parchetul European bani 脙庐n plus, s脛聝 vedem cum recupereaz脛聝 evaziunea fiscal脛聝. La orice capitol trebuie asta s脛聝 facem 脠聶i s脛聝 ne g脙垄ndim la cei mai s脛聝raci, nu la Luxemburg, unde nivelul de trai 脠聶i veniturile sunt peste media Uniunii Europene.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tilly Metz (Verts/ALE), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 Thank you very much for that question, but I think you are mixing up things there. Of course, I'm in favour that at the European level we have to fight poverty, of course. In Luxembourg, just to say, one child out of five is at risk of poverty. But that's another issue, I mean, we have to fight that.

Here is a fundamental question that every country in the European Union has a coefficient correcteur, has this corrector coefficient, except Brussels and Luxembourg. And I just want that Luxembourg, the people working there, mainly they are coming from all the countries, have the same rights that in the other European countries, so that they look at the real cost of living that exists in Luxembourg 芒聙聯 that is what this is about.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽H脙漏lder Sousa Silva (PPE). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhor Comiss脙隆rio, Caros Colegas, no contexto geopol脙颅tico em que vivemos, precisamos de um or脙搂amento robusto para garantir uma Europa forte, capaz de agir, mas, essencialmente, defender os nossos valores democr脙隆ticos.

Quero, em primeiro lugar, destacar o resultado obtido pela equipa de negocia脙搂脙拢o do Parlamento, na que tive a honra de participar, pois assegur脙隆mos um aumento de 230 milh脙碌es de EUR no financiamento de programas de particular relev脙垄ncia para os europeus.脗聽Sublinho dois: o Mecanismo Europeu de Prote脙搂脙拢o Civil e a Europol.

Em segundo lugar, quero evidenciar a inclus脙拢o, j脙隆 em 2025, de 3 mil milh脙碌es de EUR para a assist脙陋ncia aos Estados芒聙聭Membros afetados pelas recentes cat脙隆strofes naturais, entre os quais Portugal.

Em terceiro lugar, saliento o acordo para a reintrodu脙搂脙拢o no or脙搂amento de 420 milh脙碌es de EUR para refor脙搂ar o programa Erasmus+ - e vejo uma juventude na plateia -, dedicado essencialmente 脙聽 juventude, que 脙漏 um programa bandeira da Uni脙拢o Europeia.

Em suma, este Or脙搂amento responde 脙聽s necessidades e aspira脙搂脙碌es de todos os europeus. Parab脙漏ns ao Parlamento, 脙聽 Comiss脙拢o e ao Conselho.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Matja脜戮 Nemec (S&D). 芒聙聯 Gospa predsednica. Spo脜隆tovani, hitro spreminjajo脛聧i se svet od Unije terja a脜戮urno in u脛聧inkovito ukrepanje, kar se mora odra脜戮ati tudi v prilagodljivosti prora脛聧una.

Ta je rezultat sodelovanja in kompromisa. S posluhom za potrebe dr脜戮avljanov in kompleksne izzive smo 芒聙聯 in tu moram 脜隆e posebej poudariti 芒聙聯 v Evropskem parlamentu zagotovili 6脗聽% pove脛聧anja prora脛聧una za prihodnje leto. Pozdravljam, da se ta osredoto脛聧a na regije, ki so bile prizadete po poplavah, in predvideva namenska sredstva v vi脜隆ini treh milijard za srednjo in vzhodno Evropo, kamor spada tudi Slovenija. Dodatnih 脜隆est milijard pa bo za poplavno pomo脛聧 namenjenih iz kohezijskih sredstev, kar krepi njihovo vlogo kot klju脛聧nega orodja za podporo izrednim razmeram.

Nujno je, da prora脛聧un podpira usklajeno vizijo za prihodnost Evropske unije. Zato na tem mestu 脜隆e posebej pozdravljam pove脛聧anje sredstev za raziskave in inovacije, za podporo okoljskim in podnebnim ciljem, za krepitev upravljanja migracij in mej, za humanitarno solidarnost, za izobra脜戮evanje ter podporo mladim raziskovalcem. Verjamem, da ti ukrepi odra脜戮ajo strate脜隆ko usmerjenost Evropske unije k trajnostnemu razvoju, inovacijam, varnosti, solidarnosti tako znotraj kot zunaj njenih meja.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Beata Szyd脜聜o (ECR). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Panie Komisarzu! Szanowni Pa脜聞stwo! To nie jest dobry bud脜录et, poniewa脜录 on nie rozwi脛聟zuje tych podstawowych problem脙鲁w, kt脙鲁re w tej chwili niszcz脛聟 gospodark脛聶 europejsk脛聟, sprawiaj脛聟, 脜录e gospodarka europejska jest coraz mniej konkurencyjna. To nie jest dobry bud脜录et, poniewa脜录 on nie rozwi脛聟zuje problem脙鲁w Europejczyk脙鲁w, kt脙鲁rzy ubo脜录ej脛聟 i maj脛聟 coraz trudniejsze warunki 脜录ycia, trac脛聟 prac脛聶. Firmy upadaj脛聟. Trzeba si脛聶 nad tym zastanowi脛聡, jak wyj脜聸脛聡 z tej trudnej sytuacji i wyci脛聟gn脛聟脛聡 wnioski z b脜聜脛聶d脙鲁w, kt脙鲁re zosta脜聜y pope脜聜nione w poprzednich kadencjach. To si脛聶 ci脛聟gnie od wielu lat.

Rozpoczyna si脛聶 nowa kadencja Komisji Europejskiej. My r脙鲁wnie脜录 tutaj w Parlamencie rozpoczynamy tak naprawd脛聶 nasz脛聟 kolejn脛聟 kadencj脛聶 i czas zmieni脛聡 filozofi脛聶 my脜聸lenia, postawi脛聡 na rozw脙鲁j i konkurencyjno脜聸脛聡 gospodarki europejskiej, rozwi脛聟za脛聡 te problemy, kt脙鲁re niszcz脛聟 dzisiaj nasz脛聟 konkurencyjno脜聸脛聡, nasz脛聟 gospodark脛聶, i stworzy脛聡 takie warunki, 脜录eby Europejczycy mogli czu脛聡 si脛聶 bezpiecznie.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Joachim Streit (Renew). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, sehr geehrte Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Die Verhandlungen zum Haushalt 2025 zeigen einmal mehr: Die europ脙陇ische Politik arbeitet zu oft nach der Pr脙陇misse 芒聙聻mehr Geld hilft mehr芒聙聹. Doch das ist der falsche Ansatz. Wir brauchen keine endlose Aufstockung von Mitteln, sondern eine strikte Haushaltsdisziplin, die sicherstellt, dass wir das vorhandene Geld effizient verwenden.

Die Freien W脙陇hler sind gegen die Erh脙露hung der Budgets. Es ist unsere Pflicht gegen脙录ber den Steuerzahlern, jeden Euro verantwortungsvoll einzusetzen. Dabei setze ich mich f脙录r einen schlanken, aber wirksamen Staat ein. Wir m脙录ssen klare Priorit脙陇ten f脙录r die Zukunft setzen. Investitionen in Verteidigung und Forschung sind wichtig, um Europas Sicherheit und Wettbewerbsf脙陇higkeit zu sichern. Aber ebenso wichtig ist es, das Prinzip der Eigenverantwortung zu wahren. Die EU darf nicht alles regeln wollen. Sie muss sich auf die Bereiche konzentrieren, in denen sie tats脙陇chlich einen Mehrwert schaffen kann.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Janusz Lewandowski (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, annual budget procedure is seemingly routine, but in fact, this is never business as usual. What is unusual about 2025 is that this is the first budget based on revision of the MFF, implementing the so-called 'cascade mechanism', and the last budget of Johannes Hahn, drafted by Johannes Hahn 芒聙聯 for me, an incarnation of the honest broker.

With one minute, one issue only, that is your line, that is financing of interest costs of NextGenerationEU, bigger by EUR脗聽2.3脗聽billion than assumed. This is really annoying, this is really frustrating to dispute overnight whether this is 55/45 or 50/50. This is not decisive for the future of Europe. We need thematic special instrument, euro line, above ceilings and this is the hope of ending conciliation before midnight.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Adnan Dibrani (S&D). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! Alla budgetar har sina f脙露r- och nackdelar. Hemma i Sverige pratar vi om sunda statsfinanser. Med sunda statsfinanser kan man hantera kriser 芒聙聯 det kan vara en vanlig l脙楼gkonjunktur och det kan vara covid. Men man kan ocks脙楼 med sunda statsfinanser hantera de investeringar som beh脙露vs.

Vi m脙楼ste i det h脙陇r huset, precis som d脙陇r hemma, ha respekt f脙露r dem som arbetar p脙楼 hemmaplan och betalar in de skattemedel som vi sedan anv脙陇nder f脙露r att g脙露ra olika former av prioriteringar.

En del som vi har gjort 脙陇r att vi har byggt upp buffertar, men det 脙陇r beklagligt att vi脗聽inte anv脙陇nder buffertarna f脙露r kriser utan f脙露r att, i det h脙陇r l脙陇get, anv脙陇nda pengar f脙露r att betala uppkomna r脙陇ntekostnader. R脙陇ntekostnaderna var ingen 脙露verraskning, s脙楼 jag hade helst velat hitta en annan l脙露sning till detta.

Vi m脙楼ste kunna prioritera. Precis som man prioriterar p脙楼 hemmaplan m脙楼ste det h脙陇r huset l脙陇ra sig att hantera och prioritera viktiga fr脙楼gor. Det handlar i grund och botten om respekt f脙露r v脙楼ra medborgare.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bert-Jan Ruissen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, vier jaar geleden besloten de regeringsleiders, in antwoord op de coronacrisis, van de Europese Unie een schuldenunie te maken. Daar plukken we nu de wrange vruchten van. Kijk naar de verwachte rentelasten voor volgend jaar: meer dan 7脗聽miljard. Deze zullen de komende jaren alleen nog maar verder stijgen. Daarom zeg ik vandaag: alstublieft, geen nieuwe leningen meer! Ze kosten de belastingbetaler veel te veel geld.

Een andere belangrijke post op de begroting is die voor de cohesiefondsen:脗聽78脗聽miljard EUR. Ook hier is, wat mij betreft, een grondige bezinning nodig. Deze fondsen zijn immers bedoeld als een tijdelijke maatregel om achtergebleven gebieden een welvaartsimpuls te geven. Nu meerdere van die regio芒聙聶s zich richting het Europese gemiddelde begeven, moeten we het dus ook gaan hebben over verlaging van deze fondsen. Dat lijkt mij niet meer dan logisch!

Ziedaar, twee belangrijke aandachtspunten voor de komende jaren.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Katri Kulmuni (Renew). 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, tasa-arvo ei voi toteutua ilman alueellista tasa-arvoa. Alueiden v脙陇listen kehityserojen tasaaminen onkin yksi EU:n perustavoitteista ja siihen menee suuri osa EU:n budjetista.

Alueellinen tasa-arvo on yksi keskeinen vakaan yhteiskunnan perusta. Jos jossain maassa, jossain p脙陇in maata kansalaisille tulee k脙陇sitys, ettei heit脙陇 arvosteta, heid脙陇n kotiseutuunsa ei panosteta, eiv脙陇tk脙陇 he koe positiivisia tulevaisuusn脙陇kymi脙陇, on suuri vaara, ett脙陇 ihmiset katkeroituvat ja se on aina vaarallista. Siksi pid脙陇n tavattoman t脙陇rke脙陇n脙陇, ett脙陇 EU keskittyy perusteht脙陇v脙陇脙陇ns脙陇 eli alueellisten erojen v脙陇hent脙陇miseen ja pit脙陇脙陇 kiinni p脙陇脙陇t脙露ksenteon l脙陇heisyysperiaatteista. Asioista pit脙陇脙陇 p脙陇脙陇tt脙陇脙陇 mahdollisimman l脙陇hell脙陇 niit脙陇 ihmisi脙陇, joita asia koskee.

Usein ratkaisuksi tarjotaan aina suurempaa budjettia, mutta pid脙陇n eritt脙陇in t脙陇rke脙陇n脙陇, ett脙陇 sit脙陇 ei kasvateta kohtuuttomasti, vaan keskityt脙陇脙陇n kaikista keskeisimpiin asioihin, kuten alueelliseen tasa- arvoon.

脗聽脗聽Giuseppe Lupo (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, esprimo apprezzamento per l'accordo raggiunto, in fase di conciliazione, sul bilancio 2025 e ringrazio tutti coloro che hanno contribuito a questo importante risultato.

Ritengo importanti i risultati ottenuti per gli aiuti umanitari, la ricerca, Erasmus, l'ambiente, la salute. Maggiore attenzione meritano la Procura europea ed Europol per l'importante lavoro che stanno svolgendo.

La politica di bilancio dell'Unione europea 脙篓 fondamentale per sostenere la crescita e la competitivit脙聽, per ridurre le diseguaglianze, per rispondere alle aspettative dei cittadini europei, soprattutto delle aree pi脙鹿 svantaggiate. Per questo sottolineo il valore politico dell'intesa raggiunta tra le istituzioni europee, che ci consente di guardare oggi al futuro con maggiore fiducia per l'Europa che vogliamo.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Fernand Kartheiser (ECR). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, le budget de l'Union comprend une allocation de logement pour certaines cat脙漏gories de notre personnel vivant et travaillant au Luxembourg. Je d脙漏sire remercier tous ceux qui ont contribu脙漏 脙聽 cette r脙漏ussite. Mais au-del脙聽 de cette allocation, il faut enfin introduire un coefficient correcteur pour les agents des institutions europ脙漏ennes au Grand-Duch脙漏. 脙聙 cette fin, il faut modifier le statut.

Selon les donn脙漏es d'Eurostat, les agents, au Luxembourg, ont un pouvoir d'achat inf脙漏rieur de pr脙篓s de 20脗聽% 脙聽 celui de leurs coll脙篓gues 脙聽 Bruxelles; c'est inadmissible. L'inaction de la Commission europ脙漏enne dans ce domaine, comme ses violations continues des accords sur les services et le personnel pr脙漏sents 脙聽 Luxembourg, montrent que la Commission viole sciemment et d脙漏lib脙漏r脙漏ment ses obligations afin d'affaiblir les accords de si脙篓ge.

Nous attendons de la nouvelle Commission qu'elle change sa politique dans ce domaine.

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, mink脙陇laista on EU:n rahank脙陇ytt脙露? Euroopan tilintarkastustuomioistuin selvitti ja t脙陇ss脙陇 on tuloksia.

Tuomioistuin havaitsi, ett脙陇 EU:n kaikesta rahank脙陇yt脙露st脙陇 noin 10,7 miljardia euroa oli selke脙陇sti sen omien s脙陇脙陇nt脙露jen vastaista. Samalla tuomioistuimen sis脙陇lt脙陇 todettiin, ett脙陇 EU:n elpymispaketin rahojen valvonta on pett脙陇nyt ja oikeastaan niit脙陇 ei pystyt脙陇 edes valvomaan.

Tuomioistuin totesi my脙露s EU:n olevan elpymispaketin takia altis sille, ett脙陇 EU-rahaa my脙露nnet脙陇脙陇n tuplana, mutta t脙陇t脙陇kin on vaikea ja hankala j脙陇ljitt脙陇脙陇. Lis脙陇ksi tuomioistuin havaitsi, ett脙陇 joillakin EU:n rahoittamilla ilmastosopeutustoimilla oli vain v脙陇h脙陇n, ellei ollenkaan tai miettik脙陇脙陇, jopa heikent脙陇v脙陇 vaikutus sopeutumiskyvyn lis脙陇脙陇ntymiseen. Joten mist脙陇 siis tiet脙陇脙陇, ett脙陇 rahaa menee hukkaan? Siit脙陇, ett脙陇 k脙陇ytt脙陇j脙陇n脙陇 on EU.

脗聽脗聽Kinga Koll脙隆r (PPE). 芒聙聯 Tisztelt Eln脙露k Asszony! A 2025-脙露s k脙露lts脙漏gvet脙漏s kapcs脙隆n az egyik szemem s脙颅r, a m脙隆sik nevet. Nevet, mert a k脙露lts脙漏gvet脙漏sb脜聭l t脙露bb p脙漏nz fog jutni a di脙隆kok k脙录lf脙露ldi tanul脙隆s脙隆t lehet脜聭v脙漏 tev脜聭 Erasmus+-ra 脙漏s a kutat脙鲁k mobilit脙隆s脙隆t t脙隆mogat脙鲁 Horizont programra. Ez egy脙漏rtelm脜卤 siker. Ugyanakkor a m脙隆sik szemem s脙颅r, mert 脙潞gy t脜卤nik, Magyarorsz脙隆g ezekb脜聭l a pluszforr脙隆sokb脙鲁l nem fog r脙漏szes脙录lni.

A korm脙隆ny ugyanis nem teremtette meg azokat a szab脙隆lyokat, melyek alapj脙隆n az egyetemek a nekik j脙隆r脙鲁 forr脙隆sokat 脙隆tl脙隆that脙鲁 脙漏s elsz脙隆moltathat脙鲁 m脙鲁don tudj脙隆k elk脙露lteni. 脙聧gy egyetemeink nem f脙漏rnek hozz脙隆 a gazdas脙隆gi n脙露veked脙漏s szempontj脙隆b脙鲁l is fontos di脙隆kcsere- 脙漏s kutat脙鲁i programokhoz.

脗聽脗聽Tom脙隆脜隆 Zdechovsk脙陆 (PPE). 芒聙聯 Pan脙颅 p脜聶edsedaj脙颅c脙颅, v脙隆脜戮en脙陆 pane komisa脜聶i, d脙隆my a p脙隆nov脙漏, dovolte mi na za脛聧脙隆tku 脜聶脙颅ct, 脜戮e z脙颅tra schvalujeme rozpo脛聧et na rok 2025. A pro脛聧 je tento rozpo脛聧et d脜炉le脜戮it脙陆? Proto脜戮e si mus脙颅me uv脛聸domit, 脜戮e mus脙颅me p脜聶estat nal脙漏vat pen脙颅ze do projekt脜炉, kter脙漏 nic nep脜聶in脙隆脜隆脙颅, kter脙漏 nemaj脙颅 脜戮脙隆dnou hodnotu. Nesm脙颅me z t脛聸chto pen脛聸z pos脙颅lat ani jedno euro 脜戮脙隆dn脙陆m terorist脜炉m a organizac脙颅m, kter脙漏 financuj脙颅 teroristy. Nesm脙颅me z t脛聸chto pen脛聸z d脙隆vat pen脙颅ze do ni脛聧eho jin脙漏ho ne脜戮 do obrany, energetick脙漏 bezpe脛聧nosti a podpory inovac脙颅. N脙隆脜隆 rozpo脛聧et mus脙颅 b脙陆t p脜聶ehl脙颅dkou vize a ne p脜聶ehl脙颅dkou byrokratick脙陆ch kompromis脜炉. Ka脜戮d脙漏 euro mus脙颅 m脙颅t hmatateln脙漏 v脙陆sledky. To si mus脙颅me uv脛聸domit. A to jsme my jako Evropsk脙隆 lidov脙隆 strana v tomto rozpo脛聧tu prosazovali.

Catch-the-eye procedure

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, liebe Mitmenschen! Ich freue mich, dass wir hier eine konstruktive Debatte 脙录ber den Haushalt 2025 f脙录hren k脙露nnen. Schlie脙聼lich hat es genau wegen solch einem Thema vor kurzem erst die deutsche Bundesregierung zerlegt. Das Budget der Europ脙陇ischen Union ist riesig. Es umfasst f脙录r das Jahr 2025 eine Summe von 199脗聽438,4 Millionen Euro. Eine unendlich gro脙聼e Zahl, die nicht nur meine Vorstellungen sprengt, sondern ich glaube, die Vorstellung von jedem, der nicht eine Eins plus in Mathe in der Schule hatte.

Dieses Ausma脙聼 zeigt die gro脙聼e Verantwortung, die wir haben mit unserem Budget, denn es handelt sich am Ende des Tages um Steuergeld. Eine Menge Steuergeld, aber Steuergeld. W脙陇hrend ich im Namen meiner Partei, der Partei des Fortschritts, den Haushaltsplan also begr脙录脙聼en darf, bin ich sehr betroffen 脙录ber einen Punkt, und das ist der mangelnde Ausbau der Finanzierung des Erasmus+芒聙聭Programms. Das Erasmus+芒聙聭Programm ist ein Leuchtturmprojekt. Es bringt die k脙录nftigen Generationen Europas zusammen und sollte immer eine Priorit脙陇t im Budget der Europ脙陇ischen Union haben.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(End of catch-the-eye procedure)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Johannes Hahn, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, indeed, it's my last speech, and I would like to thank you all for this debate about the next annual budget. If I may continue where the last speaker has just finished: Erasmus constantly gets an improvement, but we try to have linear improvement in order to avoid a peak only in 2027. So it is important to have a constant increase and this is guaranteed.

Second, if I may say also for future debates on the next annual budget: I think it is always important to keep as much as possible flexibility. We have seen only in very recent months unforeseen events to which we have to react. And today, it is always necessary to change regulations to modify programmes. All this is a lengthy process and it would be more efficient and more, let's say, in the interest of our citizens, if we would have more flexibility, allowing us to react faster and quicker.

Third, I wanted to highlight and to stress what was raised by some Members about Luxembourg and the Luxembourg allowance. I think it is a big achievement and I am grateful for the agreement we could find, at least to have this Luxembourg allowance of around EUR脗聽10脗聽million per year, and it will exactly address the needs of the lower income grades being in Luxembourg or who should be attracted to Luxembourg. To be honest, if we would apply a possible correction coefficient, it would cost us EUR脗聽270脗聽million per year, which is not affordable and which would immediately lead to the opening of staff regulations 芒聙聯 this is not something we want to see, number one. Number two, to be fair, many people working for European institutions in Luxembourg are not living in Luxembourg. They are commuting on a daily basis from France, Belgium or Germany. So it's not easy but I think the solution we have found is a good one.

As it indeed is my last speech, I would like to thank all of you, those who are present, those who are not present, and also many previous Members who have not been any more in the European Parliament for the excellent cooperation in the past five years, also the Council. I think in these five years we have not only negotiated an unprecedented Multiannual Financial Framework, we have NextGenerationEU with all the effects we are discussing and which you will discuss in the future.

All this happened, remember, in very difficult circumstances, and I still believe it is a miracle that nobody in all the negotiating teams were infected during these negotiations because you cannot negotiate the MFF in video conferences. So we had to come together, it was an exemption, and it was during lockdowns in order to enable indeed to get the budget done. We had this year a mid-term revision, which was for the first time a real mid-term revision, a substantial one. Finally, we negotiated altogether five reasonable budgets.

So, ladies and gentlemen, until Saturday, I will be Commissioner Hahn; as of Sunday, I will be Master Hahn, master of my own time. But be reassured, I will spend at least part of this time engaging in future debates about the next MFF, because I think it is important to engage also with some experience in a different position on the next Multiannual Financial Framework, which is future-oriented, future-proof and enables us to serve our citizens and their interests. Thank you very much.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

PRESIDENZA: ANTONELLA SBERNA
Vicepresidente

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽J脙隆nos B脙鲁ka, President-in-Office of the Council. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, honourable Members, Commissioner, ladies and gentlemen, I thank you very much for this exchange of views. It has shown that, notwithstanding the differences in perspectives and priorities, we are united in our commitment to build a better Europe to the benefit of all of our citizens.

The agreement reached on the budget for 2025 is a sensible compromise, allowing the Union to adequately finance its different priorities and fulfil its legal obligations. I reiterate that the Council expects the European Parliament to approve the joint text to allow for an orderly start of 2025.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Johan Van Overtveldt, voorzitter van de commissie BUDG. 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, commissaris, collega芒聙聶s, de begroting voor 2025 is de best mogelijke budgettaire oefening onder de huidige omstandigheden. Omstandigheden die ons dwingen tot aanpassingen, zoals ook de lidstaten dat moeten doen. In ieder geval kan het niet verder zoals we nu bezig zijn.

De EU-begroting is te veel gebonden aan langetermijnplanning, waar nieuwe initiatieven niet op kunnen aansluiten en waarbij we onvoldoende kunnen beantwoorden aan de verwachtingen van onze burgers en bedrijven. De oefening voor de komende meerjarenbegroting, die vanaf 2028 zal starten, moet daarom meteen op gang getrokken worden. Wanneer de Commissie straks groen licht krijgt, moet dit bovenaan de prioriteiten staan. Geld uitgeven kan helpen, op voorwaarde dat er geld is en wanneer de kwaliteit van de investeringen kan worden gewaarborgd en er dus echte toegevoegde waarde is.

Naast het bestemmen van uitgaven moeten we echter veel meer doen en dat hoeft niet eens altijd geld te kosten. Het grote pijnpunt blijft de immense regeldruk als gevolg van Europese wetgeving. Onze burgers en ondernemingen roepen om vereenvoudiging, vragen om meer flexibiliteit en om drastische vermindering van de regeldruk en bovenal van de rapporteringsverplichtingen. Als we onze burgers en bedrijven willen steunen in hun groei, om hen te laten innoveren en onze welvaart veilig te stellen, is dit de weg voorwaarts.

Het gaat hier bij dit alles niet eens om een links/rechts-tegenstelling. Het gaat hier om vertrouwen geven aan onze burgers en ondernemingen en om hen te steunen in wat ze graag willen doen en om het hoofd te bieden aan deze onzekere tijden. In de Raad wordt gesproken over een revolutie om alles te vereenvoudigen, en mevrouw Von der Leyen heeft ook de consolidatie aangekondigd van de groene rapporteringsverplichtingen. Ik zou zeggen: doe het gewoon en doe het meteen.

Tot slot rest mij verder nog de rapporteurs en alle medewerkers te bedanken voor het geleverde werk.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Johan Van Overtveldt, Chair of the BUDG Committee. 芒聙聯 And most finally, in English I address myself to the Commissioner with whom we were able to work for five years. We often had discussions, heated discussions, but always in good spirit, and therefore I thank him a lot.

And I would like to end with some of the words of my late father, who used to say, trees don't run into each other, but people do. So I hope I have the chance to run into you more frequently also in the past.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Presidente. 芒聙聯 La discussione 脙篓 chiusa.

La votazione si svolger脙聽 domani.

A Patri脙鲁t脙隆k Eur脙鲁p脙隆脙漏rt frakci脙鲁ja ugyanakkor nem tudja elfogadni ezt a k脙露lts脙漏gvet脙漏st. Ez egy h脙隆bor脙潞s k脙露lts脙漏gvet脙漏s, amelynek az a c脙漏lja, hogy minden uni脙鲁s k脙露lts脙漏gvet脙漏si eszk脙露zzel biztos脙颅tsa az Ukrajn脙隆ban zajl脙鲁 h脙隆bor脙潞 folytat脙隆s脙隆t. Az eur脙鲁pai parlamenti v脙隆laszt脙隆sokon veres脙漏get szenvedett baloldal k脙露lts脙漏gvet脙漏si eszk脙露z脙露kkel folytatja az int脙漏zm脙漏nyes politikai zsarol脙隆st, a jog脙隆llamis脙隆gi dzsih脙隆dot is. Mi Patri脙鲁t脙隆k ezzel szemben ki脙隆llunk az uni脙鲁s tag脙隆llamok nemzeti szuverenit脙隆sa mellett!

Magyarorsz脙隆gnak, a magyar embereknek jog szerint j脙隆rnak az uni脙鲁s fejleszt脙漏si forr脙隆sok. A magyar di脙隆koknak, a magyar egyetemeknek jog szerint j脙隆rnak az Erasmus 脙漏s kutat脙隆s-fejleszt脙漏si uni脙鲁s forr脙隆sok. M脙漏gis, a magyar ellenz脙漏k k脙漏pvisel脜聭i krokodilk脙露nnyeket hullatnak, hogy a magyar di脙隆kokat 脙漏s egyetemeket az Eur脙鲁pai Bizotts脙隆g jogellenes d脙露nt脙漏s脙漏vel 2025-ben is ki akarj脙隆k z脙隆rni az eur脙鲁pai programokb脙鲁l.

A Fidesz-KDNP eur脙鲁pai parlamenti k脙漏pvisel脜聭csoportja nemet mond a h脙隆bor脙潞ra, nemet mond az illeg脙隆lis migr脙隆ci脙鲁ra 脙漏s nemet mond a woke 脜聭r脙录let terjed脙漏s脙漏re. K脙露vetelj脙录k, hogy a Magyarorsz脙隆gnak j脙隆r脙鲁 fejleszt脙漏si forr脙隆sokat fizess脙漏k ki, a tag脙隆llamok politikai zsarol脙隆s脙隆t fejezz脙漏k be, az Eur脙鲁pai Bizotts脙隆g magyar di脙隆koknak 脙漏s egyetemeknek h脙隆tr脙隆nyt okoz脙鲁 diszkriminat脙颅v d脙露nt脙漏s脙漏t pedig vonj脙隆k vissza!

脗聽脗聽Maria Grapini (S&D), 脙庐n scris. 芒聙聯 Condi脠聸iile actuale 脠聶i contextul general impun nu doar existen脠聸a unui buget pentru anul 2025, ci 脠聶i proceduri mult mai exigente privind eficien脠聸a cheltuirii banilor. Bugetul pentru 2025 a crescut cu 6脗聽% fa脠聸脛聝 de 2024, 脙庐ns脛聝 nu toate aloc脛聝rile sunt cele mai inspirate, 脙庐n special pentru c脛聝 nu au fost condi脠聸ionate de cre脠聶terea eficien脠聸ei cheltuirii resurselor financiare.

De exemplu, alocarea unui buget suplimentar pentru Parchetul European ar fi trebuit s脛聝 fie condi脠聸ionat脛聝 de sumele recuperate din fraude 脠聶i evaziune fiscal脛聝. Mi-a脠聶 fi dorit, de asemenea, mai multe fonduri pentru agricultori, dar 脠聶i pentru politicile de eradicare a s脛聝r脛聝ciei, care nu se pot realiza doar prin ajutoare sociale. Este nevoie de investi脠聸ii 脙庐n reindustrializare 脠聶i de sprijin pentru IMM-uri pentru a crea locuri de munc脛聝 bine pl脛聝tite.

Sper ca noua Comisie European脛聝 s脛聝 adopte o viziune mai puternic脛聝 脙庐n privin脠聸a cheltuirii banilor publici.

脗聽

8. The Autumn 2024 Economic Forecast: a gradual rebound in an adverse environment (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Presidente. 芒聙聯 L'ordine del giorno reca la discussione sulla dichiarazione della Commissione inerente alle previsioni economiche dell'autunno 2024: graduale ripresa in un contesto sfavorevole (2024/2941(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Johannes Hahn, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, you see, it was not my real last speech, but I'm doing this on behalf of my colleague Paolo Gentiloni, who might come very soon. As you know, we have at the same time our College meetings, and it's the last one, and he is there to discuss as he is chef de fil for the脗聽European Semester recommendations.

Coming back to the agenda of today, this is related to what he is just presenting to the colleagues: a few days ago, on 15脗聽November, the Commission released its latest autumn forecast. Let me recall the five key messages emerging from this forecast.

First, the EU economy is resuming moderate growth in an increasingly challenging context. After returning to growth in the first quarter of 2024, the EU economy continued to expand in the second and third quarters at a steady but still subdued pace. In the first half of 2024, household consumption grew only modestly, despite continued improvement in disposable income; a still high cost of living and rising economic uncertainty pushed households to save. At the same time, investment clearly disappointed with deep and broad based contraction across most Member States and asset categories. Making up for the weakness in domestic demand, net external demand contributed positively to growth thanks to a rebounding global goods trade and continued expansion of trade in services.

Consumption is estimated to have gained strength in the third quarter, but investment to have contracted further. Over the next two years, the restraint to consumption is set to loosen further as the purchasing power of wages strengthens further. Employment continues to expand and the saving rate decreases. Investment is expected to rebound on the back of strong corporate balance sheets, recovering profits, improving credit conditions and the impulse of the Recovery and Resilience Facility.

Real GDP growth is expected to reach 0.9脗聽% this year, before picking up to 1.5脗聽% next year and 1.8脗聽% in 2026. In the euro area, the real GDP growth is expected at 0.8脗聽% this year, 1.3脗聽% in 2025 and 1.6脗聽% in 2026. Most Member States are posting an economic expansion this year, with Germany representing a notable exception, with a marginal contraction in economic activity. In 2025 and 2026, we see growth resuming or picking up in all Member States.

As a second key message from the autumn forecast, service inflation is set to drive the disinflation process ahead. Headline inflation in the euro area is expected to stabilise at 2.2脗聽% until mid-2025, and to gradually fall to 1.8脗聽% by the end of 2026 in the EU. Inflation would fall towards the target, you know the target is 2脗聽%, a bit later, but also within the forecast horizon. This moderation hinges upon the gradual easing of price pressures in services.

The assumptions for energy commodity prices are based on futures market prices. All prices are on a downward path over the forecast horizon, whereas TTF gas prices are expected to remain broadly stable until the end of next year before declining in 2026. Electricity market prices, after their broad stabilisation this year, are expected to increase in 2025 and to fall again in 2026. While the disinflationary process is projected to continue in all Member States, its pace differs widely. Inflation is set to remain higher in central and eastern European countries, a pattern that has characterised our inflation maps since the beginning of the energy crisis.

The third key message is that the EU labour market held up well in the first half of 2024, and is expected to remain strong despite some cooling as the pace of employment growth decelerates. The EU economy generated jobs for a further 750脗聽000 workers in the first half of the year. This brings the number of new employed persons since the start of the pandemic, we are talking about the fourth quarter of 2019, to 8脗聽million, which is remarkable. However, the pace of employment growth is set to slow. Following a contraction in 2023, productivity is set to stagnate in 2024. It is then expected to post a cyclical rebound in 2025 and gain strength in 2026. We know how important it is to lift our productivity, to improve our competitiveness. The unemployment rate is projected to remain at a record low.

Fourth, government deficits are set to narrow, but debt ratios edge up again. The budget deficit of the EU as a whole is back on a declining path, if only a mild one. The deficit is forecast to increase from 3.1脗聽% in 2024 to 2.9脗聽% in 2026, both in the EU and in the euro area. In 2024 and 2025, deficit reduction is supported by budgetary restraint, partly due to the phasing out of energy support measures.

The contractionary impact of national fiscal policies on economic activity is expected to be offset in 2025 by the accelerating rollout of EU funds, including the RRF. Public debt is projected to increase slightly in the European Union from 82.1脗聽% of GDP in 2023 to 83.4脗聽% in 2026. In the euro area, it is set to rise from 88.9脗聽% last year to 90脗聽% in 2026. This rise reflects the effect of still elevated primary deficits and increasing interest expenditure, which are no longer offset by high nominal GDP growth. Nevertheless, this follows the sizeable decrease of the debt ratio between 2020 and 2023 by almost ten percentage points.

The fifth and last key message concerns the risks surrounding the forecast, which are tilted to the downside and uncertainty has increased. Russia's protracted war of aggression against Ukraine and the intensified conflict in the Middle East remain key geopolitical risks. Moreover, a sharp turn in protectionist trade policies could upend global trade with negative impacts on the Union's highly open economy. Also, delays in the implementation of the RRF or a more restrictive fiscal stance, especially in 2026, could further dampen the resumption of growth. The recent extreme weather events in Spain are a tragic reminder that environmental risks are mounting. Thank you very much.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Markus Ferber, im Namen der PPE-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Herr Kommissar, liebe Kolleginnen, liebe Kollegen! Die Herbstprognose der Europ脙陇ischen Kommission stellt abermals keine angenehme Lekt脙录re zur Verf脙录gung. Das Wachstum in der Europ脙陇ischen Union, in der Eurozone bleibt in diesem Jahr unter einem Prozent. Defizite und Staatsverschuldung bleiben hoch, die Produktivit脙陇t stagniert und auch in den kommenden Jahren ist nicht mit einem richtigen Schub f脙录r das Wachstum zu rechnen.

In Deutschland, das einst der Wachstumsmotor in der Europ脙陇ischen Union war, sieht die Lage besonders d脙录ster aus. Drei Jahre Ampelregierung haben immensen Schaden f脙录r unsere Volkswirtschaft angerichtet. Inzwischen ist Deutschland nicht mehr der Wachstumsmotor, es ist der Bremsklotz. Sie haben das ja sehr charmant beschrieben, dass Deutschland hier eine Ausnahme spiele. Insbesondere das geringe Produktivit脙陇tswachstum sollte uns zu denken geben, denn es ist kein Einmaleffekt, sondern ein strukturelles Problem, das offenkundig wird. In den vergangenen 20脗聽Jahren war das Produktivit脙陇tswachstum in den USA doppelt so hoch wie in der Europ脙陇ischen Union. Herr Draghi hat es gerade in seinem Bericht noch einmal aufgef脙录hrt.

Die schlechten Wirtschaftsdaten sind kein Zufall. Sie sind auch eine Folge einer falschen Politik und spiegeln eine dramatische Verschlechterung der Wettbewerbsf脙陇higkeit dar. Die Antwort darauf kann nicht 芒聙聻neue Konjunkturprogramme und Subventionspakete芒聙聹 lauten, denn strukturelle Schw脙陇chen werden damit nicht abgebaut, sondern nur prolongiert, nur verl脙陇ngert. Wir m脙录ssen hier eine grunds脙陇tzlich andere Politik machen, und zwar nicht morgen oder 脙录bermorgen, sondern sofort. Wir m脙录ssen jetzt die Wende einleiten hin zu einer neuen Angebotspolitik: B脙录rokratielasten senken, Berichtspflichten reduzieren, Planungsverfahren vereinfachen, Energiekosten runterbringen, Binnenmarkt vollenden, Kapitalmarktunion voranbringen. Das sind die Themen, die jetzt auch im 100芒聙聭Tage芒聙聭Programm der neuen Kommission vorkommen sollten.

脗聽脗聽Gabriele Bischoff, im Namen der S&D-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Herr Kommissar Hahn, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Und deshalb ist es f脙录r uns wichtig, auch in Europa hier die Weichen zu stellen. Wir wissen, im Moment d脙陇mpft die Aufbau- und Resilienzfazilit脙陇t alles noch ab. Die l脙陇uft aus. Europa muss hier umstellen und den Mut zu mehr Investitionen haben.

脗聽脗聽Enik脜聭 Gy脜聭ri, a PfE k脙漏pvisel脜聭csoport nev脙漏ben. 芒聙聯 Tisztelt Eln脙露k Asszony! A Letta- 脙漏s a Draghi-jelent脙漏sek ut脙隆n a Bizotts脙隆g saj脙隆t el脜聭rejelz脙漏se is ki脙隆br脙隆nd脙颅t脙鲁. Ujjal mutogat a nemzetk脙露zi partnerek protekcionista int脙漏zked脙漏seire, pedig az elm脙潞lt ciklusban z脙露ld k脙露nt脙露sbe 脙露lt脙露ztetve maga is alkalmazta azokat.

A versenyk脙漏pess脙漏ghez Eur脙鲁p脙隆nak min脙漏l t脙露bb 脙露sszek脙露ttet脙漏sre lenne e helyett sz脙录ks脙漏ge, a fellend脙录l脙漏shez pedig 脙隆lszents脙漏g 脙漏s politikai j脙隆tszm脙隆k helyett pragmatikus, a saj脙隆t 脙漏rdekeinket szolg脙隆l脙鲁 azonnali int脙漏zked脙漏sekre. Fordulatot akarnak a patri脙鲁t脙隆k a gazdas脙隆gpolitik脙隆ban.

脗聽脗聽Adrian-George Axinia, 脙庐n numele grupului ECR. 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, stima脠聸i colegi, s脛聝 nu v脛聝 sup脛聝ra脠聸i pe mine, dar am senza脠聸ia acut脛聝 c脛聝 ne afl脛聝m 脙庐ntr-o 脠聶edin脠聸脛聝 de terapie de grup. E ca 脙庐n filmul 脙庐n care meteoritul se pr脛聝bu脠聶e脠聶te pe P脛聝m脙垄nt 脠聶i unii beau 脠聶ampanie admir脙垄nd r脛聝s脛聝ritul. Realitatea obiectiv脛聝 este c脛聝 economia european脛聝 este 脙庐ntr-o zon脛聝 de colaps. A spus-o eufemistic Draghi. O sim脠聸im 脠聶i noi 脠聶i o 脠聶tim cu to脠聸ii. 脙聨n loc s脛聝 analiz脛聝m ca 脙庐n Evul Mediu c脙垄脠聸i 脙庐ngeri pot dansa pe v脙垄rful unui ac, ar trebui s脛聝 ac脠聸ion脛聝m concret 脠聶i decisiv.

S脛聝 renun脠聸脛聝m la Pactul verde 脠聶i s脛聝 脙庐ncerc脛聝m s脛聝 ajut脛聝m industria european脛聝, 脙庐ndeosebi industria auto 脠聶i cea a o脠聸elului. S脛聝 discut脛聝m cu Trump 脠聶i cu chinezii cum evit脛聝m un r脛聝zboi al tarifelor vamale. S脛聝 rezolv脛聝m problemele care au dus la coma profund脛聝 脙庐n care a intrat spa脠聸iul Schengen. S脛聝 aloc脛聝m bani pentru cercetare-dezvoltare, dar nu 脙庐n cheie ideologic脛聝, ci urm脛聝rind principiile pie脠聸ei libere. S脛聝 nu mai st脛聝m ca ni脠聶te solda脠聸i de lut, uit脙垄ndu-ne cum al脠聸ii pompeaz脛聝 sume colosale 脙庐n inteligen脠聸a artificial脛聝 脠聶i s脛聝 vedem ce putem face 脠聶i noi. 脙聨n aceast脛聝 cheie, poate ne g脙垄ndim 脠聶i cum va ar脛聝ta economia viitorului 脠聶i dac脛聝 nu cumva ne este necesar脛聝 reformarea pie脠聸ei muncii pentru 脙庐ncurajarea anumitor tipuri de meserii practice.

脗聽脗聽Billy Kelleher, on behalf of the Renew Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, the autumn economic forecast for the EU seems cautiously optimistic. After prolonged stagnation, we are returning to very modest growth. Inflation is still decreasing and should continue to ease. The labour market is holding up and employment is expected to continue to grow.

But the current geopolitical landscape means there are strong winds against us. We face a triple threat of trade wars, taxation and tensions. To withstand these challenges, we must ensure that our citizens can emerge from this cost-of-living crisis. We must redouble our efforts.

If President von der Leyen is able to garner the support of this House tomorrow for her College of Commissioners, in return we expect swift and assertive action in the area of competitiveness 芒聙聯 for it to become the cornerstone of our policies. We want to see the implementation of the Draghi report and the Letta report, and we cannot continue to sit on our hands and watch the EU economies slowly stagnate.

I would also issue a word of caution to political factions around Europe. This is not the time to play politics and political games. We need to stabilise the EU economy and brace ourselves, what may come, because there are headwinds that could be very, very severe if we do not handle them well.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie Toussaint, au nom du groupe Verts/ALE. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, nous sommes ici r脙漏unis pour parler de rebond 脙漏conomique dans un environnement d脙漏favorable. Mais de quel rebond 脙漏conomique parlons-nous, quand 118脗聽millions d'Europ脙漏ennes et d'Europ脙漏ens s'enfoncent chaque jour dans la pauvret脙漏? Quand sept des neuf limites plan脙漏taires 芒聙聯脗聽le climat, la biodiversit脙漏, la pollution脗聽芒聙聯 sont d脙漏pass脙漏es? Quand des fermes partout disparaissent ou que des entreprises ferment, par exemple en France, o脙鹿 150脗聽000脗聽emplois sont menac脙漏s, Vencorex, Michelin, Auchan et maintenant Arcelor?

Votre obsession pour la croissance vous fait perdre des yeux les vraies priorit脙漏s. L'脙漏conomie n'est pas une fin en soi, mais c'est un moyen au service de l'int脙漏r脙陋t g脙漏n脙漏ral. Alors je vous 脙漏coute, et vous n'avez qu'un mot 脙聽 la bouche, ce terme de comp脙漏titivit脙漏.

Pourtant, pour garantir la comp脙漏titivit脙漏 europ脙漏enne, Mario脗聽Draghi nous a dit que nous avions besoin de 800脗聽milliards脗聽d'euros par an. O脙鹿 est cet argent? Et je vais aller plus loin. Les estimations nous disent qu'au moins 10脗聽000脗聽milliards d'euros suppl脙漏mentaires sont n脙漏cessaires chaque ann脙漏e d'ici脗聽2050 pour garantir seulement la transition 脙漏nerg脙漏tique. Cela veut dire au moins 260脗聽milliards d'euros publics par an. L脙聽 encore, o脙鹿 est cet argent? Parce que je regarde partout, mais je ne vois rien sur la table.

Au contraire, les r脙篓gles aust脙漏ritaires que vous avez remises en place entravent toute capacit脙漏 d'investissement dans l'indispensable transition 脙漏cologique et, soyons clairs, condamnent 脙聽 terme l'脙漏conomie europ脙漏enne 脙聽 la d脙漏pendance vis-脙聽-vis d'脙聣tats tiers et tout simplement 脙聽 la disparition.

Alors il est encore temps de changer de cap. Sortons de cette 脙漏conomie de la pr脙漏dation pour aller vers une 脙漏conomie du脗聽care, une 脙漏conomie du soin, des services publics, de la sant脙漏, du travail d脙漏cent, des droits du vivant. En somme, une 脙漏conomie de la protection du climat et de l'脙漏radication de la pauvret脙漏.

脗聽脗聽Jussi Saramo, The Left-ryhm脙陇n puolesta. 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, koronapandemia ja Ven脙陇j脙陇n imperialismi osoittavat, kuinka riippuvaisia olemme 脙露ljyvaltioista ja Kiinasta.

Mutta pahinta mit脙陇 voisimme nyt tehd脙陇, on tuudittautua n脙陇ihin talousennusteisiin ja uskoa paluuseen vanhoihin hyviin aikoihin. Se, ett脙陇 energian hinnat nyt laskevat ja talous elpyy, antaa meille aikaa kiihdytt脙陇脙陇 siirtym脙陇脙陇 kohti itsen脙陇isemp脙陇脙陇 Eurooppaa.

Ennusteessakin mainitut uhat ja ilmastokriisi ovat jo k脙陇ynniss脙陇 ja kun USA:n presidentiksi noustaan drill baby drill -intoilulla, on irrottautuminen fossiilituhon akselista my脙露s taloudellisesti v脙陇ltt脙陇m脙陇t脙露nt脙陇. Kun Eurooppaa vaaditaan hylk脙陇脙陇m脙陇脙陇n vihre脙陇 siirtym脙陇 halvan polttoaineen vuoksi ja koska salin oikealta laidalta suorastaan ihaillaan Trumpin ja Putinin kaltaisia heid脙陇n vahvoina pit脙陇mi脙陇脙陇n johtajia, on meid脙陇n hyl脙陇tt脙陇v脙陇 itsekkyys ja katsottava pidemm脙陇lle.

脗聽脗聽Marcin Sypniewski, w imieniu grupy ESN. 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Bunkr脙鲁w nie ma, ale te脜录 jest fajnie 芒聙聯 tak wydaje si脛聶 m脙鲁wi脛聡 Komisja Europejska w jesiennej prognozie gospodarczej. Ale w rzeczywisto脜聸ci wygl脛聟da na to, 脜录e zrobili脜聸cie gospodarce jesie脜聞 脜聸redniowiecza. Zastanawiam si脛聶, w jakiej alternatywnej rzeczywisto脜聸ci 脜录yjemy i czemu niby jest dobrze, skoro jest tak 脜潞le.

Tylko w tym p脙鲁脜聜roczu rozmawiali脜聸my o kryzysie w bran脜录y motoryzacyjnej, w bran脜录y hutniczej czy o wysokich cenach energii. Dopiero co zagl脛聟dali脜聸my kryzysowi w oczy, a teraz nagle jeste脜聸my karmieni umiarkowanym optymizmem. Coraz wi脛聶cej ludzi 脜录yje w ub脙鲁stwie. S脛聟 masowe zwolnienia ze wzgl脛聶du na wysokie koszty pracy i wysokie koszty energii. A tutaj czytamy sobie laurk脛聶 i prognoz脛聶, 脜录e w sumie to teraz b脛聶dzie troch脛聶 lepiej.

Na pewno nie dla milion脙鲁w Polek i Polak脙鲁w, kt脙鲁rzy w pocie czo脜聜a pracuj脛聟, nierzadko na dw脙鲁ch etatach, 脜录eby zwi脛聟za脛聡 koniec z ko脜聞cem. Ten raport to obraza dla moich rodak脙鲁w, kt脙鲁rzy cz脛聶sto staj脛聟 teraz przed dylematem: kupujemy leki, p脜聜acimy czynsz czy wysoki rachunek za pr脛聟d? Mimo 脜录e te wiadomo脜聸ci o spodziewanym odbiciu gospodarczym s脛聟 optymistyczne, to odpowiedzi脛聟 na te wie脜聸ci jest niestety zwi脛聶kszony udzia脜聜 Unii i rz脛聟d脙鲁w w gospodarce. Na marginesie, gdyby prognozy by脜聜y bardzo z脜聜e albo bardzo dobre, to odpowied脜潞 zapewne by by脜聜a ta sama, bo po prostu tutaj nie umiecie inaczej my脜聸le脛聡, a to po prostu nie dzia脜聜a. Nie dzia脜聜a脜聜o w Zwi脛聟zku Sowieckim, nie dzia脜聜a脜聜o w Niemczech Wschodnich, nie dzia脜聜a脜聜o na Kubie, w Wenezueli nie dzia脜聜a i nie dzia脜聜a te脜录 w Unii Europejskiej. To nie politycy tworz脛聟 dobrobyt, a przedsi脛聶biorcy, kt脙鲁rzy konkuruj脛聟 mi脛聶dzy sob脛聟 o spe脜聜nienie potrzeb konsument脙鲁w. Politycy nie pomagaj脛聟 w budowaniu bogactwa, a wr脛聶cz przeciwnie 芒聙聯 notorycznie przeszkadzaj脛聟 jednostkom, kt脙鲁re dzia脜聜aj脛聟 na wolnym rynku. Pozw脙鲁lcie ludziom dzia脜聜a脛聡, a nie tylko prognozy b脛聶d脛聟 dobre, ale dobre b脛聶d脛聟 te脜录 wyniki. Sko脜聞czcie z Zielonym 脜聛adem, z tymi szale脜聞stwami klimatycznymi, kt脙鲁re niszcz脛聟 nasz脛聟 gospodark脛聶. Zamiast uchwala脛聡 nowe programy, nowe raporty, zlikwidujmy tysi脛聟ce przepis脙鲁w, kt脙鲁re utrudniaj脛聟 wszystkim 脜录ycie. Niech Unia stanie si脛聶 wielkim, wolnym rynkiem, a nie zielonym skansenem.

脗聽脗聽Fernando Navarrete Rojas (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, las recientes previsiones econ脙鲁micas de la Comisi脙鲁n contemplan una d脙漏bil recuperaci脙鲁n sometida a grandes riesgos a la baja. Este contexto exige actuar con determinaci脙鲁n para fortalecer nuestra capacidad de respuesta a corto y a largo plazo.

Para ello, en primer lugar, debemos impulsar una ambiciosa agenda de reformas estructurales que aumenten la competitividad y profundicen en el mercado 脙潞nico.

En segundo lugar, debemos mejorar la eficiencia del gasto p脙潞blico. Esto exige reorientar hacia el nivel comunitario el gasto y la gesti脙鲁n de aquellas pol脙颅ticas que proveen de bienes p脙潞blicos europeos 脙隆reas como la defensa, las interconexiones energ脙漏ticas o el desarrollo de capacidades tecnol脙鲁gicas.

Y, en tercer lugar, debemos garantizar la credibilidad de un nuevo marco de reglas fiscales con un cumplimiento estricto por parte de todos los Estados miembros.

Como bien se脙卤ala el Informe Draghi, sin unos m脙隆rgenes fiscales s脙鲁lidos cualquier respuesta ser脙隆 insuficiente y tard脙颅a. Adem脙隆s, resulta esencial dise脙卤ar un mecanismo fiscal europeo contrac脙颅clico que permita asegurar que la respuesta fiscal del conjunto de la Uni脙鲁n sea la m脙隆s adecuada al contexto macroecon脙鲁mico y que sea compatible con el esfuerzo de consolidaci脙鲁n fiscal en los Estados miembros.

En definitiva, es fundamental combinar la responsabilidad fiscal con la flexibilidad estrat脙漏gica, priorizando las inversiones en innovaci脙鲁n y adoptando una pol脙颅tica econ脙鲁mica que fomente la productividad y refuerce nuestro posicionamiento global.

Europa no puede permitirse otra d脙漏cada de retrasos en reformas estructurales y sostenibilidad fiscal. Es hora de liderar con visi脙鲁n y rigor.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jon脙隆s Fern脙隆ndez (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤or comisario, es un placer compartir con usted este 脙潞ltimo debate al que hac脙颅a referencia, en esta casa, y quer脙颅a felicitarle por el trabajo en estos a脙卤os.

Yo creo que hoy discutimos el marco en el que vamos a desarrollar la pr脙鲁xima legislatura y cu脙隆les son las perspectivas, las previsiones de actividad econ脙鲁mica, de empleo y de productividad. Realmente, el cuadro macro es 芒聙聰podr脙颅amos decir芒聙聰 mediocre. Es cierto que vamos recuper脙隆ndonos de los 脙潞ltimos golpes, pero a脙潞n estamos muy lejos de las tasas de crecimiento y de inversi脙鲁n que necesitamos.

En mi opini脙鲁n, la larga d脙漏cada que sigui脙鲁 a la crisis financiera dej脙鲁 a Europa con unos niveles de inversi脙鲁n absolutamente insostenibles, inversi脙鲁n que necesitamos recuperar desde el lado privado, sin duda, pero tambi脙漏n desde el lado p脙潞blico. Desde el lado p脙潞blico necesitamos inversi脙鲁n europea, necesitamos mantener el NextGenerationEU, y desde el lado privado necesitamos mejorar el funcionamiento de la uni脙鲁n bancaria y de la uni脙鲁n de los mercados de capitales. Esa es la misi脙鲁n que tenemos para esta legislatura y estamos todos destinados a trabajar en ella.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Paolo Borchia (PfE). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, l'allarme sull'economia credo che sia oltre i livelli di guardia. Io personalmente sono molto preoccupato e non posso accettare che l'orchestra continui a suonare mentre il Titanic sta affondando.

Abbiamo bassa crescita, elevati deficit pubblici, alti stock di debito pubblico. Poi non si capisce che il problema non sono le esportazioni: il problema 脙篓 un mercato interno asfittico, ai limiti del moribondo.

Troppi investimenti vengono fatti al di fuori dell'Unione europea, perch脙漏 qui le imprese faticano ad investire a causa della troppa burocrazia, a causa dei prezzi dell'energia troppo alti.

E queste problematiche sono legate ad errori che la maggioranza di questo Parlamento ha commesso. Quindi c'脙篓 una corresponsabilit脙聽 da parte di questo Parlamento. Il malato 脙篓 grave e la cura da troppi anni non sta funzionando.

脗聽脗聽Katri Kulmuni (Renew). 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, talouskasvu on pysynyt Euroopassa ja erityisesti euroalueella hitaana jo pidemm脙陇n aikaa. Silti j脙陇senmaiden v脙陇lill脙陇 on ollut suuriakin eroja. Toisissa j脙陇senmaissa talouskasvu on ollut jopa negatiivista, kuten Saksassa, Virossa, Irlannissa, It脙陇vallassa ja Suomessa, kun taas joissakin j脙陇senmaissa on ylletty jopa muutaman prosentin kasvuvauhtiin, kuten Espanjassa, Kroatiassa, Maltalla ja Kyproksella.

Inflaation osalta on ollut isojakin alueellisia eroja ja se on vaihdellut euroalueella yhdest脙陇 l脙陇hes viiteen prosenttiin ja oma kotimaani Suomi kuuluu n脙陇ihin ensin mainittuihin. T脙陇m脙陇 kaikki tietenkin vaikuttaa siihen, ett脙陇 EKP:n rahapolitiikka kohtelee eri j脙陇senmaita eri tavoin. Vaikka koronlaskut ovat nyt alkaneet ja korkotaso on alentunut, olisi se voinut joitakin maita ajatellen alkaa jo aikaisemmin ja olla alemmalla tasolla n脙陇it脙陇 maita ajatellen. Nimitt脙陇in liian korkea korkotaso tietenkin lamauttaa taloutta ja aiheuttaa ty脙露tt脙露myytt脙陇, josta erityisesti rakennusala on k脙陇rsinyt.

Euroopan taloudet euroalueella ovat vaihtelevia. Valuutta-alue ei varsinaisesti ole optimaalinen. Miten EKP voi t脙陇m脙陇n huomioida? Sen per脙陇脙陇n kannattaa kysy脙陇.

脗聽脗聽Catarina Martins (The Left). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, os bancos acumulam lucros recorde, mas os sal脙隆rios ainda est脙拢o longe de recuperar o valor real anterior 脙聽 crise inflacionista. 脗聽脗聽Fabio De Masi (NI). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, sehr geehrte Damen und Herren! In Deutschland ist nun die Regierung an der Schuldenbremse zerbrochen. Gleichzeitig soll Frankreich laut den Pl脙陇nen der EU芒聙聭Kommission im n脙陇chsten Etat 110脗聽Milliarden Euro sparen. 脗聽脗聽L脙颅dia Pereira (PPE). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, caros Colegas, vamos ser claros. A Europa vai crescer, mas vai crescer pouco. A infla脙搂脙拢o vai reduzir, mas vai reduzir pouco. O d脙漏fice vai baixar, mas vai baixar pouco.

脗聽脗聽Jo脙拢o Oliveira (The Left), Pergunta segundo o procedimento 脗芦cart脙拢o azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 Nestas previs脙碌es de Outono, a Comiss脙拢o Europeia faz recomenda脙搂脙碌es de que Portugal se concentre na consolida脙搂脙拢o or脙搂amental, que mantenha excedentes or脙搂amentais, que tenha saldos prim脙隆rios positivos, ou seja: conten脙搂脙拢o dos gastos com os trabalhadores.

脗聽脗聽Jo脙拢o Cotrim De Figueiredo (Renew). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhor Comiss脙隆rio Hahn, na previs脙拢o econ脙鲁mica de Outono, a Comiss脙拢o tenta dar-nos raz脙碌es para otimismo. Tenta, mas n脙拢o consegue, porque crescimento de 0,9 % em 2025 e 1,8 % em 2026 脙漏 muito pouco e tudo parece demasiado fr脙隆gil.

脗聽脗聽Marina Mesure (The Left). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, parler de rebond 脙漏conomique alors que nous entrons dans une crise sociale d'ampleur, il fallait oser.

L'h脙漏morragie dans l'industrie est sans pr脙漏c脙漏dent, car, partout en Europe, nos usines ferment les unes apr脙篓s les autres; rien qu'en France, 46 sont actuellement menac脙漏es. Pour l'industrie automobile et la sid脙漏rurgie, on parle d'une perte potentielle de 15脗聽millions d'emplois. Et ce n'est que le d脙漏but de l'effet domino qui s'amplifie et qui touche des secteurs interd脙漏pendants, par exemple 70脗聽% des d脙漏bouch脙漏s industriels d'ArcelorMittal, Dunkerque et l'automobile脗聽芒聙聯 tout est li脙漏.

Mais ne soyons pas na脙炉fs pour autant: certaines multinationales utilisent le contexte 脙漏conomique pour faire toujours plus de profits. Je pense notamment 脙聽 ArcelorMittal, qui a d脙漏gag脙漏 800脗聽millions de b脙漏n脙漏fices cette ann脙漏e et a pourtant licenci脙漏 des centaines de travailleurs.

Alors, quelles solutions pour ce rebond 脙漏conomique? Le protectionnisme, la r脙漏gulation des prix de l'脙漏nergie, l'investissement public et le conditionnement essentiel des aides publiques au maintien de l'emploi. Mais certainement pas plus de libre-脙漏change, la lib脙漏ralisation comme vous le proposez, alors m脙陋me que Trump s'appr脙陋te 脙聽 nous lancer une v脙漏ritable guerre commerciale. Agissons et vite.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Aura Salla (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, yes, the Commission's autumn forecast shows a slight uptick in our economic outlook. But the challenges in the horizon are rising: protectionism, escalating geopolitical tensions led by Putin and Trump, and the crisis situation in the Middle East.

Our economic stability and growth rely on open markets and rule-based ambitious trade. We need to ensure European companies have the ability to grow in an enabling home market. We need to act fast, finalise trade agreements, diversify supply chains, strengthen alliance on data use with the US, build rapidly one single entry point for our companies entering the EU market, harmonise and speed up our licensing processes, especially when it comes to the food sector, and make sure we have enough risk capital for our companies to grow and that there is a skilful workforce for them.

The clock is ticking, Europe. We must act fast and lead, not react and follow.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Irene Tinagli (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, let's face it, while we've been pretty good in overcoming the pandemic and the energy crisis, we are not as successful in relaunching our economy in the context of the great transitions that we are facing.

This is linked in part, of course, to persistent geopolitical uncertainty, but also to our own policies. Extraordinary measures such as the NextGenerationEU, SURE, the ECB's pandemic-purchase programme that have helped us out of the crisis have ended or are about to end.

Monetary policy has been restrictive since 2022, and only recently there has been a timid change of direction. New fiscal rules are in place, and judging by the multi-annual plans presented by the Member States, the euro area's aggregate fiscal stance risks being restrictive for years to come.

So a common initiative is urgently needed to relaunch the competitiveness of our economy and to steer it towards sustainable growth. I hope that the new Commission will rise to these challenges, also ensuring adequate public funding to tackle these challenges. Without such efforts, the Union as a whole will struggle to adapt to these transformations and the price will be paid by our citizens.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jorge Mart脙颅n Fr脙颅as (PfE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidente, se脙卤or脙颅as, las previsiones econ脙鲁micas de la Comisi脙鲁n reflejan, de nuevo, un crecimiento pobre cuya causa radica en el fanatismo ideol脙鲁gico de las pol脙颅ticas de la Uni脙鲁n; un fanatismo que empobrece e impide las leg脙颅timas aspiraciones de nuestros compatriotas de prosperar y mejorar las condiciones de vida de sus familias.

Hay que desechar toda la industria pol脙颅tica e ideol脙鲁gica que lastra la riqueza de las naciones. Hay dos caminos: subordinar la econom脙颅a a criterios ideol脙鲁gicos y sectarios con dinero de los contribuyentes europeos o seguir el ejemplo de la Argentina de Javier Milei y de las medidas anunciadas por Donald Trump.

Un camino genera pobreza y nos ahoga en impuestos y burocracia; otro favorece la creaci脙鲁n de empresas, de empleos dignos y de oportunidades para nuestros j脙鲁venes.

Se脙卤or脙颅as, guste o no, cada vez m脙隆s europeos se van a inclinar por el camino de la libertad y de la prosperidad. La Comisi脙鲁n puede adelantarse y tomar nota o, de lo contrario, poco a poco y a trav脙漏s de las distintas citas electorales, no le quedar脙隆 otro remedio que tomar esas medidas.

脗聽脗聽Andreas Schwab (PPE). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Herr Kommissar, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Der Ausblick der Europ脙陇ischen Union zeigt, die Inflation ist in den letzten Jahren von 6,4脗聽% im vergangenen Jahr auf 2,6脗聽% gesunken. N脙陇chstes Jahr werden 2,0脗聽% erwartet. Die Arbeitslosenquote bleibt mit 5,9脗聽% auf historischem Tiefstand. Das Wachstum wird 2024 allerdings bei nur 0,9脗聽% liegen und bis 2026 EU芒聙聭weit nur auf 1,8脗聽% steigen. Diese Zahlen zeigen, dass wir in der Europ脙陇ischen Union unsere Hausaufgaben noch nicht vollst脙陇ndig gemacht haben.脗聽Ich sage das ganz besonders auf mein Heimatland bezogen 芒聙聯 auf Deutschland. Denn wir d脙录rfen uns von dieser schrittweisen Erholung der Zahlen nicht t脙陇uschen lassen. Die Investitionen sind im Jahr 2024 um 2,5脗聽% gesunken, und globale Krisen belasten unsere Wirtschaft. Das Besch脙陇ftigungswachstum in der EU 芒聙聯 der Draghi芒聙聭Bericht weist darauf hin 芒聙聯 wird sich voraussichtlich von 0,8脗聽% im Jahr 2024 auf 0,5脗聽% im Jahr 2026 verlangsamen: ein Zeichen f脙录r schwache Innovationskraft und geringe Unternehmensdynamik.

Deswegen, meine Damen und Herren, m脙露chte ich darauf hinweisen: Die Herbstprognose ist ein Anzeichen daf脙录r, dass die Arbeit der Europ脙陇ischen Union jetzt schnellstm脙露glich beginnen muss. Wir brauchen weniger Regulierung und mehr Investitionen. Mehr in die Wertsch脙露pfung investieren m脙录ssen wir, weniger Berichtspflichten, und wir brauchen mehr vertrauensbasierte Regulierung.

脗聽脗聽Carla Tavares (S&D). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhor Comiss脙隆rio, as previs脙碌es econ脙鲁micas da Comiss脙拢o mostram que nos pr脙鲁ximos tr脙陋s anos a Uni脙拢o Europeia continuar脙隆 a afastar-se face aos Estados Unidos. S脙鲁 em 2024, os Estados Unidos v脙拢o crescer o triplo da Uni脙拢o Europeia. At脙漏 2026, a Europa continuar脙隆 a crescer menos, investir menos, inovar e gerar menos emprego que os Estados Unidos. E estes s脙拢o dados preocupantes.

O programa da nova Comiss脙拢o e o desenho do pr脙鲁ximo or脙搂amento p脙鲁s 2027, s脙拢o duas oportunidades 脙潞nicas para avan脙搂armos com medidas europeias urgentes, ambiciosas e inovadoras e quebrarmos alguns tabus. Num contexto de crescente inseguran脙搂a, protecionismo e desigualdades, temos de ter a capacidade de acelerar a inova脙搂脙拢o e a competitividade, mas, ao mesmo tempo, e com a mesma ambi脙搂脙拢o, temos de resolver problemas estruturais para a nossa coes脙拢o social interna, como 脙漏 o caso da habita脙搂脙拢o e o aumento do custo de vida.

O custo da habita脙搂脙拢o tem crescido muito acima da infla脙搂脙拢o e s脙拢o necess脙隆rios apoios e investimentos ao n脙颅vel europeu para apoiar os governos. N脙拢o nos iludamos. Sem acesso a habita脙搂脙拢o acess脙颅vel e justa para todos, ser脙隆 mais dif脙颅cil convencer os europeus a refor脙搂ar os investimentos tamb脙漏m importantes, como a transi脙搂脙拢o clim脙隆tica e digital ou mais apoios para a defesa.

Senhor Comiss脙隆rio Hahn, permita-me neste momento felicitar pelo trabalho realizado e agradecer-lhe a coopera脙搂脙拢o que sempre tem tido com este Parlamento. Muito obrigada.

脗聽脗聽Paulius Saudargas (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, Commissioner, dear colleagues, while the Commission's autumn forecast predicts a modest return to economic growth in the EU 芒聙聯 0.9脗聽% GDP in 2024 芒聙聯 this cautiously positive outlook must not blind us to the reality. Let us compare that to global growth predictions, which are 3.2脗聽%, United States 2.6脗聽%, and China 4.8脗聽% growth. Who is losing?

Also, it is vital to address geopolitical risks that could undermine this moderate long-term stability. Russia's continued brutal aggression against Ukraine and the escalating conflict in the Middle East impacts energy security and economic resilience.

Growing protectionism in global trade will harm EU's export-oriented economy. The automotive sector is under pressure from a shrinking workforce, rising costs of essential raw materials and unfair competition from China. European steel, heavy manufacturing and textile industry faces challenges as well. The European Union must act decisively to strengthen its industrial base, address labour force shortages, secure resilient supply chains, and focus on targeted policy interventions to ensure the competitiveness of our key sectors.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Adnan Dibrani (S&D). 芒聙聯 Fru talman! EU:s prognos har kommit f脙露r 2024 och det b脙露rjar ljusna i ekonomin. Ekonomin 脙楼terh脙陇mtar sig, men det g脙楼r l脙楼ngsamt och det 脙陇r fortfarande tufft f脙露r v脙陇ldigt m脙楼nga d脙陇r ute i v脙楼r union.脗聽Dessutom 脙陇r s脙陇kerhetsl脙陇get oerh脙露rt os脙陇kert.

EU m脙楼ste jobba med sin konkurrenskraft, det tycker jag 脙陇r otroligt viktigt.脗聽Draghi-rapporten har n脙陇mnts h脙陇r tidigare. Det 脙陇r en j脙陇tteviktig rapport. Men Draghi-rapporten efterlyser ocks脙楼 mer medel. Ska man f脙楼 in mer medel i systemet s脙楼 beh脙露ver man jobba med sunda statsfinanser.

Plus och minus m脙楼ste g脙楼 ihop i respektive medlemsland.脗聽Man m脙楼ste anpassa kostymen utifr脙楼n sina utgifter, man m脙楼ste v脙楼ga omprioritera, man m脙楼ste v脙楼ga prioritera och man m脙楼ste ha respekt f脙露r alla de m脙陇nniskor i v脙楼r union som betalar in skattemedel. Det tycker jag 脙陇r A脗聽och脗聽O.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

Procedura "catch-the-eye"

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Majdouline Sbai (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, l'analyse de la Commission que vous nous pr脙漏sentez sur les pr脙漏visions 脙漏conomiques se veut optimiste. Mais pour qui et pour quoi?

Derri脙篓re les mots 脗芦r脙漏duction des d脙漏ficits脗禄, il y a une r脙漏alit脙漏. La semaine derni脙篓re, les maires et les 脙漏lus locaux ont manifest脙漏 en France, car la r脙漏duction de 5脗聽milliards de d脙漏penses publiques leur est devenue insoutenable. 脙聙 quoi devront-ils renoncer? Accueillir les enfants plac脙漏s? R脙漏nover les 脙漏coles? Conserver un service de sant脙漏? 脙聙 quoi?

Une autre r脙漏alit脙漏, ce sont les salari脙漏s d'ArcelorMittal qui, 脙聽 l'heure o脙鹿 je vous parle, manifestent pour sauver leurs emplois dans ma r脙漏gion, 脙聽 Denain. Ils sont menac脙漏s 脙聽 cause de la concurrence d脙漏loyale: c'est la moiti脙漏 de l'industrie de l'acier qui est menac脙漏e en France.

Cette r脙漏alit脙漏 nous oblige, 脙聽 moins d'autosatisfaction, 脙聽 sortir de l'aust脙漏rit脙漏 et 脙聽 prot脙漏ger notre 脙漏conomie pour une vraie prosp脙漏rit脙漏 durable et partag脙漏e en Europe.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(Fine della procedura "catch the eye")

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Johannes Hahn, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, to sum up, after the soft landing of 23, the European economy has started expanding again. However, growth remains modest and exposed to increasing risks. Economic policy, uncertainty related to escalating geopolitical tensions and, increasingly, domestic political instability have the potential to further delay firms' investment plans and hamper the projected decline in precautionary savings.

Against this background, the accelerated implementation of the Recovery and Resilience Facility remains a priority to support corporate households and public investment.

Looking beyond the horizon of the Recovery and Resilience Facility, however, the European Union must find ways to combine its large investment needs with the imperative of safeguarding public finances. The capital markets union is part of the answer. More efforts are needed to ensure innovative entrepreneurs can start and scale up new businesses and, more broadly, ensure access to finance for the needs of the EU economy to keep up with major technological innovations.

At the same time, as suggested by Draghi, the EU leadership must be able to devise instruments for funding the provision of those European public goods that will be crucial to ensure its competitiveness and autonomy.

The European Union economy is also facing a more unstable, complex and overall less favourable global trade environment. The European Union will need to navigate the possible trade off between pursuing its open trade agenda, that has been key to its post-war economic expansion, while improving economic security.

As indicated by Letta, the completion of the single market to sectors such as telecommunication, energy and finance is key to build domestic resilience and withstand shocks originated from an increasingly complex geopolitical environment.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Presidente. 芒聙聯 La discussione 脙篓 chiusa.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(La seduta 脙篓 sospesa alle 14.40)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

PRESIDENZA: PINA PICIERNO

脗聽脗聽Presidente. 芒聙聯 Il processo verbale della seduta di ieri e i testi approvati sono stati distribuiti. Vi sono osservazioni? Non mi pare.

Se non vi sono osservazioni, il processo verbale 脙篓 approvato.

11. Question Time with Commissioners - Challenges in the implementation of cohesion policy 2021-2027
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Presidente. 芒聙聯 L'ordine del giorno reca il tempo delle interrogazioni alla Commissione. Porgo il benvenuto alla commissaria Ferreira per il tempo delle interrogazioni che quest'oggi verteranno sul tema "Sfide per l'attuazione della politica di coesione 2021 2027".

Alcune informazioni di servizio. La durata prevista per il tempo delle interrogazioni, come sapete, 脙篓 di 90 minuti. Avrete a disposizione un minuto per formulare l'interrogazione e due minuti per la risposta.

Per il primo turno di oratori sar脙聽 possibile porre una interrogazione supplementare di 30 secondi, con due minuti per la risposta. Vi ricordo che l'eventuale interrogazione supplementare 脙篓 concessa soltanto se strettamente correlata all'interrogazione principale e non consiste in una nuova interrogazione.

Chi intende formulare un'interrogazione 脙篓 invitato a registrare ora la propria richiesta utilizzando la funzione "catch-the-eye" sul dispositivo di voto, dopo aver inserito, ovviamente, la tessera. Durante il tempo delle interrogazioni, i deputati interverranno dal proprio scranno.

Dunque, vi invito sin d'ora a rispettare il tempo di parola assegnato. I deputati potrebbero aver bisogno di alcuni minuti per registrare la loro richiesta tramite il dispositivo di voto. Quindi vi rammento che 脙篓 necessario presentare la vostra richiesta ora e inizieremo sin da subito, dandovi il tempo necessario per registrarvi per le interrogazioni.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sun脛聧ana Glavak (PPE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovana predsjedavaju脛聡a, kolegice i kolege, dolazim iz Hrvatske, najmla脛聭e 脛聧lanice Europske unije, i mislim da je Hrvatska jedan od sjajnih primjera kako zapravo kohezijska politika mo脜戮e promijeniti vizure jedne zemlje.

Do kraja pro脜隆le godine Hrvatska je povukla gotovo 脛聧etiri puta vi脜隆e sredstava nego 脜隆to je uplatila u europski prora脛聧un, ostvariv脜隆i neto dobitak od gotovo 17 milijardi eura. Ovo je sna脜戮an pokazatelj kako 脛聧lanstvo u Europskoj uniji donosi konkretne mjere na脜隆im gra脛聭anima od ulaganja u na脜隆e ceste, bolnice, 脜隆kole, poduze脛聡a, digitalizaciju i zelenu tranziciju. Ali moramo biti iskreni - postoji jo脜隆 prostora za napredak.

Administrativne prepreke i nedostatak lokalnih kapaciteta 脛聧esto usporavaju povla脛聧enje sredstava. Kako Komisija planira podr脜戮ati dr脜戮ave 脛聧lanice koje se suo脛聧avaju s prekomjernim administrativnim optere脛聡enjem u istovremenoj implementaciji vi脜隆egodi脜隆njeg financijskog okvira i nacionalnog plana oporavka i otpornosti? Ho脛聡e li se razmatrati dodatna tehni脛聧ka pomo脛聡 ili fleksibilnost u rokovima kako bi se osiguralo u脛聧inkovito povla脛聧enje sredstava i da - kohezijska sredstva ne smijemo smanjivati.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Elisa Ferreira, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Thank you very much for your question, and thank you, in particular, for testifying how important cohesion policy is and the incredible impact it had in countries such as Croatia.

In fact, Croatia is a good example of how you can grow faster with more resilience and, at the same time, how you can address some unforeseen disasters, because also the two earthquakes that affected so seriously the Zagreb and also the nearby 60脗聽km distance area that was also affected in the first and second earthquakes. There was EUR脗聽1脗聽billion that, in fact, supported this reconstruction.

Now you put an important question: that is how we can do even better. So I think there is a part of the issue has got to be addressed internally by the Member State, and the internal cohesion must be at the core, at the internal agenda.

From our side, we have different ways to help, to support. We have been supporting Member States, and Croatia has been using the technical support instrument with DG REFORM, but also the specific support that is offered by DG REGIO with JASPERS, with technical assistance. But naturally we need to progress and to reinforce this interchange of good practices.
