脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jorge Mart脙颅n Fr脙颅as, en nombre del Grupo PfE. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤or脙颅as, la Comisi脙鲁n ha desempe脙卤ado un lamentable papel desde los terribles atentados cometidos contra Israel el 7 de octubre; un papel en el que ha predominado la equidistancia, cuando no el ataque al Gobierno de Israel.

Situar en el mismo nivel 芒聙聰como acaba de hacer la Corte Penal Internacional y ha abrazado, con entusiasmo, el se脙卤or Borrell芒聙聰 a una organizaci脙鲁n terrorista como Ham脙隆s y al primer ministro de una naci脙鲁n democr脙隆tica como el se脙卤or Benjam脙颅n Netanyahu, denota una falta de claridad moral que demuestra hasta qu脙漏 punto el multilateralismo est脙隆 podrido y tomado por las peores causas e ideas del islamoizquierdismo.

脗驴Cu脙隆ntas 脙鲁rdenes ha emitido la Corte Penal Internacional contra los cabecillas criminales de reg脙颅menes totalitarios como el de La Habana, Caracas, Teher脙隆n y Managua? 脗驴O contra los responsables del UNRWA que han permitido que haya asesinos entre sus empleados?

Se脙卤or脙颅as, Israel debe defenderse y proteger a sus ciudadanos.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Bert-Jan Ruissen, namens de ECR-Fractie. 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, dat de humanitaire situatie in Gaza uitermate zorgelijk is en dat we er daarom alles aan moeten doen om de hulp ter plekke te krijgen, ik denk dat we het daar vandaag snel over eens zijn.

Als dat echter onze belangrijkste zorg is, dan begrijp ik niet waarom de Commissie en sommige collega芒聙聶s alleen maar naar UNRWA kijken. Alsof dat de enige instantie is die zou kunnen helpen. Dat is namelijk niet zo. Er zijn wel degelijk alternatieven. Denk aan UNOCHA, UNHCR, het World Food Programme. Instanties die erop zijn ingericht om op heel korte termijn noodhulp te bieden in crisisgebieden. Zeker nu Isra脙芦l UNRWA terecht in de ban doet vanwege de banden met Hamas, moeten we wel andere instanties gaan inzetten.

Laten we bij dat alles ook de gijzelaars inderdaad niet vergeten. Ook vandaag zeggen we: Hamas, laat ze gaan!

Tegen lidstaten die zich nu blind achter het ICC scharen en zeggen bereid te zijn Netanyahu te arresteren, zeg ik: u vergist zich! Landen hebben hierin namelijk ook een eigen verantwoordelijkheid en hebben op zijn minst de plicht na te gaan of de onderbouwing van het arrestatiebevel wel deugt. Daar zijn namelijk hele grote vraagtekens bij te zetten.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hilde Vautmans, namens de Renew-Fractie. 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, het wordt moeilijk om me met mijn speech aan mijn blad te houden na zulke uitlatingen van collega Ruissen, maar ik ga het toch maar doen.

De oorlog in Isra脙芦l en Palestina houdt Europa een fameuze spiegel voor. Wij hebben hier, collega Ruissen, altijd de mond vol over het internationaal recht wanneer het over China of Rusland gaat. Nu echter die westerse bondgenoot geconfronteerd wordt met datzelfde internationaal recht, horen we een oorverdovende stilte, en zelfs protest en ontkenning hier op de banken. Als het arrestatiebevel van het Internationaal Strafhof genegeerd wordt, is het internationaal strafrecht niets meer waard.

Wat me minstens evenveel zorgen baart, is de dramatische humanitaire situatie daar. Het is onwaarschijnlijk wat daar gebeurt. V脙鲁脙鲁r de aanslagen 500脗聽vrachtwagens per maand, nu nog脗聽37.

Ik heb hier heel veel analyses gehoord. Ik heb twee heel concrete vragen. Ten eerste: Hoe gaat Europa ervoor zorgen dat het Internationaal Strafhof en de uitspraken gerespecteerd worden door alle Europese lidstaten? En ten tweede: Hoe gaat u ervoor zorgen dat UNRWA nu, onmiddellijk toegang krijgt? Er is, collega Ruissen, geen alternatief 芒聙娄 (De Voorzitter ontneemt de spreker het woord)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Catarina Vieira, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear Commissioner, international law emerged as a safeguard against the horrors that humanity should never repeat. But that safeguard is under immense pressure today.

In Gaza, we see unthinkable destruction, mass displacement, civilians that are deliberately targeted and starvation being used as a method of warfare. So, naturally, internationally recognised judicial bodies are ringing all the alarm bells about Israeli war crimes. The ICJ concluded that there is a risk of genocide. The ICC issued an arrest warrant against Prime Minister Netanyahu.

But what does this change? Israel is still a major Western partner. Our weapons are still being exported there, our agreements are still not being questioned. Some Member States are even outright dismissing the ICC decision. If we apply double standards for Israel, why would anyone adhere to the rules? International law only works if it's strongly enforced, regardless of the perpetrator, and that is what the European Union must do.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lynn Boylan, on behalf of The Left Group. 芒聙聯 A Uachtar脙隆in, the international order is very clear. Targeted attacks against civilian infrastructure, with the clear aim to cut off men, women and children from water, electricity and heating are pure acts of terror.

These are war crimes. I absolutely agree, and so did President von der Leyen, because they are her words in October 2022. However, fast forward to November 2024 and the ICC have issued arrest warrants for Netanyahu and Yoav Gallant, citing starvation and the deliberate deprivation of food, water and medical supplies to a civilian population as the basis for the charges of war crimes and crimes against humanity.

Yet there has been radio silence from President von der Leyen and frankly, the mealy-mouthed responses from some Member States have been shameful. The EU's credibility is in shreds, not just in Palestine and the Global South, but even within its own Member States. Israel is driving a coach and horses through the institutions of international law and multilateralism, and they are being aided and abetted by these institutions.

Arming Israel is complicity with genocide. Trading with Israel is complicity. Absence of sanctions is complicity. International law depends on the willingness of governments to support justice, no matter where the abuses are committed or by whom. The failure to defend international law is a threat to all of us. This is not about politics, it's not about personalities 芒聙聯 it's about justice. It's time to sanction Israel.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Antonio L脙鲁pez-Ist脙潞riz White (PPE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, una vez m脙隆s estamos reunidos aqu脙颅, como cada mes desde hace un a脙卤o, para seguir discutiendo sobre este terrible conflicto, sin aportar ninguna soluci脙鲁n desde esta Casa. Pero eso s脙颅, servir脙隆 芒聙聰me imagino芒聙聰 para justificar las redes sociales y las notas de prensa de la ultraizquierda.

Por favor, dejen de utilizar el sufrimiento de los pueblos palestinos e israel脙颅es para sus campa脙卤as pol脙颅ticas, se脙卤ores de la ultraizquierda, y aporten soluciones ya de una vez. Soluciones que yo mismo pienso estudiar si reconocen que Ham脙隆s fue la que empez脙鲁 esa tragedia, si reconocen que Ir脙隆n financia y apoya a Ham脙隆s y a Hizbul脙隆, si piden la liberaci脙鲁n de los rehenes israel脙颅es, si condenan las torturas, las violaciones y los asesinatos de Ham脙隆s sobre ciudadanos, especialmente mujeres, israel脙颅es, si reconocen el derecho de Israel a existir y a no ser borrada del mapa, como asociaciones con las que ustedes trabajan piden diariamente.

Si confirman estos cinco puntos, entonces merecer脙隆 la pena venir aqu脙颅 a debatir en este pleno.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nacho S脙隆nchez Amor (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤or L脙鲁pez-Ist脙潞riz, yo compro esas cinco premisas. Es dif脙颅cil encontrar nuevas palabras, pero vamos a intentarlo.

Los dobles est脙隆ndares son el c脙隆ncer de la pol脙颅tica exterior de la Uni脙鲁n Europea: erosionan gravemente nuestra credibilidad en el mundo y, especialmente, en el sur global. Y, a pesar de ello, insistimos en exhibirlos con frivolidad cada vez que tratamos la guerra de agresi脙鲁n rusa y la monstruosa desproporci脙鲁n del invocado derecho de autoprotecci脙鲁n de Israel.

La autom脙隆tica acusaci脙鲁n de antisemitismo ante cualquier cr脙颅tica est脙隆 vaciando de sentido el concepto: si todos somos antisemitas, al final nadie lo es; estamos castrando el grave contenido de esa acusaci脙鲁n de tanto manosearlo. El pueblo jud脙颅o est脙隆 perdiendo un escudo, una protecci脙鲁n que ha conservado, hasta ahora, todo el sentido hist脙鲁rico y toda su capacidad de proteger.

No hay un Derecho internacional para Rusia y otro para Israel: son el mismo Derecho internacional, y nuestra posici脙鲁n tiene que ser la misma ante similares violaciones. Y eso, por supuesto, incluye los mandatos de detenci脙鲁n de los tribunales internacionales, que merecen nuestra protecci脙鲁n, para que, tras la guerra, no queden igual de arrasados que la Franja de Gaza.

No hemos podido salvar a Gaza; salvemos, al menos, la justicia internacional.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jaroslav B脜戮och (PfE). 芒聙聯 Pane p脜聶edsedaj脙颅c脙颅, j脙隆 se budu v脛聸novat v t脙漏to debat脛聸 pouze jedn脙漏 脛聧脙隆sti, a to je role agentury UNRWA. Je opravdu ta role nezbytn脙隆? Je opravdu tak z脙隆sadn脙颅, nebo je agentura UNRWA sou脛聧脙隆st脙颅 cel脙漏ho probl脙漏mu? Pokud vezmeme v 脙潞vahu, 脜戮e agentura UNRWA funguje u脜戮 dek脙隆dy a dek脙隆dy let, tak vlastn脛聸 podporuje generace z脙隆visl脙漏 na mezin脙隆rodn脙颅 pomoci bez jasn脙漏ho pl脙隆nu na ukon脛聧en脙颅 uprchlick脙陆ch status脜炉. Nejde pouze o humanit脙隆rn脙颅 pomoc. UNRWA d脛聸l脙隆 samoz脜聶ejm脛聸 i ostatn脙颅 projekty, nap脜聶脙颅klad 脜隆kolstv脙颅. Co se u脛聧脙颅 ve 脜隆kolstv脙颅? Co vyu脛聧uj脙颅 zam脛聸stnanci agentury UNRWA? Je to d脜戮ih脙隆d nap脜聶脙颅klad v脜炉脛聧i Izraeli? Je to d脜戮ih脙隆d srdcem nebo me脛聧em? Jak m脜炉脜戮e b脙陆t agentura nez脙隆visl脙隆, pokud vezmeme v 脙潞vahu, 脜戮e b脙陆val脙陆 脜隆脙漏f se opakovan脛聸 sch脙隆zel s teroristick脙陆mi organizacemi? O to z脙隆va脜戮n脛聸j脜隆脙颅 jsou informace o mo脜戮n脙漏m zapojen脙颅 zam脛聸stnanc脜炉 do hr脜炉zn脙漏ho teroristick脙漏ho 脙潞toku dne 7. 脜聶脙颅jna 2023. UNRWA nedok脙隆zala zajistit neutralitu person脙隆lu, ale umo脜戮nila, aby infrastruktura agentury byla zneu脜戮ita k 脜隆脙颅脜聶en脙颅 nen脙隆visti a n脙隆sil脙颅. Pokud opravdu chceme, aby ta role byla nezbytn脙隆, tak mus脙颅 okam脜戮it脛聸 doj脙颅t k reorganizaci cel脙漏 agentury, proto脜戮e v tuto chv脙颅li je sp脙颅脜隆e sou脛聧脙隆st脙颅 probl脙漏mu ne脜戮 jeho 脜聶e脜隆en脙颅m.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rihards Kols (ECR). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, dear colleagues, the escalation in the Middle East calls for moral clarity and actionable pragmatism. Israel has every right to defend itself against terror and peace cannot be built while hostages remain captive. We demand their unconditional and immediate release.

But let us address another obstacle to stability: UNRWA. This organisation 芒聙聯 intended as temporary measure 芒聙聯 has evolved into a bureaucratic nightmare, from educational curriculums promoting hatred to the misuse of facilities by Hamas, it has abdicated neutrality and failed its mandate.

Alternatives do exist, as we have heard in public expert discourse. Yet, Mr脗聽Borrell has continued to fuel anti芒聙聭Israel sentiment, shifting the Overton window to dangerous extremes.

This failure of balance does not foster peace. It undermines it. Events like the recent pogrom芒聙聭like violence in Amsterdam show us the consequences of this recklessness.

Peace in the Middle East demands engagement from all regional players and solutions that address the root issues, not one芒聙聭sided narratives or institutional inertia.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Barry Andrews (Renew). 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner, I would first of all say that Mr脗聽Kols is wrong, of course:脗聽the European Union carried out a very extensive evaluation to satisfy itself that there is no risk whatsoever of the diversion of EU funding from its intended purpose. That has been established time and time again by the European Commission.

I would call on the President of the Commission to make a statement putting the full weight of the European Commission behind a request to the Government of Israel to request the Knesset to extend the 90-day period, because, as everybody knows, there is no replacement for UNRWA.

As our HR/VP said, all ICC decisions are binding on all Member States. Despite the overwhelming support of EU Member States, we have had no statement from the President of the Commission, and I think it is reasonable to conclude that that silence undermines the legitimacy of the Court and of international law.

The EU Action Plan on the ICC states: 'The EU and its Member States will undertake consistent action to encourage full cooperation of states with the ICC, including the prompt execution of arrest warrants'.

(The speaker declined to take a blue-card question from Rihards Kols)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alice Kuhnke (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Herr talman! Krigsbrott, brott mot m脙陇nskligheten 芒聙聯 det allvarligaste och mest avskyv脙陇rda som vi m脙陇nniskor kan g脙露ra mot varandra 芒聙聯 det 脙陇r vad ni som v脙陇grar att g脙露ra vad som kr脙陇vs f脙露r att stoppa Netanyahu har m脙露jliggjort.

Alla de civila 芒聙聯 som mest 脙陇r kvinnor och barn 芒聙聯 som d脙露dats i Israels anfall eller som d脙露tt av sv脙陇lt p脙楼 grund av att mat, medicin och annan n脙露dhj脙陇lp medvetet h脙楼lls borta f脙楼r vi aldrig tillbaka. Inte heller gisslan som d脙露dats i Hamas tunnlar.脗聽Men ni har fortfarande chansen att t脙陇nka om och g脙露ra r脙陇tt. Uppr脙陇tth脙楼ll ICC:s arresteringsordrar. Pausa EU:s associeringsavtal med Israel. Inf脙露r omedelbart vapenembargo. Och 脙露ka st脙露det till UNRWA.

脗聽脗聽Jo脙拢o Oliveira (The Left). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, quando propusemos este debate, tentaram trav脙隆-lo, mas a gravidade da decis脙拢o de Israel de tentar impedir a a脙搂脙拢o da UNRWA tornou imposs脙颅vel impedi-lo. Aqui continuamos a intervir pelos direitos do povo palestiniano, como na iniciativa que protagoniz脙隆mos neste mesmo Parlamento Europeu, dando voz aos palestinianos e 脙聽 pr脙鲁pria UNRWA, atrav脙漏s de Marta Lorenzo, sua diretora para a Europa.

Disse-nos que o que est脙隆 em causa n脙拢o 脙漏 a ag脙陋ncia, 脙漏 o povo palestiniano que serve, as suas vidas, os seus direitos. S脙拢o as 350 000 crian脙搂as palestinianas a quem a UNRWA garante educa脙搂脙拢o nos territ脙鲁rios ilegalmente ocupados. 脙聣 a garantia de metade da resposta humanit脙隆ria e 2/3 dos cuidados de sa脙潞de prim脙隆rios na Faixa de Gaza.

H脙隆 que travar Israel nesta tentativa de impedir a a脙搂脙拢o da UNRWA, criada em 49 para acudir aos refugiados palestinianos, refugiados que Israel quer condenar 脙聽 fome e 脙聽 doen脙搂a e retirar da equa脙搂脙拢o pol脙颅tica. O papel da UNRWA 脙漏 fundamental. A quest脙拢o que resta 脙漏: o que vai fazer a UE para o defender?

脗聽脗聽Grzegorz Braun (NI). 芒聙聯 Panie Przewodnicz脛聟cy, trwa kolejna debata na temat ludob脙鲁jstwa w Gazie, na temat ludob脙鲁jstwa, kt脙鲁re dokonywane jest tam przez terrorystyczne pa脜聞stwo 脜录ydowskie. Ale debata b脛聶dzie ja脜聜owa, poniewa脜录 w tym samym czasie jeden z premier脙鲁w pa脜聞stwa cz脜聜onkowskiego zaprasza ludob脙鲁jczego premiera pa脜聞stwa po脜聜o脜录onego w Palestynie i gwarantuje mu nietykalno脜聸脛聡 w sytuacji, gdy mi脛聶dzynarodowy Trybuna脜聜 upomina si脛聶 o pana Netanjahu i jego rze脜潞nik脙鲁w.

Mo脜录e wi脛聶c czas najwy脜录szy podnie脜聸脛聡 t脛聶 debat脛聶 na wy脜录szy poziom? I mo脜录e czas og脜聜osi脛聡 koniec eksperymentu, jakim jest 脜录ydowskie terrorystyczne pa脜聞stwo w Palestynie? To pa脜聞stwo powsta脜聜o moc脛聟 demokratycznego g脜聜osowania w Organizacji Narod脙鲁w Zjednoczonych przed laty. Proponuj脛聶 g脜聜osowanie, proponuj脛聶 inicjatyw脛聶 Unii Europejskiej: wnie脜聸my do Narod脙鲁w Zjednoczonych t脛聶 kwesti脛聶 i przeg脜聜osujmy zako脜聞czenie eksperymentu, kt脙鲁ry ko脜聞czy si脛聶 ludob脙鲁jstwem. Niekt脙鲁rzy m脙鲁wi脛聟 o rozwi脛聟zaniu dwupa脜聞stwowym. Popr脛聶 to rozwi脛聟zanie, je脜聸li...

(Przewodnicz脛聟cy odebra脜聜 m脙鲁wcy g脜聜os)

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Mandl (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, dear colleagues, (inaudible) defend democracy and the rule of law, separation of powers, independent justice, freedom of speech and opinion, freedom of press.

These are pillars our civilisation is about. And who is fighting at the utmost frontier for this civilisation and for these values? It's the Israeli Defence Forces, it's the Israeli government and it's the Israeli people in the first place.

So, I very much listen also to courts, but I also very much use my right to say my opinion in the framework of freedom of speech, freedom of expression.

I think Churchill won the Second World War, not alone, but he was the leader. Netanyahu will win the war for this very civilisation, not alone, but he is the leader.

He will be politically defined by the people of Israel in the first place because this is a democratic country 芒聙聯 and this is one other of the pillars of our civilisation.脗聽This is also what people in The Hague, decision takers there should understand.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Evin Incir (S&D). 芒聙聯 Mr President, let's be clear about the actions of some individuals and political groups in here. It is hypocrisy to question the ICC arrest warrant. It is shameful that the far-right groups and the conservative EPP attempted to block this issue from today's agenda. I expected such behaviour from anti-democrats, but what justification does the EPP Group have?

The ICC arrest warrants for Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu, former Defence Minister Gallant and Hamas commander-in-chief Deif should not be questioned by anyone. It is an obligation of all our Member States to implement it. It is their obligation 芒聙聯 not question it, by anyone.

Respect for international law must be maintained consistently, regardless of who is the aggressor and who is the victim. And as far as I know, there is not an international law for Israel and another international law for the rest of the world.

There is no difference between perpetrators and those silently and actively watching people being killed. Don't forget that silence is to be complicit. Stop defending war crimes, for God's sake!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Elena Yoncheva (Renew). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, dear colleagues, the humanitarian situation in Gaza is an urgent matter that affects hundreds of thousands of Palestinian refugees.脗聽We must acknowledge the essential role played by the humanitarian agencies actively working on the ground.

UNRWA is a leading organisation providing food, shelter and health care to nearly 2脗聽million脗聽people and it's responsible for 63脗聽% of primary health care just in Gaza. It's the only UN organisation, UN agency educating more than 350脗聽000 children in Gaza and in the West Bank. Those services are crucial for the well芒聙聭being and dignity of the people there.

Yesterday, positive news regarding the possible peace deal for Lebanon offers a hope, a powerful reminder that peace is achievable. We must extend similar efforts to Gaza, promoting a two芒聙聭state solution. As the Union, we have the capacity to play a more active role in fostering peace.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ana Miranda Paz (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, ahora mismo, 600脗聽000 ni脙卤os necesitan vacunas contra la polio en Palestina. Hasta un mill脙鲁n de personas han encontrado refugio durante la guerra gracias al trabajo y esfuerzo del UNRWA sobre el terreno. Ser脙颅a una cat脙隆strofe humanitaria, se脙卤ora comisaria, prohibir la presencia del UNRWA en Gaza y Cisjordania.

No existe un plan B por parte del Gobierno de Netanyahu; si se proh脙颅be la presencia del UNRWA, nadie proporcionar脙隆 ninguna ayuda a los palestinos. Repito: nadie. Sin embargo, Israel s脙颅 tiene un plan A, y ese plan consiste en dos pasos, se脙卤ores de la extrema derecha: limpieza 脙漏tnica y genocidio; porque lo que est脙隆 cometiendo es un genocidio.

La Corte Penal Internacional emiti脙鲁 un mandato claro: Netanyahu es un criminal de guerra, y los criminales de guerra tienen que ser perseguidos tambi脙漏n en Europa. Sin embargo, la se脙卤ora Von der Leyen 芒聙聰que estar脙隆 aqu脙颅 ma脙卤ana芒聙聰 ni siquiera se molest脙鲁 en hacer un comentario al respecto. Una verdadera verg脙录enza.

Dejen de socavar el Derecho internacional y humanitario y los derechos humanos y cuiden a la poblaci脙鲁n palestina, que est脙隆 pasando hambre, est脙隆 sin hospitales y, sobre todo, est脙隆 siendo asesinada. 脗隆Viva el pueblo palestino!

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hildegard Bentele (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, ladies and gentlemen, there is one single action that could decisively change the situation in Gaza: the release of the hostages by Hamas, unlike an ICC arrest warrant that oversteps its competences. It's been 417脗聽days!

The State of Israel has had to fight repeatedly for its survival. The attack of 7脗聽October was not spontaneous: it was backed by extensive military preparations and significant funding. Israel, understandably, is now determined once and for all to ensure its security with all its might.

However, the release of the hostages by Hamas would put significant pressure on Israel to alter its current actions in Gaza. Releasing 100脗聽hostages in exchange for improving the humanitarian conditions for millions of Palestinians 芒聙聯 only terrorists devoid of any humanity can reject that logic. Israel is offering USD脗聽5脗聽million per hostage to anyone who assists in their liberation.

Blurring the lines between terrorists and civilians, as well as the tolerance of terrorism, is one of the greatest threats to any society. I hope more and more people will become aware and engaged in this fight, and draw a clear line between those who are willing to embrace the values of life, dignity and humanity, and those who don't.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marta Temido (S&D). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, Senhora Comiss脙隆ria, o 脙潞ltimo relat脙鲁rio de avalia脙搂脙拢o do Comit脙漏 Especial da ONU 脙漏 inequ脙颅voco. Em Gaza, os palestinianos s脙拢o alvo de a脙搂脙碌es que intencionalmente lhes s脙拢o apenas dirigidas por serem palestinianos, de condi脙搂脙碌es de exist脙陋ncia fatais, com priva脙搂脙拢o de 脙隆gua e alimentos, de assassinatos em massa e atentados 脙聽 sua integridade f脙颅sica e ps脙颅quica, de desumaniza脙搂脙拢o permanente. Em suma, de uma pol脙颅tica com caracter脙颅sticas consistentes com aquilo que 脙漏 um genoc脙颅dio.

J脙隆 na Cisjord脙垄nia, onde a expans脙拢o dos colonatos israelitas e a restri脙搂脙拢o de direitos econ脙鲁micos, sociais e culturais dos palestinianos constituem efetiva discrimina脙搂脙拢o, vive-se um regime de apartheid. Foi neste contexto que o TPI emitiu mandados de deten脙搂脙拢o, nomeadamente em rela脙搂脙拢o ao primeiro-ministro Netanyahu, por responsabilidade criminal, 脙聽 luz do direito internacional.脗聽Esta n脙拢o 脙漏 uma decis脙拢o pol脙颅tica. 脙聣 uma decis脙拢o judicial. E cumpre aos 123 signat脙隆rios do Estatuto de Roma, nomeadamente os 27 Estados芒聙聭Membros da Uni脙拢o Europeia, garantir o seu cumprimento.

Mas sabemos bem que, para a popula脙搂脙拢o de Gaza e Cisjord脙垄nia, confrontada com uma estrat脙漏gia para lhe tornar a vida insuport脙隆vel,脗聽confrontada com o novo modelo de guerra sem precedentes, o cumprimento deste mandato chegar脙隆 sempre tarde de mais. Por isso, n脙拢o deixaremos de insistir para que os Estados-Membros da Uni脙拢o honrem a sua especial responsabilidade hist脙鲁rica e usem a sua diplomacia comum, agora mais do que nunca.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Irena Joveva (Renew). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, I used to believe that the European Union embodies its core values, but how can anyone still believe that? What we are witnessing is crimes against humanity. Israel went as far as to making targeted deadly attacks on UNRWA staff and property, and when that didn't make them leave, they decided to ban them.

Still, the EU's silence and failure to condemn Israel's actions means we are de facto supporting it. I'll admit, I was hopeful last week that the EU will stand united in backing of the ICC ruling to issue arrest warrants against senior Israel leaders and Hamas officials, as we portray ourselves as defenders of the rule of law.

Yet, once again, the deeply rooted double standards have prevailed. You should be ashamed. And to all of you who still pick and choose when and for what you uphold EU values, there is no decision to be made. Do better colleagues and be quiet when the children are sleeping, not when they are being killed.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Emma Fourreau (The Left). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, s'en prendre 脙聽 l'UNRWA, comme le fait Isra脙芦l, c'est affirmer d脙漏finitivement que l'on veut la fin du peuple palestinien. Mettre en doute sa neutralit脙漏, jusqu'ici en Europe, c'est affirmer d脙漏finitivement que le droit international n'a plus aucune valeur aux yeux des d脙漏fenseurs du g脙漏nocide.

Les pires criminels de guerre, toujours, pi脙漏tinent le droit international et entravent l'action humanitaire. Isra脙芦l d脙漏truit, massacre les femmes, affame les enfants, ass脙篓che les ressources vitales pour les Palestiniens pour les regarder mourir 脙聽 petit feu.

Le boucher Netanyahou veut couper la derni脙篓re main tendue 脙聽 la Palestine et porter le coup fatal 脙聽 une population plong脙漏e dans un effroyable d脙漏sastre humanitaire, que les ONG et les m脙漏decins constatent avec impuissance.

Isra脙芦l interdit les activit脙漏s de l'UNRWA; l'Union europ脙漏enne doit r脙漏pondre avec fermet脙漏, en suspendant enfin l'accord d'association et en r脙漏affirmant son soutien aux actions de l'ONU. Tous les 脙聣tats membres ont le devoir d'ex脙漏cuter le mandat d'arr脙陋t d脙漏livr脙漏 par la Cour p脙漏nale internationale 脙聽 l'endroit du criminel de guerre Netanyahou.

脗芦L'affirmation de la paix est le plus grand des combats脗禄, disait Jean脗聽Jaur脙篓s. Ajoutons aujourd'hui qu'il n'y a pas de paix sans justice. Justice pour le peuple palestinien!

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Davor Ivo Stier (PPE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovani predsjedavaju脛聡i, kolegice i kolege, slu脜隆aju脛聡i neke rasprave, mogli bismo se zapitati je li postoji razlika izme脛聭u Rusije i Izraela. Njihovi lideri imaju optu脜戮nice Me脛聭unarodnog kaznenog suda. Me脛聭utim, ipak postoji jedna fundamentalna razlika. Ona je sljede脛聡a: 24. velja脛聧e 2022. Rusija je pokrenula agresiju. 7. listopada 2023. Izrael je bio 脜戮rtva agresije. I to je klju脛聧na razlika.

Kad se neki pitaju za脜隆to se tretiraju razli脛聧ito Rusija i Izrael - zato. Jer je jedan pokrenuo agresiju, drugo je bila 脜戮rtva agresije. To ne zna脛聧i da 脜戮rtva agresije mo脜戮e 脛聧initi 脜隆to 脜戮eli. Mora se dr脜戮ati me脛聭unarodnoga prava i ako to ne 脛聧ini, mora se i odgovarati. Ali me脛聭unarodna zajednica, ako je i脜隆ta od nje jo脜隆 ostalo, ne smije izjedna脛聧iti agresora i 脜戮rtvu. A ne smijemo to ni 脛聧initi mi, ovdje, u ovome domu.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Matja脜戮 Nemec (S&D). 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear colleagues, in the light of the daily war crimes committed in Gaza and Lebanon, the ICC arrest warrants are a grim consolation for tens of thousands of victims. I welcome the ICC's decision and call on all states to respect it and to defend international law and institutions. Let us be clear: protecting international law and ensuring accountability for all, regardless of nationality or position, is imperative.

The ICC must fulfil its mandate to uphold justice and prevent future violations. This applies equally to Putin and Netanyahu 芒聙聯 both facing war crimes charges. What's even more striking than the double standards often applied, including in the EU institutions, toward Israel, is the silence of President von der Leyen. Silence is complicity.

In 2012, the European Union received a prize for its contribution to post-war peace in Europe. The question is: does it still deserve it? The actions of its leaders speak for themselves. Not in my name!

Ceasefire now! Sanctions now! Peace now!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lucia Yar (Renew). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, dear colleagues, nothing can justify the brutal Hamas attack on 7脗聽October or the suffering of Israelis still held hostage. While recognising Israel's right to self-defence, it is impossible to justify its response, resulting in over 40脗聽000 civilian deaths in Gaza.

And I want to address this humanitarian catastrophe happening in front of our eyes: this winter, millions of Palestinians face hunger and diseases, lacking homes, food and medical supplies.

The role of UNRWA is vital. It is providing essentials like food, water, healthcare and education, and there is no replacement in the region.脗聽Israel's bills labelling this UN agency as terrorist organisation take effect in two months. Yet, as I said, there is absolutely no viable alternative in the region that can replace this agency's crucial aid.

Let me be clear: Israel has no plan脗聽B for war refugees, and if we all fail to act, history will judge us.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Per Clausen (The Left). 芒聙聯 Hr. formand! Arrestordren fra Den Internationale Domstol var klar. Den israelske regering har systematisk brugt sult som krigsv脙楼ben mod den pal脙娄stinensiske befolkning, forhindret dem adgang til l脙娄gehj脙娄lp og bevidst angrebet civile, og vi kan kun forvente, at Israels bandlysning af UNRWA vil f脙楼 yderligere katastrofale konsekvenser for den humanit脙娄re situation i Pal脙娄stina. Man m脙楼 stille sig selv sp脙赂rgsm脙楼let: hvordan kan EU forsvare sin trov脙娄rdighed, hvis vi tillader Netanyahu at forts脙娄tte dette folkemord uden konsekvenser? EU m脙楼 i det mindste suspendere sin associeringsaftale med Israel, indf脙赂re en v脙楼benembargo og indf脙赂re sanktioner mod Israel. Jeg m脙楼 sige, at jeg skammer mig over EU's hykleri, over, hvordan EU p脙楼 den ene side h脙娄vder at forsvare menneskerettighederne og den internationale lov, og p脙楼 den anden side har forholdt sig passive, mens tusindvis af uskyldige pal脙娄stinensere er blevet dr脙娄bt. Menneskerettigheder og international lov kan ikke gradb脙赂jes. Det er p脙楼 tide, at EU s脙娄tter handling bag ordene og g脙赂r alt, hvad vi kan for at forhindre krigsforbrydelserne mod det pal脙娄stinensiske folk.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Wouter Beke (PPE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, een moorddadige aanslag op 8脗聽oktober in Isra脙芦l. De beelden uit het Midden-Oosten: tienduizenden burgerslachtoffers, waaronder veel kinderen, ook in de Gaza. Onafgebroken Isra脙芦lische bombardementen in Libanon. Om nog maar te zwijgen van de gruweldaden van Hamas en Hezbollah die hele samenlevingen ontwrichten, vaak gesteund door het Iraanse regime.

Er is geen goede kant aan dit verhaal. Er is maar 脙漏脙漏n kant waar we achter moeten staan en dat is die van het internationaal recht. Als vredesproject, par excellence, moet Europa meer doen, zowel in de humanitaire ondersteuning van slachtoffers als rond sancties tegen daders van zulk geweld.

Meer dan ooit moet er een duurzame oplossing gevonden worden in het Midden-Oosten, te beginnen met een tijdelijk en een gedragen vredesbestand. Daarvan is vandaag sprake. Een tijdelijk bestand van zestig dagen kan een eerste stap zijn. Het kan het begin zijn. Waar is Europa om dat mede mogelijk te maken?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Cecilia Strada (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, per la seconda volta ci troviamo a discutere di Gaza e di Medio Oriente ma di nuovo senza un testo da votare, lasciando il Parlamento ancora vergognosamente muto davanti a una catastrofe umanitaria, davanti a un genocidio.

Ogni mese a Strasburgo parliamo di Ucraina, giustamente, e votiamo ogni tipo di sostegno alle vittime e di condanna dell'aggressore. Su questo invece stiamo zitti, nonostante le gravissime violazioni dei diritti umani e del diritto umanitario, l'invasione della sovranit脙聽 territoriale del Libano, i mandati di arresto per la leadership di israeliani e di Hamas, le vergognose leggi anti-UNRWA che aggraveranno ancora di pi脙鹿 la situazione della popolazione palestinese, che 脙篓 gi脙聽 allo stremo.

Io ringrazio davvero di cuore l'alto rappresentante per aver mantenuto la barra dritta sui diritti umani in questi mesi, di fronte a un Consiglio invece incapace di mostrarsi responsabile.

Alla vigilia 芒聙聯 forse 芒聙聯 di un accordo sul cessate il fuoco in Libano, possiamo solo ribadire quel che chiediamo da inizio legislatura: cessate il fuoco a Gaza, che si vieti il commercio con gli insediamenti illegali e l'esportazione di armi verso Israele, che si liberino gli ostaggi e che i criminali di guerra vengano perseguiti senza impunit脙聽, quando sono terroristi e anche quando sono capi di governo.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ingeborg Ter Laak (PPE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, ik vraag u om even stil te staan: 90脗聽% van de bewoners in Gaza hebben geen huis meer. Van alle doden: vier op de tien doden zijn kinderen, de meesten tussen de vijf en negen jaar, en toegang tot humanitaire hulp is nul. Wij mogen niet wegkijken.

Momenteel is de hele regio instabiel, met meerdere fronten waar gevechten plaatsvinden. Daarom zijn humanitaire organisaties zoals UNRWA nodig om humanitaire hulp te verlenen en meer slachtoffers te voorkomen. Wij moeten blijven werken aan een vreedzame en duurzame oplossing voor het complexe conflict. De onderhandelingen over een staakt-het-vuren en de onderhandelingen over het vrijlaten van alle gijzelaars moeten daarom doorgaan. Want alleen samen, met alle partijen, kan er een oplossing worden gevonden voor dit conflict.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Thijs Reuten (S&D). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, de geweldspiraal in het Midden-Oosten draait door. Dag in, dag uit. We gaan het normaal vinden: enorme aantallen slachtoffers verdwijnen naar pagina zes en zeven, terwijl we wegkijken en de vernietiging van Gaza, de Palestijnse bevolking en dorpen/wijken in Libanon op haar beloop laten. De struisvogelpolitiek moet stoppen!

Er is geen rechtvaardiging voor het onvoorstelbare aantal burgerslachtoffers door geweld, maar ook door honger en verdrijving als wapen. De EU moet het internationaal recht strikt naleven. De arrestatiebevelen van het ICC zijn gerechtvaardigd en simpelweg bindend. Netanyahu芒聙聶s barbaarse en disproportionele moordcampagne ondermijnt duurzame vrede en de vrijlating van de gijzelaars, want Netanyahu heeft de gijzelaars vanaf het begin in de steek gelaten.

Geen enkele hoeveelheid humanitaire hulp van de EU verbloemt het g脙陋nante gebrek aan concrete actie richting Isra脙芦l. Schort het associatieverdrag op. Stop de levering van wapens en stop het goedpraten van oorlogsmisdaden. Een mensenleven is een mensenleven. Recht op leven zou niet af moeten hangen van je nationaliteit of afkomst. En de reactie van de EU evenmin.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tom脙隆脜隆 Zdechovsk脙陆 (PPE). 芒聙聯 Pane p脜聶edsedaj脙颅c脙颅, v脙隆脜戮en脙隆 pan脙颅 komisa脜聶ko, d脙隆my a p脙隆nov脙漏, osobn脛聸 p脜聶脙颅li脜隆 脛聧asto nekomentuji rozhodnut脙颅 jak脙漏hokoliv soudu, proto脜戮e moc soudn脙颅 m脙隆 b脙陆t nez脙隆visl脙隆 na moci politick脙漏. Ale odm脙颅t脙隆m jako ob脛聧an, aby bylo vlastn脛聸 vyu脜戮脙颅v脙隆no Mezin脙隆rodn脙颅ho trestn脙颅ho soudu k politick脙陆m hr脙隆m. Zatyka脛聧 na p脜聶edstavitele st脙隆tu, kter脙陆 se br脙隆n脙颅 barbarsk脙陆m 脙潞tok脜炉m proti civilist脜炉m 芒聙聯 d脙隆vat na 脙潞rove脜聢 t脛聸ch, kte脜聶脙颅 tento barbarsk脙陆 脛聧in sp脙隆chali, to jsou v脜炉dci Ham脙隆su 芒聙聯, to je podle m脛聸 naprosto, naprosto neskute脛聧n脙漏. Jak脙陆m pr脙隆vem soudci hodnot脙颅 vlastn脛聸 utrpen脙颅 nevinn脙陆ch civilist脜炉, kte脜聶脙颅 byli zavra脜戮d脛聸ni, uneseni a vyhn脙隆ni ze sv脙陆ch domov脜炉? Jak脙陆m pr脙隆vem soud zleh脛聧uje 脙潞toky terorist脜炉, kter脙漏 jsou zlo脛聧inem proti civilizovan脙漏mu sv脛聸tu? J脙隆 chci 脜戮脙颅t ve spole脛聧nosti, kter脙隆 se dr脜戮脙颅 pr脙隆vn脙颅ch z脙隆sad. A pevn脛聸 v脛聸脜聶脙颅m, 脜戮e Evropsk脙隆 unie 脜聶ekne tomuto absurdn脙颅mu rozhodnut脙颅 jednozna脛聧n脙漏 NE.

Catch-the-eye procedure

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tomislav Sokol (PPE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovani predsjedavaju脛聡i, dana 7. listopada 2023. izvr脜隆en je najsmrtonosniji napad izravno usmjeren na 脜戮idovski narod jo脜隆 od vremena Drugog svjetskog rata. I budimo iskreni, Izrael je mnogo puta ponudio ruku Palestincima. Dopustio je stvaranje Palestinske samouprave devedesetih. Ehud Barak nudio je stvaranje palestinske dr脜戮ave na najve脛聡em dijelu Zapadne obale i cijelom teritoriju Gaze. Ariel 脜聽aron je 2005. naredio povla脛聧enje iz Gaze i ru脜隆enje svih 脜戮idovskih naselja. Od tada goleme koli脛聧ine novca u脜隆le su u Gazu, dovoljno da postane nova 脜聽vicarska. 脜聽to su s time napravili? Gradili tunele i proizvodili rakete kako bi mogli ubijati izraelske civile.

Ukratko, stvari su jasne. Teroristi脛聧ka organizacija Hamas treba prestati postojati. Bilo kakvo relativiziranje toga je sramotno. Zato 脜戮alosti da i sada neki zastupnici u ovome domu poku脜隆avaju izjedna脛聧iti teroriste sa 脜戮rtvama, dok istovremeno diljem Europe islamisti, propalestinski prista脜隆e, 脜隆ire govor mr脜戮nje i podr脜戮avaju teroriste. Zato je va脜戮no nastaviti solidarizirati se sa 脜戮rtvama teroristi脛聧kog napada na Izrael i poduprijeti pravo Izraela na samoobranu. Islamizam predstavlja prijetnju, ne samo Izraelu, ve脛聡 i budu脛聡nosti Europe i zato treba podr脜戮ati Izrael u njegovoj pravednoj borbi protiv ovog zla.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hana Jalloul Muro (S&D). 芒聙聯 Mr President, we have to call for a ceasefire in Lebanon and Gaza, for humanitarian aid to reach families in northern Gaza. We have always condemned the terrorist attacks of Hamas on 7脗聽October. We demand the release of all hostages with no conditions. Families are suffering and Netanyahu is not bringing them back home.

We need an arms embargo that will avoid more deaths. We need the terrorists of Hezbollah to surrender arms and to reinforce the Lebanese Government and Lebanese institutions. We need to support the ICC decisions: we support them for Putin, so we need to support their decisions for Netanyahu, who is responsible for the killings of thousands of civilians.

We need to maintain the international law system. We need peace. People in the Middle East have the right to live. The lives of Jews and Arabs are worth the same. They are brothers and sisters. Ceasefire now!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tiago Moreira de S脙隆 (PfE). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, a pol脙颅tica externa da Uni脙拢o Europeia parece ter esquecido a m脙隆xima do cardeal Richelieu, segundo a qual os Estados n脙拢o t脙陋m imortalidade. A sua salva脙搂脙拢o 脙漏 agora ou nunca.

Os idealistas capturaram os realistas e est脙拢o a destruir as rela脙搂脙碌es internacionais da Europa. A UE ataca e ofende o presidente eleito dos Estados Unidos da Am脙漏rica, um aliado fundamental. Ataca e ofende o primeiro-ministro de um Estado-Membro, a Hungria. Ataca e quer prender o primeiro-ministro de Israel, uma democracia amiga e o principal poder no M脙漏dio Oriente, que foi v脙颅tima de um ataque genocida a 7/10.

O resultado est脙隆 脙聽 vista. Amiga dos seus inimigos e inimiga dos seus amigos, a Uni脙拢o Europeia vai acabar cercada de inimigos e sem amigos para a ajudar a defender-se.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nina Carberry (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, the humanitarian crisis in Gaza has reached unprecedented levels, with countless lives depending on the indispensable work of the United Nations Relief and Works Agency. Suspending funding for UNRWA would be catastrophic, cutting off critical services to millions of Palestinian refugees. No organisation matches UNRWA's comprehensive aid delivery in Gaza, and recent Israeli legislative efforts to ban its operations threaten to violate international law and jeopardise regional stability.

At this critical moment, the international community must uphold humanitarian principles, stand against these violations, and strengthen support for UNRWA's vital mission to save lives and maintain stability in the region.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Maria Grapini (S&D). 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, doamn脛聝 comisar, stima脠聸i colegi, cred c脛聝 avem datoria s脛聝 nu trat脛聝m cu dubl脛聝 m脛聝sur脛聝. Avem dou脛聝 conflicte 脠聶i trebuie s脛聝 avem aceea脠聶i defini脠聸ie a atacatorului 脠聶i a celui care se ap脛聝r脛聝. Trebuie neap脛聝rat s脛聝 fie elibera脠聸i ostaticii. Discut脛聝m din octombrie anul trecut eliberarea de ostatici 脠聶i nu s-au 脙庐ntors ace脠聶ti ostatici. Trebuie, de asemenea, s脛聝 se g脛聝seasc脛聝 c脛聝i diferite 脠聶i multe pentru a aduce ajutoare umanitare. Totu脠聶i, nu putem s脛聝 spunem c脛聝 Israelul este singurul vinovat at脙垄t timp c脙垄t Hamas a atacat, at脙垄t timp c脙垄t Hamas este o organiza脠聸ie terorist脛聝, at脙垄t timp c脙垄t am avut 脠聶i alte atacuri din partea acestor organiza脠聸ii teroriste.

Cred c脛聝 trebuie s脛聝 ne pozi脠聸ion脛聝m corect 脠聶i s脛聝 脙庐ncerc脛聝m s脛聝 g脛聝sim solu脠聸ia pentru pace, pentru a nu mai muri oameni 脠聶i a nu mai r脛聝m脙垄ne copii f脛聝r脛聝 hran脛聝, copii f脛聝r脛聝 mam脛聝 脠聶i tat脛聝. Asta este solu脠聸ia, nu s脛聝 ne pozi脠聸ion脛聝m de-o parte 脠聶i de alta, 脙庐n func脠聸ie de alte principii. Asta este datoria noastr脛聝: s脛聝 trat脛聝m la fel conflictele interna脠聸ionale.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jaume Asens Llodr脙聽 (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤ora comisaria, por orden de la Corte Penal Internacional, Netanyahu ya es un fugado de la justicia que debe ser perseguido, detenido y juzgado.

Pero no olvidemos una cosa importante: al lado de 脙漏l, en el banquillo de acusados, deben estar tambi脙漏n los Estados Unidos y Europa, porque los l脙颅deres occidentales no participan directamente de los cr脙颅menes de Netanyahu, pero los hacen posible con su apoyo financiero, pol脙颅tico y militar.

Es cierto: los cientos de miles de v脙颅ctimas 芒聙聰la mayor脙颅a ni脙卤os, ni脙卤as, mujeres...芒聙聰 apuntan con su dedo acusador a Netanyahu, pero tambi脙漏n a Occidente. Su complicidad no es pasiva, es activa; como decimos los abogados penalistas: es cooperador necesario, tiene las manos manchadas de sangre.

Por eso, estas 脙鲁rdenes de detenci脙鲁n son un aviso a esos l脙颅deres 芒聙聰a muchos de sus compa脙卤eros y compa脙卤eras芒聙聰 que, quiz脙隆 alg脙潞n d脙颅a, reciban alg脙潞n aviso de la Corte Penal Internacional, que ya no es un mero instrumento al servicio de los poderosos, al servicio de Occidente, para perseguir a sus enemigos, a Putin, o los cr脙颅menes cometidos en 脙聛frica o en Asia; ahora est脙隆 ganando independencia.

Por tanto, yo que muchos de ustedes, no dormir脙颅a tranquilo porque, a veces, la justicia act脙潞a, y no siempre sale gratis cometer delitos o apoyarlos.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marc Botenga (The Left). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, quel embarras pour la pr脙漏sidente de la Commission, Ursula脗聽von脗聽der脗聽Leyen, alors qu'aujourd'hui la Cour p脙漏nale internationale dit de Netanyahou脗聽芒聙聯 l'homme qu'elle a rencontr脙漏 脙聽 Tel-Aviv, 脙聽 qui elle a serr脙漏 la main avec un grand sourire, 脙聽 qui elle a promis tout son soutien, l'homme 脙聽 qui l'Europe vend encore des armes, 脙聽 qui on donne des fonds publics europ脙漏ens脗聽芒聙聯 qu'il faut l'arr脙陋ter pour crimes contre l'humanit脙漏, pour crimes de guerre!

L'embarras de Mme脗聽von der Leyen, qui n'arrive pas 脙聽 poster un tweet pour dire: 脗芦Ah ben oui, Poutine, oui oui, je veux qu'il soit arr脙陋t脙漏, mais M.脗聽Netanyahou, mon alli脙漏, mon ami, doit 脙陋tre arr脙陋t脙漏 aussi!脗禄, est palpable partout dans le monde. Et, partout dans le monde, on voit que cette Union europ脙漏enne a deux脗聽poids, deux脗聽mesures et脗聽芒聙聯 m脙陋me si elle pr脙漏tend le contraire脗聽芒聙聯 que les vies palestiniennes ne valent pas, pour cette Union europ脙漏enne, ce que valent les vies ukrainiennes. Que m脙陋me dans ce Parlement, on n'arrive pas 脙聽 voter une r脙漏solution pour condamner les crimes de guerre isra脙漏liens. C'est inacceptable!

Alors aujourd'hui, dans ce monde, l'Europe a un choix 脙聽 faire: est-on du c脙麓t脙漏 de la justice ou du c脙麓t脙漏 du colonialisme, ill脙漏gal, isra脙漏lien? Faisons ce choix et faisons-le maintenant.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Elisa Ferreira, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President and honourable Members, the humanitarian situation in Gaza continues to deteriorate. Too many people are dying. We believe that the European Union cannot stay silent.

Last week, at the Foreign Affairs Council, the High Representative脗聽/脗聽Vice-President proposed to the European Union foreign ministers to suspend the political dialogue with Israel. Member States did not agree with this proposal. We do hope that Member States will be able to agree on sanctioning violent settlers and those that enable them in the West Bank, as well as Hamas supporters.

After the United States election, the European Union's engagement on the two-state solution will be all the more important, including through the Global Alliance for the Implementation of the Two-State Solution. Some 100 states and international organisations participate in the initiative.

The recent meeting in Riyadh, which gathered nearly 100 representatives of states and international organisations, demonstrated the broad interest in this platform. The High Representative脗聽/脗聽Vice-President will chair 芒聙聯 with the Belgian Foreign Minister, incoming Commissioner Hadja Lahbib 芒聙聯 the next meeting of the Global Alliance in Brussels on 28脗聽November, at the invitation of Belgium and the European Union.

Our intention is to use the meeting to push for more structured cooperation on practical contributions to help protect and advance the two-state solution. We should try to reinforce this alliance, as we know well that only a political solution with the deep involvement of Israel and Palestine's neighbours will bring peace and stability in the Middle East.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The debate is closed.

脗聽

13. Corrigenda (Rule 251) (action taken)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 Dear colleagues, pursuant to Rule 251(4), I would like to inform you that there was no request to put to the vote the six corrigenda announced in plenary yesterday at the opening of the sitting. The corrigenda are therefore deemed to have been approved.

脗聽

14. Outcome of COP 29 and challenges for international climate policy (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽President. 芒聙聯 The next item is the debate on the Commission statement on the outcome of COP29 and challenges for international climate policy (2024/2948(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Wopke Hoekstra, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Dear Mr脗聽President, honourable Members, and in particular those Members who have been with me at least part of the last week, thank you very much for having the opportunity to reconvene and there are a couple of messages that I'm sure many of you have already incorporated yourselves, but I do feel are good to share.

First of all, we have all seen and all of us who were there have also sensed that this was truly very difficult. It was truly very difficult to come to an agreement. I have to tell you, at many moments, particularly in the last evening, we were on the on the verge of collapse. I do feel at the same time that it is of tremendous importance that we did manage to strike a deal, a deal that is in many ways less than ideal, but still is very important for the world.

The first reason that that is the case is that in the end, we did manage to build bridges to our friends and allies and partners from all over the world, particularly those most in need. Secondly, because if you look at the world today, geopolitics, the geopolitical situation is already tremendously complex and a deal in it has value in itself, has inherent value in itself because it shows that multilateralism is still possible.

The other reason, of course, why it is so important that we did manage to broker this deal in the end, and again, after very intensive conversations with people from all over the globe, is that we managed to reach an agreement on the funding and the USD脗聽300脗聽billion where it is crystal clear that that the European Union and other developed countries will continue to lead, where many of the multilateral development banks are actually making a significant step up, but where we also manage to bring others to the table.

So it is not just a tripling, but it is also that we have managed to enlarge the contributor base and that is to many here in Europe, whether they are ambitious or even very ambitious on climate, this is important because it helps to bring more money to the table and it helps in the domain of of fairness.

Where I am, frankly speaking, disappointed, even though that was not the main focus of the COP29, is the domain of mitigation itself. The good news is that, against the odds, we did manage to safeguard the agreements that we put on the table last year in Dubai, so the UAE Consensus is safeguarded. But I'm also not going to hide the fact from this Parliament that was not easy and that of course, ideally, we would have made a step up because that is one of the reasons why we came to the COP29 this time. So, a lot of work ahead of us, particularly in the domain of mitigation, but also in the domain of adaptation and everything that is related to it in the 12 months that we have ahead of us.

Another part of good news, and I wanted to to highlight that because it is something that you might not have seen in the various newspaper articles, but that was part of the core of what we were striving for, is the agreement on carbon markets. It got a lot less attention, but it is of pivotal importance from a content perspective, but also because it builds a bridge between those in the Global North and the Global South in terms of ambition, so I think that is also a very clear win, even though, as I said, it is less prominently featured than the tripling of the amounts that we put to the table.

Dear Mr脗聽President, last but not least 芒聙聯 and it is of course up to Parliament to judge what they make of the result and what they like or potentially dislike about it 芒聙聯 I do want to express my thanks to the many many actions and conversations ministers from the whole European Union have been involved in, but also Members of this Parliament who played a very active role and with whom I had the pleasure to meet a couple of times, so thank you very much for that.

This was truly a team effort. It was truly a European effort. The notion that we that we managed to sing from the same hymn sheet all the time was actually of vital importance in making sure we would land this truly complex deal.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽L脙颅dia Pereira, em nome do Grupo PPE. 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, 93脗聽% das pessoas consideram as altera脙搂脙碌es clim脙隆ticas um problema s脙漏rio, com 78脗聽% a classific脙隆-lo como muito s脙漏rio. Estes dados do Eurobar脙鲁metro revelam que, para as pessoas, para os europeus, as altera脙搂脙碌es clim脙隆ticas s脙拢o o problema mais grave que o mundo enfrenta atualmente.

Temos a obriga脙搂脙拢o de ouvir as pessoas, de responder 脙聽s suas preocupa脙搂脙碌es. Foi com esta consci脙陋ncia que, na COP29, a delega脙搂脙拢o do Parlamento Europeu defendeu a nossa posi脙搂脙拢o adotada nesta Casa com mensagens claras. Temos de ser mais ambiciosos nos objetivos, mais eficazes nas solu脙搂脙碌es, mais solid脙隆rios com os pa脙颅ses em desenvolvimento e aqueles que mais sofrem com as altera脙搂脙碌es clim脙隆ticas.

O nosso compromisso foi fundamental para os resultados da COP29. Conseguimos um acordo para triplicar o financiamento 脙聽s economias em desenvolvimento e mais transpar脙陋ncia, evitando a corrup脙搂脙拢o. Avan脙搂脙隆mos nos mercados de carbono. Quem mais polui tem de pagar.

Esta COP29 ficou tamb脙漏m marcada, infelizmente, por pa脙颅ses como a Ar脙隆bia Saudita, que estavam interessados em n脙拢o serem mais ambiciosos, mas em vez disso, porem em causa o abandono gradual do uso dos combust脙颅veis f脙鲁sseis. E tamb脙漏m pela relut脙垄ncia de pa脙颅ses como a China em serem doadores permanentes no financiamento clim脙隆tico.

Apesar destes desafios, apesar do contexto geopol脙颅tico, a Europa liderou. E eu gostava de dar uma palavra de reconhecimento pelo trabalho do comiss脙隆rio Wopke Hoekstra, que conseguiu nunca desistir e continuar a liderar em mat脙漏ria de ambiente.

N脙拢o podemos falhar. A ci脙陋ncia 脙漏 clara e a vontade dos cidad脙拢os 脙漏 evidente. E juntos, de facto, fazemos a diferen脙搂a. E, portanto, uma palavra tamb脙漏m 脙聽 Team Europe, que foi bem-sucedida em mais uma COP.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Mohammed Chahim, namens de S&D-Fractie. 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, laat ik eens positief beginnen. We hebben onze ambities gelukkig niet teruggedraaid, maar stilstand is, wat mij betreft, ook achteruitgang. Het is niet gelukt om tijdens deze klimaattop COP29 het hoofdstuk klimaatfinanciering af te sluiten, wat echt nodig is om van andere landen meer ambitie te vragen. Voor eilandstaten als de Malediven en Samoa is klimaatverandering elk jaar een stukje meer verdwijnen in de oceaan.

Daarom is die 1脗聽300脗聽miljard nodig. Waar we in 2022 pas voldeden aan de 100脗聽miljard die we in 2015 beloofden, beloven we nu in 2035 300脗聽miljard, wat vooral bestaat uit leningen. Dus ik snap de teleurstelling, kritiek en het gebrek aan vertrouwen bij veel landen. Met dit tempo hebben we meer dan honderd jaar nodig om die 1脗聽300脗聽miljard te bereiken.

De vraag is hoeveel van die eilandstaten er dan nog bestaan. De dialoog rond klimaatverandering blijft gelukkig, maar hebben we een goede uitgangspositie voor volgend jaar? De vraag stellen is hem beantwoorden. E脙漏n ding staat vast: we hebben veel werk te verzetten, niet alleen voor de financiering voor klimaat, maar ook om de ambities op te trekken. Want klimaatactie wacht niet op het juiste podium.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Isabella Tovaglieri, a nome del gruppo PfE. 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, alla conferenza sul clima appena terminata a Baku abbiamo assistito ad un surreale gioco del Monopoli dell'ambiente, perch脙漏 siamo passati dai 1.300 miliardi inizialmente richiesti per la transizione ambientale, ad accordarsi a tarallucci e vino, per soli 300 miliardi, che ovviamente saranno i cittadini europei a pagare.

脙聢 stata l'ennesima conferenza a consentire di fatto al pi脙鹿 grande inquinatore del mondo, la Cina, di continuare ad inquinare senza limiti e senza che gli si chieda nemmeno di mettere un euro per ci脙虏 che fa, perch脙漏 tanto il conto della transizione lo paga l'Europa.

Sempre l'Europa che con i suoi soldi finanzia le auto elettriche cinesi prodotte in fabbriche ancora alimentate a carbone. Per cui, alla fine, siamo solo l'unico continente a pagare per la transizione: con la distruzione dell'industria, con la perdita dei posti di lavori, ma in pi脙鹿 ci tocca ancora una volta dare i soldi per finanziare i grandi inquinatori, per cui 脙篓 ora che l'Unione europea difenda il nostro sviluppo e pretenda che siano i veri inquinatori, finalmente, a pagare il conto.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Anna Zalewska, w imieniu grupy ECR. 芒聙聯 Panie Przewodnicz脛聟cy! Panie Komisarzu! Kole脜录anki i Koledzy! Kilka s脜聜脙鲁w prawdy o tym, co dzia脜聜o si脛聶 na szczycie klimatycznym.

Po pierwsze, porozumienie po zako脜聞czeniu COP-u wym脛聶czone, powierzchowne i 脜录yczeniowe. Chocia脜录by owe 300 miliard脙鲁w co roku, w脜聜a脜聸ciwie bez konkretnych wskaza脜聞 i konkretnych kwot, przy bardzo ma脜聜ym zaanga脜录owaniu Stan脙鲁w Zjednoczonych, kt脙鲁re dostarcza脜聜y pieni脛聟dze. Mamy zapowied脜潞, 脜录e Stany Zjednoczone w og脙鲁le wyjd脛聟 z porozumienia paryskiego. To po pierwsze.

Po drugie, ogromne emocje zwi脛聟zane z tymi, kt脙鲁rzy s脛聟 rozwini脛聶ci i bogaci, a nie chc脛聟 by脛聡 na li脜聸cie inaczej nazwani. W zwi脛聟zku z tym s脛聟 zwolnieni z tych脜录e op脜聜at, jak na przyk脜聜ad Chi脜聞czycy. Po drugie, absolutna negacja tych ambitnych plan脙鲁w Unii Europejskiej. Wi脛聶kszo脜聸脛聡 kraj脙鲁w m脙鲁wi脜聜a po prostu: mo脜录ecie sobie realizowa脛聡 swoje ambicje, ale my nie pozwolimy na to, 脜录eby niszczy脛聡 nasze gospodarki. Tak naprawd脛聶 w kuluarach ka脜录dy chodzi脜聜 z raportem Draghiego i pokazywa脜聜, do czego doprowadzi脜聜a zielona polityka i ambicje niemo脜录liwe do realizacji. Wreszcie po raz pierwszy wyra脜潞nie nie ma mowy o wycofywaniu si脛聶 z paliw kopalnych. Czas zweryfikowa脛聡 Zielony 脜聛ad Unii Europejskiej, bo prowadzi on do zapa脜聸ci gospodarczej i do katastrofy finansowej Europejczyk脙鲁w.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sigrid Friis, for Renew-Gruppen. 芒聙聯 Hr. formand! Det var en interessant tur til Baku i sidste uge p脙楼 COP 29. Omgivet af oliedampe og lugten af gas kunne man alligevel blive enige om tre hundrede milliarder dollars 脙楼rligt i klimabistand til verdens mest udsatte lande, men det er tusind milliarder mindre, end der er brug for, for at kunne investere i de n脙赂dvendige klimatiltag. Og ja, en aftale er bedre end ingenting, is脙娄r n脙楼r Trump truer med at tr脙娄kke USA ud af Parisaftalen. Men COP 29 er stadigv脙娄k en skuffelse. Saudi-Arabien og resten af olieproducenterne er kommet ud som sejrherrer, og de kan grine hele vejen til banken. Det samme kan Kina. Et land, som kan sende rumraketter til m脙楼nen, men som stadig anses som et udviklingsland og ikke er forpligtet til at betale deres fair del. Og i mods脙娄tning til sidste 脙楼r p脙楼 COP 28 kunne verdens lande heller ikke blive enige om, at vi skal nedbringe brugen af fossile br脙娄ndsler. Men vi skal jo ikke g脙楼 i bakgear, n脙楼r det kommer til at bek脙娄mpe klimaforandringerne. Vi skal s脙娄tte speederen i bund. S脙楼 nu haster det. Hvis det globale klimalederskab s脙楼 skal EU alts脙楼 indsende et ambiti脙赂st klimam脙楼l f脙赂r 2035, f脙赂r COP 30, inden fristen i februar. Vil du som klimakommiss脙娄r garantere det og sikre, at klimam脙楼let stemmer overens med det lovede 2040-m脙楼l p脙楼 90 %?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Michael Bloss, im Namen der Verts/ALE-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, sehr geehrte Damen und Herren! Das Ergebnis dieser Klimakonferenz ist zu mickrig, um der Klimakrise gerecht zu werden. Daraus ziehe ich drei Lehren: Erstens: Fossile Autokratien d脙录rfen nie mehr Gastgeber einer Klimakonferenz sein. Aserbaidschan hat seine fossile Agenda vorangetrieben, w脙陇hrend unser Planet brennt. Der Brandstifter soll das Feuer l脙露schen. Das geht nicht.

Zweitens: Stimmen aus Bangladesch, den Malediven und den Philippinen haben es auf den Punkt gebracht. Jeder verlorene Tag kostet Leben. Die Klimakrise ist 脙录berall angekommen. Sie wartet nicht, sie verhandelt nicht, sie versch脙陇rft sich.

Und drittens: Europa ist seiner globalen F脙录hrungsrolle noch nicht gewachsen. Die Abwesenheit von Scholz, Macron und von der Leyen zu Beginn war fatal. Ich danke Herrn Hoekstra f脙录r seinen Einsatz. Aber Europa muss nach vorne gehen und sein neues Klimaziel, seinen NDC schon im Januar vorlegen. Europa tr脙陇gt nicht nur Verantwortung, Europa hat die Pflicht, Klimaschutz anzuf脙录hren.

脗聽脗聽Anja Arndt, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident! Auf der Klimakonferenz in Baku ging es wieder hoch her. Es soll noch mehr Geld flie脙聼en f脙录r den aussichtslosen Kampf gegen den Klimawandel, und zwar von den Industriestaaten zu den Entwicklungsl脙陇ndern. Das Geld soll f脙录r klimafreundliche gr脙录ne Technologien verwendet werden. Wer hat sich das eigentlich ausgedacht? Die Anbieter gr脙录ner Technologien? Vertreter von Entwicklungsl脙陇ndern nannten die neu vereinbarte Summe, die recht hoch ist脗聽芒聙聯 sie ist n脙陇mlich in der H脙露he von 300脗聽000 Millionen Dollar pro Jahr angesetzt脗聽芒聙聯, sie nannten sie einfach mal abgr脙录ndig klein und d脙录rftig. Sie sei eine Beleidigung und ein Witz.

Bisher flossen schon jedes Jahr 100脗聽000 Millionen Dollar von den Industriestaaten zu den Entwicklungsl脙陇ndern. Dieses Geld wird脗聽芒聙聯 und das ist nun der echte Witz des Tages脗聽芒聙聯 auch an Indien, China, die reichen Golfstaaten und S脙录dkorea gezahlt. Warum? Weil sie nach der 30脗聽Jahre alten UNO芒聙聭Einstufung als Entwicklungsl脙陇nder eingestuft werden. Es wird aber noch absurder: Durch die EU芒聙聭Klimapolitik werden die eigenen Industriestaaten im Eiltempo deindustrialisiert, zahlungsunf脙陇hig und damit selbst zu Entwicklungsl脙陇ndern gemacht. Das arme Portugal und Griechenland und schon in K脙录rze das arme Deutschland sollen f脙录r das reiche China und Saudi芒聙聭Arabien zahlen. Wir ruinieren uns selbst mit unserer Klimapolitik. Das ist klimapolitischer Extremismus. Wir von der AfD und dem ESN sagen: Schluss damit!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Peter Liese (PPE). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Der Klimawandel bleibt eine der gr脙露脙聼ten Herausforderungen unserer Politikergeneration. Und bei aller berechtigten Kritik an dem internationalen Prozess 芒聙聯 ich habe noch niemanden getroffen, der eine bessere Methode vorgeschlagen hat. Einfach nur zu sagen, ja, man m脙录sste es irgendwie anders machen, das hilft nicht weiter.

Sondern 芒聙聯 so schwierig dieser Prozess auch ist 芒聙聯 wir m脙录ssen mit ihm arbeiten. Aber ich kann es auch nicht verhehlen, dass wir nur teilweise erfolgreich waren: Dass China und andere sich nicht gesetzlich, nicht rechtlich verpflichtet haben 芒聙聯 verbindlich 芒聙聯, dass sie beitragen zur Finanzierung des Klimaschutzes, bleibt ein Problem. Und die Kommission ist aufgerufen, wir alle sind aufgerufen, an diesem Thema weiterhin zu arbeiten.

Entscheidend ist, dass wir auf dem Weg bis n脙陇chstes Jahr gute Ziele f脙录r das Jahr 2035 erreichen. Und hier haben wir, glaube ich, noch eine Herausforderung. Wir haben eine Position der Kommission f脙录r 90脗聽% f脙录r 2040. Ich bin noch nicht davon 脙录berzeugt, dass das ein Ziel ist, das wir auch wirklich schaffen k脙露nnen. Daran m脙录ssen wir hart arbeiten, die Wege aufzuzeigen, wie es dann am Ende wirklich funktioniert. Aber international haben jetzt erste Staaten schon ein Ziel f脙录r 2035 vorgelegt. Wenige 芒聙聯 alles noch nicht so dramatisch 芒聙聯, aber die Europ脙陇ische Union braucht eben auch ein Ziel f脙录r 2035. Und ich glaube nicht, dass man da einfach die Mittellinie nehmen kann zwischen 55脗聽% und 90脗聽%, sondern da bedarf es noch einmal besonderer Anstrengungen脗聽芒聙聯 auch ein impact assessment, Zusammenarbeit mit der Wirtschaft脗聽芒聙聯, da wirklich ein gutes Ziel vorzulegen. Und ich bitte die Kommission, an diesem Thema hart zu arbeiten.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Annalisa Corrado (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, 脙篓 vero, la conferenza sul clima di Baku si 脙篓 conclusa con risultati fortemente inadeguati alla dimensione della crisi in atto, che sempre pi脙鹿 spesso impatta drammaticamente sulle nostre comunit脙聽.

Ma voglio dirlo, avendo avuto l'onore di prendervi parte con la delegazione del Parlamento: abbiamo scongiurato come Unione europea, e con pochissimi altri alleati, che la conferenza fallisse. E per questo voglio ringraziare il commissario Hoekstra.

L'obiettivo di finanza climatica al 2035, con la脗聽roadmap per arrivare a mobilitare 1.300 miliardi, tiene aperto un passaggio strettissimo che ci impegna a lavorare, in vista della COP30 di Belem, con maggiore determinazione su mitigazione, adattamento e fondi per le perdite ai danni.

Porto con me da Baku un'immagine in particolare: l'incontro con il dissidente azero Gubad Ibadoghlu, finalista del nostro Premio Sacharov, in regime di detenzione anche per aver denunciato i legami opachi tra l'industria di petrolio e gas del suo Paese e la Russia.

Lavorare per la transizione energetica significa anche questo: liberarci dalle dipendenze fossili che non ci consentono di alzare la voce quanto necessario per il rispetto dei diritti umani.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ond脜聶ej Knotek (PfE). 芒聙聯 Pane p脜聶edsedaj脙颅c脙颅, v脙隆脜戮n脛聸 tady oslavujete, 脜戮e zem脛聸, kter脙漏 sni脜戮uj脙颅 emise sklen脙颅kov脙陆ch plyn脜炉, v脛聧etn脛聸 t脛聸ch evropsk脙陆ch, budou platit 300 miliard dolar脜炉 ro脛聧n脛聸 zem脙颅m, kter脙漏 naopak emise v posledn脙颅ch letech v脙陆razn脛聸 zv脙陆脜隆ily, s t脙颅m, 脜戮e mo脜戮n脙隆 nakonec p脜聶estanou? Ukazujete t脙颅m, 脜戮e tyto konference a mezin脙隆rodn脙颅 panely pro klima ji脜戮 d脙隆vno neslou脜戮脙颅 k zaji脜隆t脛聸n脙颅 udr脜戮iteln脙漏 klimatick脙漏 politiky. Stal se z nich n脙隆stroj na p脜聶erozd脛聸len脙颅 bohatstv脙颅 a uspokojen脙颅 klimatick脙陆ch alarmist脜炉. V脙陆sledkem je okraden脙颅 na脜隆ich ob脛聧an脜炉 nejenom o jejich pen脙颅ze, ale i o jejich budoucnost. A vy tomu je脜隆t脛聸 tlesk脙隆te? Klimatu nepom脜炉脜戮ete a Evropu hospod脙隆脜聶sky zahub脙颅te. Va脜隆e ideologick脙隆, zelen脙隆, progresivn脙颅 a neudr脜戮iteln脙隆 politika je cestou do z脙隆huby. Tak ji pros脙颅m skon脛聧ete.

(脜聵e脛聧n脙颅k souhlasil s t脙颅m, 脜戮e odpov脙颅 na vystoupen脙颅 na z脙隆klad脛聸 zvednut脙颅 modr脙漏 karty)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Emma Wiesner (Renew), blue-card question. 芒聙聯 Ond脜聶ej, the 3.1 脗掳C scenario of climate change 芒聙聯 that is what will destroy Europe's economy. Is that really your message to the small island states, the developing countries that have not emitted historically but are now suffering from climate change? Their islands are disappearing, and your message is 'we are destroying our economy'? We built our economy based on fossil fuels. They have none, and we are destroying their possibilities of living. That is what the USD脗聽3脗聽billion is all about, taking responsibility for what we have caused them.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ond脜聶ej Knotek (PfE), blue-card answer. 芒聙聯 (Start of speech off mic) ... is that your policies, policies of climate activists, as you are, are not leading to saving the climate. You are lying to people about that if they will be ambitious, that there will be no floods and so on and so on. But that's not true. You have no data, you have no science base, you have no impact assessments: you are just lying. So you are the root cause.

脗聽脗聽Ond脜聶ej Krut脙颅lek (ECR). 芒聙聯 Pane p脜聶edsedaj脙颅c脙颅, v脙隆脜戮en脙陆 pane komisa脜聶i, j脙隆 se pokus脙颅m tu debatu tro脜隆i脛聧ku zklidnit. Kdy脜戮 jsme p脜聶ed dv脛聸ma t脙陆dny diskutovali mand脙隆t na COP 29, tak j脙隆 jsem vyjad脜聶oval ohledn脛聸 z脙隆sadn脙颅ho posunu skeptickou pozici. Konference prob脛聸hla, jsem r脙隆d, 脜戮e jsem mohl b脙陆t sou脛聧脙隆st脙颅 parlamentn脙颅 delegace, nicm脙漏n脛聸 ohledn脛聸 dohody, kter脙隆 vznikla stran financov脙隆n脙颅, nem脜炉脜戮e b脙陆t podle m脙漏ho soudu spokojen v脜炉bec nikdo. Uk脙隆zalo se, 脜戮e hlas Evropsk脙漏 unie nen脙颅 nejsiln脛聸j脜隆脙颅. Mus脙颅me b脙陆t realist脙漏 a mus脙颅me se pou脛聧it z toho, co se stalo. S ostatn脙颅mi st脙隆ty nem脜炉脜戮eme jednat z pozice s脙颅ly a nad脜聶azenosti, ale kreativn脛聸. Jenom tak m脜炉脜戮eme dos脙隆hnout toho, aby 脙潞脛聧et neplatili jenom tradi脛聧n脙颅 pl脙隆tci v脛聧etn脛聸 n脙隆s, ale i dal脜隆脙颅 ekonomicky vysp脛聸l脙漏 zem脛聸, jako je t脜聶eba 脛聦脙颅na. A na脜隆im partner脜炉m mus脙颅me neust脙隆le p脜聶ipom脙颅nat, k 脛聧emu se zav脙隆zali. Nesm脙颅me z脜炉stat izolovan脙陆m ostrovem, kter脙陆 jako jedin脙陆 vzorn脛聸 pln脙颅 svoje dekarboniza脛聧n脙颅 芒聙聯 a chci dodat ambici脙鲁zn脙颅 dekarboniza脛聧n脙颅 芒聙聯 c脙颅le bez ohledu na z脙隆va脜戮n脙漏 ekonomick脙漏 a spole脛聧ensk脙漏 dopady.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Emma Wiesner (Renew). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, so, how to summarise this COP29? It's a mixed bag of feelings: from disappointment to embarrassment to determination and a relief that there is a deal. But we have to ask ourselves, what will future generations remember from Baku?

And just as many times before, we didn't do enough, we didn't do it in time, and we didn't do it in place. We have to face it. The USD脗聽300脗聽billion 芒聙聯 well, looking at inflation until 2035, it's barely an increase of ambitions.

We have to learn from this. The EU, in this Parliament, we can do more, we can do better, we can do it earlier and we can do it sooner. And this will have to be done if we are going to change and get away from the 3.1脗聽脗掳C hell scenario of climate change. Next year in Brazil, we have to do better!

I'm not satisfied with the efforts of this Union. I am not satisfied by the efforts of this Parliament, nor with the global commitment. But I am determined that we can improve, simply because we have to.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lena Schilling (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear colleagues, I would love to start with talking about climate finance, but I need to start with a message I received a lot in the last few days from Azerbaijan:

'Shame on you and glory to Azerbaijan! COP29 turned out to be a fantastic success story, and you have failed'.

This is one of dozens of messages I have received from Azerbaijani numbers since COP. Fossil fuel regimes try to sabotage phasing out fossil fuels, even as they witness the destruction and suffering it causes. Fossil fuel dictators host climate conferences, put gas deals over fighting the climate crisis and call it a success story.脗聽Dear President Aliyev, no, COP was not a success story. You silenced activists, journalists and civil society. You imprisoned 300 individuals who dared to stand against you. You tried to use COP29 as a stage to greenwash your regime: you failed.

We need climate action above fossil interests. We can't continue making oil and gas deals with these autocratic regimes. And that is what we need to do right now.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Li Andersson (The Left). 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, parempi v脙陇h脙陇n kun ei mit脙陇脙陇n. N脙陇in ilmastokokousten lopputulosta on kuvattu, mutta niin ei ole. Meill脙陇 ei ole en脙陇脙陇 aikaa ajatella, ett脙陇 "v脙陇h脙陇n" on tyydytt脙陇v脙陇 ratkaisu ilmastonmuutoksen vastaisessa taistelussa. Meill脙陇 ei ole en脙陇脙陇 aikaa yhteenk脙陇脙陇n v脙陇likokoukseen tai k脙陇denl脙陇mp脙露iseen kompromissiin, kun kyse on planeetan elinkelpoisuudesta.

Kokouksen p脙陇脙陇aiheena oli rahoitus. Tutkijoiden mukaan ulkoisen rahoituksen tarve kehittyville maille on t脙陇ll脙陇 hetkell脙陇 vajaa tuhat脗聽miljardia vuodessa. Silti kokouksessa p脙陇脙陇tettiin vain 286 miljardin vuosittaisesta rahoituksesta ja t脙陇st脙陇kin osan pit脙陇isi tulla markkinoilta.

Markkinaehtoisen rahoituksen ongelma on, ett脙陇 monet k脙露yhemm脙陇t ja ilmastonmuutoksen vaikutuksille alttiit maat ovat jo raskaasti velkaantuneita ja niiden markkinoilta saaman rahoituksen hinta on moninkertainen vauraampiin maihin verrattuna. Samaan aikaan vauraat maat k脙陇ytt脙陇v脙陇t 6,6 biljoonaa euroa fossiilisen energian tukiin. T脙陇m脙陇 on t脙陇ysin kest脙陇m脙陇t脙露nt脙陇.

脗聽脗聽Milan Mazurek (NI). 芒聙聯 V脙隆脜戮en脙陆 p脙隆n predsedaj脙潞ci, chcete zachr脙隆ni脜楼 plan脙漏tu? Tak v prvom rade presta脜聢te s va脜隆imi zelen脙陆mi nezmyslami, ktor脙漏 ni脛聧ia 脜戮ivot 脛戮udom v 脛聧lensk脙陆ch 脜隆t脙隆toch Eur脙鲁pskej 脙潞nie. Vy sn脙隆脛聫 nevid脙颅te, ko脛戮ko dnes v Eur脙鲁pe stoja nehnute脛戮nosti? Ko脛戮ko musia 脛戮udia plati脜楼 za to, aby si mohli k脙潞pi脜楼 dom alebo byt, pr脙颅padne si t脙潞to nehnute脛戮nos脜楼 prenaja脜楼? Vy sn脙隆脛聫 nevid脙颅te, 脜戮e mlad脙颅 脛戮udia si u脜戮 nem脙麓脜戮u v Eur脙鲁pe zaklada脜楼 rodiny, preto脜戮e je v脜隆etko extr脙漏mne drah脙漏? A 脛聧o je va脜隆a odpove脛聫? Zavediete 脛聫al脜隆ie opatrenia? Zavediete 脛聫al脜隆ie n脙隆klady? Poviete im, 脜戮e bud脙潞 ma脜楼 emisn脙漏 povolenky na vykurovanie vo vlastn脙陆ch dom脙隆cnostiach? Poviete im, 脜戮e bud脙潞 plati脜楼 脜隆peci脙隆lnu da脜聢 z benz脙颅nu, 脜隆peci脙隆lnu da脜聢 z plynu, aby e脜隆te aj energie, ktor脙漏 s脙潞 neuverite脛戮ne drah脙漏, boli e脜隆te drah脜隆ie rok za rokom? Vy sn脙隆脛聫 nevid脙颅te, kam sa Eur脙鲁pa prepad脙隆? Nevid脙颅te, 脜戮e chudobnieme? Nevid脙颅te, 脜戮e cel脙陆 svet n脙隆s predbehol? Unik脙隆 n脙隆m a vy poviete, 脜戮e m脙隆me plati脜楼 脛聫al脜隆脙颅ch 300 mili脙隆rd na va脜隆e zelen脙漏 nezmysly, ktor脙漏 doteraz v脜戮dy a z脙隆konite zlyhali? Plan脙漏ta potrebuje zachr脙隆ni脜楼 od va脜隆ich hl脙潞pych n脙隆padov.

脗聽脗聽Ingeborg Ter Laak (PPE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, je bent de puinhoop nog aan het opruimen en daar komt de volgende overstroming alweer aan. Het is nu letterlijk dweilen met de kraan open voor eilanden in de Stille Oceaan. Dit gebeurt nu daar, maar het staat Europa ook te wachten. De boodschap op de klimaattop was duidelijk: geef meer geld!

De meest kwetsbare mensen worden het hardst geraakt. Het is aan ons om te zorgen dat niemand achterblijft. De kosten van nietsdoen zijn vele malen groter. Gelukkig is er uiteindelijk een akkoord gekomen, maar dit is niet het punt waarop wij kunnen stoppen. Volgend jaar op de klimaattop in Brazili脙芦 zullen wij gezamenlijk doelen voor 2035 vaststellen. Deze doelen zijn belangrijk, maar nog veel belangrijker is het dat wij en andere landen deze doelen gaan naleven.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tsvetelina Penkova (S&D). 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner, colleagues, as part of the delegation at COP29 in Baku last week, I can assure you that the participation of the European Parliament there is key and crucial. Apart from the call for greater financing for the developing countries, which was one of the requests from the Global South, there was an additional one: a clear and foreseeable regulatory framework.

The agreed commitment for USD脗聽300脗聽billion of investment is a small step. However, in the upcoming years, we will not only be facing the lack of financing; we will also need clear and predictable investments from the developing countries that are already experiencing impressive economic growth.

What I saw on the ground during our meetings with the various national delegations is the same desire for predictability that I see here in Europe, in the Just Transition regions. People want to be certain that the rules of the game will not change midway. If Europe wants to remain at the forefront of climate diplomacy, we must first make sure that we solve our problems domestically and lead by example.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jorge Buxad脙漏 Villalba (PfE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, he le脙颅do el discurso de cierre de la CP29 y estas son mis conclusiones.

Cuando un bur脙鲁crata dice una barbaridad, como que triplicar la financiaci脙鲁n en pol脙颅ticas verdes es una p脙鲁liza de seguros para la humanidad, pero que hay que pagar la prima 脙颅ntegramente y cuanto antes, acaba de desvelar que su 脙潞nico inter脙漏s es vaciar los bolsillos de los europeos para llen脙隆rselos 脙漏l. Su discurso es el de otro soci脙鲁pata que se cree con derecho a planificar nuestra sociedad y nuestra econom脙颅a. Poner como ejemplo al Reino Unido y Brasil 芒聙聰dos Gobiernos izquierdistas que se han pasado el verano metiendo en la c脙隆rcel a disidentes pol脙颅ticos y violando las libertades civiles芒聙聰 demuestra cu脙隆l es su clase de referentes.

脗驴Qu脙漏 creen que pensaron los vecinos de Valencia cuando vieron a Pedro S脙隆nchez subirse al Falcon y dirigirse a la Conferencia de las Naciones Unidas sobre el Cambio Clim脙隆tico para esconderse de los espa脙卤oles? 脗驴Qu脙漏 creen que piensan los que han padecido en carne propia las consecuencias letales de la histeria clim脙隆tica a la que quieren triplicar su locura? Lo que hubiera salvado vidas es triplicar la inversi脙鲁n en limpiar los cauces de los r脙颅os y ejecutar presas y canalizaciones.

Miren, el Acuerdo de Par脙颅s se ha revelado como una soga al cuello de nuestra libertad y nuestra prosperidad.

(El orador acepta responder a varias preguntas formuladas con arreglo al procedimiento de la 脗芦tarjeta azul脗禄)

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽L脙颅dia Pereira (PPE), Pergunta segundo o procedimento 脗芦cart脙拢o azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 Senhor Deputado, depois do que aconteceu recentemente com as cheias em Val脙陋ncia, com os inc脙陋ndios em Portugal no in脙颅cio de setembro, com as cheias no Leste Europeu, o senhor acredita ou n脙拢o acredita nas altera脙搂脙碌es clim脙隆ticas? 脙聣 que, se acredita, se calhar conv脙漏m que a Uni脙拢o Europeia fa脙搂a mais do que tem feito. E isso tem sido poss脙颅vel 脙聽 custa do di脙隆logo com outros pa脙颅ses e com os pa脙颅ses mais vulner脙隆veis. Portanto, a pergunta que lhe fa脙搂o 脙漏 muito simples: acredita ou n脙拢o acredita nas altera脙搂脙碌es clim脙隆ticas?

脗聽脗聽Jorge Buxad脙漏 Villalba (PfE), respuesta de 脗芦tarjeta azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 Su pregunta es sencilla y mi respuesta tambi脙漏n es sencilla: creo, claramente, en la capacidad del ser humano para adaptarse, innovar, trabajar y, sobre todo, buscar soluciones a los problemas. El hombre ha tenido siempre que enfrentarse a la naturaleza, dominarla.

Lo que s脙颅 le puedo decir es que, respecto a Valencia, la responsabilidad exclusiva es de Teresa Ribera, a la que ustedes van a nombrar vicepresidente de la Comisi脙鲁n Europea, que dej脙鲁 de ejecutar las canalizaciones, las presas y la limpieza de los cauces, provocando la muerte de m脙隆s de 230 espa脙卤oles. De eso no tengo ninguna duda.

脗聽脗聽Jorge Buxad脙漏 Villalba (PfE), respuesta de 脗芦tarjeta azul脗禄. 芒聙聯 Pues se lo voy a volver a repetir. Creo en la capacidad del hombre y creo en Dios, evidentemente, no creo en teor脙颅as pol脙颅ticas o en teor脙颅as ideol脙鲁gicas. Por supuesto, creo que el hombre, desde que lleg脙鲁 a este planeta, se ha adaptado, ha ido innovando, ha ido descubriendo, inventando. Tenemos ciudades y pa脙颅ses que est脙隆n por debajo del nivel del mar. Hemos sabido canalizar r脙颅os. Gracias a las obras que se hicieron en Espa脙卤a en el a脙卤o 1957, la ciudad, la capital de Valencia, no ha sido arrasada por las inundaciones. No, si con tanta tarjeta azul me tendr脙隆 que dejar hablar. Esa es la realidad. Creo en esto, en la capacidad del ser humano de adaptarse y mejorar.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rasmus Nordqvist (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, this year we have witnessed devastating floods in Central Europe, wildfires in Greece and most recently, human catastrophe in Spain 芒聙聯 all driven by climate change.

Climate change is no longer a distant future scenario. It is here. It's tangible, it's costly, and it's dangerous. Climate change has entered a new and more critical phase, but the way we talk about it remains the same. And the poor outcome of this year's COP29 is clear proof of that.

The Global North bears an historical responsibility for the crisis we are facing. This was acknowledged in Article脗聽9 of the Paris Agreement. And yet, the Global North has shown itself to be unwilling and unprepared to honour its commitments by failing to provide necessary funding.

We are creating policies that are totally disconnected from reality. Instead, we must make reality the foundation of our political decisions. I am sorry, we actually decided on 1.5 and we already breached this.

Und es sind gerade die L脙陇nder des globalen S脙录dens, die am allermeisten unter Klimafolgen leiden m脙录ssen. 300脗聽Milliarden US-Dollar bis 2035 芒聙聯 ein Tropfen auf den hei脙聼en Stein. 脗聽脗聽Andrea Wechsler (PPE). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, sehr geehrter Herr Kommissar Hoekstra, sehr geehrte Damen und Herren! Auf der Klimaweltkonferenz COP脗聽29 hat sich gezeigt: Wir Europ脙陇er haben die globale F脙录hrungsrolle im Bereich des Klimaschutzes in Verantwortung f脙录r unsere B脙录rgerinnen und B脙录rger 脙录bernommen. Allen, die dazu beigetragen haben, m脙露chte ich an dieser Stelle herzlich danken. Wir haben klare Fortschritte erzielt, gemeinsam mit unseren internationalen Partnern.

Mit dem neuen globalen Finanzierungsziel von 300脗聽Milliarden Dollar j脙陇hrlich bis 2035 haben wir Solidarit脙陇t bewiesen, ohne unsere europ脙陇ischen M脙露glichkeiten zu 脙录berfordern. Ein entscheidender Erfolg ist nun, dass auch L脙陇nder mit wachsender wirtschaftlicher St脙陇rke ihren Beitrag leisten werden. Dabei ist eines klar: Basis eines verl脙陇sslichen und erfolgreichen Klimaschutzes ist eine wettbewerbsf脙陇hige Industrie in Europa. Daran arbeiten wir Hand in Hand mit dem Klimaschutz. Dabei werden wir weiterhin den Menschen in das Zentrum unserer Klimapolitik stellen.

脗聽脗聽Nikolina Brnjac (PPE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovani predsjedavaju脛聡i, po脜隆tovani povjereni脛聧e, dolazim iz Hrvatske, jedne od zemalja Mediterana, koji je suo脛聧en s ozbiljnim izazovima zbog klimatskih promjena. Mediteran, dom vi脜隆e od pola milijarde ljudi, zagrijava se br脜戮e od globalnog prosjeka, 脜隆to ga 脛聧ini jednom od klimatski najranjivijih regija na svijetu.

Hrvatska, s vi脜隆e od 1200 otoka i tre脛聡om najdu脜戮om obalom Sredozemnog mora, duboko razumije da vrijeme za odlu脛聧nu borbu protiv klimatskih promjena istje脛聧e. Zato mi je drago da nakon Glasgowske deklaracije imamo i novu deklaraciju o unaprije脛聭enoj klimatskoj akciji u turizmu.

Nastavljanje dosada脜隆njeg modela turizma, prema procjenama bi moglo dovesti do 脛聧ak 25 % pove脛聡anja emisija stakleni脛聧kih plinova povezanih s turizmom, koji je ve脛聡 sada odgovoran za 8,8 % globalnih emisija. U tom kontekstu vjerujem da 脛聡e i Europska unija, kao najposje脛聡enija destinacija svijeta, preuzeti vode脛聡e mjesto u stvaranju politika koje 脛聡e smanjiti 脜隆tetni utjecaj turizma na klimu.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jo脙拢o Oliveira (The Left). 芒聙聯 Os resultados desta COP 29 podem bem ser caracterizados pela indefini脙搂脙拢o quanto a metas, a insufici脙陋ncia de financiamento e desadequa脙搂脙拢o dos mecanismos para a adapta脙搂脙拢o e mitiga脙搂脙拢o das altera脙搂脙碌es clim脙隆ticas.

Os pa脙颅ses desenvolvidos sacodem as suas responsabilidades hist脙鲁ricas e procuram determinar as a脙搂脙碌es a implementar, condicionando-as a mais empr脙漏stimos e endividamento dos demais pa脙颅ses. Insistem em falsas e perigosas solu脙搂脙碌es de mercado, garantindo que, quem pode pagar, polui. N脙拢o 脙漏 poss脙颅vel garantir uma rela脙搂脙拢o harmoniosa entre o ser humano e a natureza, mantendo as mesmas pol脙颅ticas que aqui nos trouxeram.

Alterar paradigmas produtivos, recuperar setores estrat脙漏gicos para o controlo p脙潞blico, desenvolver pol脙颅ticas de combate ao desperd脙颅cio e 脙聽 obsolesc脙陋ncia programada, refor脙搂ar o investimento na investiga脙搂脙拢o cient脙颅fica, promover adequadas pol脙颅ticas de ordenamento territorial, reconhecer a cada pa脙颅s e a cada povo a sua soberania e o seu direito a produzir, e rejeitar a guerra. Esses s脙拢o elementos de uma pol脙颅tica alternativa imprescind脙颅vel para responder aos desafios ambientais com que a humanidade se confronta.
