脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Grzegorz Braun (NI). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, it's good and nice to hear that some of you are beginning to back up from your doctrine of global warming, Green Deal and other superstitions that are proposed here. It's nice that some back up, but it would be much nicer if you simply rejected this false, not scientifically-based, not fact-checked doctrine because it's devastating. It's ruining Europe and all the world wherever you Marxists, Leninists, Lysenkoists take power. So it's really high time and probably the last minute to jump off this crazy train that is heading to some abyss.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, liebe Menschen Europas! Wie bei jeder klimabezogenen Debatte h脙露ren wir viele gute Dinge, aber auch viele Dinge, die einen wirklich in die Verzweiflung treiben. Und damit beziehe ich mich nicht nur auf einen Kollegen, der von Aberglauben spricht, w脙陇hrend er gleichzeitig viele seiner Reden mit einer religi脙露sen Preisungsformel an Jesus Christus beginnt, sondern ich beziehe mich auf Kollegen, die den Klimawandel grunds脙陇tzlich leugnen, und脗聽芒聙聯脗聽am allerschlimmsten, und das habe ich heute wirklich zum ersten Mal in dieser Deutlichkeit geh脙露rt脗聽芒聙聯 auf Kollegen, die behaupten, wir h脙陇tten den Kampf gegen den Klimawandel bereits verloren.

Wir haben den Kampf gegen den Klimawandel nicht verloren. Wir werden ihn so lange nicht verlieren, wie wir weiterk脙陇mpfen. Und selbst wenn Russland sich nicht darum schert, selbst wenn China sich nicht darum schert, selbst wenn jetzt ein lustiger, oranger Mann im Wei脙聼en Haus sitzt, der ebenfalls behauptet, das w脙陇re alles nicht wahr, k脙露nnen wir trotzdem hier in Europa wenigstens unsere Umwelt sch脙录tzen, unser Klima sch脙录tzen. Also, liebe vern脙录nftige Kolleginnen und Kollegen: Bitte geben Sie niemals auf!

脗聽脗聽

(Fin des interventions 脙聽 la demande)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Wopke Hoekstra, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr President, many thanks for the questions and comments by the various Members, and particularly those who came with those supremely nuanced perspectives and solution-oriented contributions.

I think it is probably helpful to underline at least three things that might have been misunderstood. But before I move into these areas, let me once again say that indeed, this was hard fought. This was not easy. This type of international deals are never perfect. But not having a deal would have been dramatic, and I truly do think this is a significant step in the right direction.

First, let me say also because there was misunderstanding in this area why this is a significant step. It is to a large extent because of the USD脗聽300脗聽billion, but we all know also from our part of the world the meaning of the word leverage. And if you put 300 on the table, you do have more than a fighting chance to indeed reach the USD脗聽1.3脗聽trillion. That is actually essential 芒聙聯 that does move the needle in terms of climate ambition.

Secondly, it is important to understand what was reached in terms of the contributions of those who have not yet contributed. Clearly, the logic of developed and developing is something that we in this room question in the sense that at least a significant group of countries made huge steps forward in terms of their wealth, their affluence, and we feel that they have the responsibility to contribute to the solution. It was never in the making that they would formally change these categorisations. But what is a huge step forward is that we managed to persuade them to voluntarily contribute to the solution, and many of them said that they will indeed do so.

By the way, the nature of any type of these arrangements is voluntary. There's no legal way to make sure that countries live up to them: it is a commitment, it is a diplomatic commitment that, of course, we first of all, should acknowledge and respect.

Thirdly, I do want to articulate to those who suggest that this is a huge amount of money, that this is the end of our economy and also suggest that, you know, all sorts of ministers have made all sorts of wide-ranging commitments: please truly look at the various budgetary implications. The number we are talking about is a 2035 number. There is no allocation key, so it is up to Member States to decide. Many of our Member States, by the way, do a lot because Europe pays for one third of international climate ambitions. But it is in particular an invitation to other developed, and certainly also other developing, countries to join and become part of the solution space even more.

A couple of our Member States have articulated that they indeed do want to step up and do want to do more, but that is up to Member States. As I've said before, a large chunk of this money is brought in by the MDBs, the development banks. Why is that relevant? Well, because that means that many of our Member States allocate capital, but it is something else than a cash out. So I do think those who have been critical about the money being too much or that it would be unfairly spent, given the positions of Member States that the reality truly is different.

I hope that this helps. I very much agree with all those who have articulated, once again, the tremendous importance of following science 芒聙聯 that is at the very heart of this. That is why we do this. It is not an opinion. It is not something that we just pursue for whatever fancy reason, it is a necessity. It will continue to be a necessity 芒聙聯 things are getting worse rather than better, and the cost of doing nothing will be much larger than the cost of action today.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Le Pr脙漏sident. 芒聙聯 Nous arrivons au terme de cette discussion. Le d脙漏bat est clos.

D脙漏clarations 脙漏crites (article 178)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nicol脙隆s Gonz脙隆lez Casares (S&D), por escrito. 芒聙聯 La CP29, celebrada en Bak脙潞, ha concluido con resultados claramente insuficientes ante la magnitud de la crisis clim脙隆tica.

Aunque se acord脙鲁 una nueva meta colectiva global cuantificada de movilizar 300脗聽000脗聽millones de d脙鲁lares anuales para los pa脙颅ses en desarrollo, este compromiso sigue siendo insuficiente frente a las necesidades de las naciones m脙隆s vulnerables, como los peque脙卤os estados insulares que se enfrentan a amenazas existenciales.

Es crucial avanzar con medidas ambiciosas de adaptaci脙鲁n, eliminar progresivamente los subsidios a combustibles f脙鲁siles y fortalecer las estrategias de gesti脙鲁n del agua y la resiliencia clim脙隆tica. Adem脙隆s, llamamos a una actualizaci脙鲁n ambiciosa de las contribuciones determinadas a nivel nacional (CDN) antes de la CP30 para garantizar el cumplimiento de los objetivos del Acuerdo de Par脙颅s.

Los socialistas espa脙卤oles creemos firmemente que la Uni脙鲁n Europea debe liderar con ambici脙鲁n y determinaci脙鲁n no solo en la financiaci脙鲁n clim脙隆tica, sino tambi脙漏n en la promoci脙鲁n de una transici脙鲁n justa y sostenible. Es nuestra responsabilidad demostrar que, frente a la mayor amenaza global de nuestro tiempo, es posible actuar con rapidez y determinaci脙鲁n, asegurando justicia clim脙隆tica y un futuro sostenible para todas las generaciones.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Javi L脙鲁pez (S&D), por escrito. 芒聙聯 La CP29, celebrada en Bak脙潞, ha concluido con resultados claramente insuficientes ante la magnitud de la crisis clim脙隆tica.

Aunque se acord脙鲁 una nueva meta colectiva global cuantificada de movilizar 300脗聽000脗聽millones de d脙鲁lares anuales para los pa脙颅ses en desarrollo, este compromiso sigue siendo insuficiente frente a las necesidades de las naciones m脙隆s vulnerables, como los peque脙卤os estados insulares que se enfrentan a amenazas existenciales.

Es crucial avanzar con medidas ambiciosas de adaptaci脙鲁n, eliminar progresivamente los subsidios a combustibles f脙鲁siles y fortalecer las estrategias de gesti脙鲁n del agua y la resiliencia clim脙隆tica. Adem脙隆s, llamamos a una actualizaci脙鲁n ambiciosa de las contribuciones determinadas a nivel nacional (CDN) antes de la CP30 para garantizar el cumplimiento de los objetivos del Acuerdo de Par脙颅s.

Los socialistas espa脙卤oles creemos firmemente que la Uni脙鲁n Europea debe liderar con ambici脙鲁n y determinaci脙鲁n no solo en la financiaci脙鲁n clim脙隆tica, sino tambi脙漏n en la promoci脙鲁n de una transici脙鲁n justa y sostenible. Es nuestra responsabilidad demostrar que, frente a la mayor amenaza global de nuestro tiempo, es posible actuar con rapidez y determinaci脙鲁n, asegurando justicia clim脙隆tica y un futuro sostenible para todas las generaciones.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR), in writing. 芒聙聯 USD脗聽300 billion each year by 2035.脗聽That芒聙聶s how much money will be ripped off from European and other developed countries as a result of the climate conference.脗聽For what? For funding climate work in Africa, for instance.

Europe accounts for only eight per脗聽cent of global emissions. However, we are told to pay up, while China and India only keep polluting more and more.

Once again, we have been fooled.

And one thing I cannot understand: how can we agree on financing other countries芒聙聶 climate goals with these astronomical sums, and at the same time have troubles finding the resources needed to defend our own continent?

So let芒聙聶s not be fools anymore and let芒聙聶s put our priorities in order. For us Europe must come first.

脗聽

15. Full accession of Bulgaria and Romania to the Schengen Area: the urgent need to lift controls at internal land borders (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Le Pr脙漏sident. 芒聙聯 Je suis tr脙篓s heureux d'appeler 脙聽 l'ordre du jour la d脙漏claration de la Commission sur l'adh脙漏sion pleine et enti脙篓re de la Bulgarie et de la Roumanie 脙聽 l'espace Schengen, la n脙漏cessit脙漏 urgente de lever les contr脙麓les aux fronti脙篓res terrestres int脙漏rieures (2024/2943(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ylva Johansson, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, honourable Members, this is a bit of an emotional moment for me. This is my last Strasbourg session. Standing here, I think back on the many discussions we have had here, often emotional ones. Fires, shipwrecks, terrible crimes, Covid, war 芒聙聯 that reminds us everything we do is always about people, human beings.脗聽But there are also happy moments and today is one of those. I am convinced that Bulgaria and Romania will enjoy all the benefits of the Schengen Area, and I think that will be from the start of the new year.

Think about how far we have come. Five years ago, Schengen enlargement was not on the agenda when I took office. Croatia, Bulgaria and Romania were stuck in the waiting room, even though they were ready, the Commission said they were ready, we have said that we are ready for 13 years, Parliament said they were ready, but the Council still had to take the decision.

But sometimes change can come very quickly 芒聙聯 quickly, but not automatically. We all worked very hard to make this change happen by working together and building trust. Last two years have been pivotal. Croatia entered Schengen in January last year. Romania and Bulgaria joined in March this year and checks, first at the air and sea borders, were lifted. The decision changed the lives of millions. For Romania alone, 12 million passengers have travelled without border checks and without hiccups. And now we are close to taking the final steps to lifting also the land border controls.

Last Friday, I was in Budapest. I took part in an informal ministerial meeting chaired by the Hungarian Presidency with the participation of ministers of interior from Austria, Bulgaria and Romania. The ministers reached a very important agreement last Friday, and this meeting was an important step forward. The ministers committed to put the issue on the agenda for the next Justice and Home Affairs Council in December, and I expect the Hungarian Presidency to propose to the Council in December to lift the border controls in the land borders for Bulgaria and Romania from the 1脗聽January脗聽2025. I expect that this will be actually the case.

And if you ask how we got here, it's not only by negotiations; it's about working together, taking concrete measures on the ground to counter irregular migration, to fight the smugglers, to build and invest in our common security. And measures are paying off: since Bulgaria and Romania joined the Schengen family, Dublin transfers are increasing and work very well today. Frontex is reinforcing the external borders, with nearly 250 officers in Bulgaria, 220 in Romania. Since these two Member States joined, irregular border crossings at their external borders have dropped with 80%.

I have only a few days left as Commissioner, so it will be my successor who will be present at the JHA Council next month. I won't be there, but you can be sure I will be following. I already had the champagne ready, and you can be sure that on New Year's Day, I will be thinking of my friends in Romania and Bulgaria. I am very happy because they have worked towards this goal for many, many years. And I'm very happy that at the end of this mandate, we are so close to the decision to fully welcome Bulgaria and Romania. But most importantly, I am so happy for all the citizens in Bulgaria and Romania. I would like to say to you: you belong to Schengen. You deserve to benefit from all the freedoms in the Schengen Area.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Le Pr脙漏sident. 芒聙聯 Merci, Madame la Commissaire, c'est effectivement un moment historique dans l'histoire des peuples bulgare et roumain, dans l'histoire de l'Union europ脙漏enne.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lena D脙录pont, on behalf of the PPE Group. 芒聙聯 Mr President, Commissioner, soon we will see three days in one. One will be a day to celebrate. Finally, after long years of negotiations, after fulfilling the criteria since 2011, after going through the gradual approach, finally Romania and Bulgaria will be members of the Schengen Area 芒聙聯 hopefully as of next year.

By saying so, we leave no space for the Council. The decision needs to be approved, and we will not and cannot accept any other outcome. It will not only mean a significant boost for business and travel, for trade and free movement; most importantly, for Romanians and Bulgarians it means 'welcome to the family of Schengen countries'.

Second, it is a day to take stock. Critically, it's safe to say Schengen has been in better shape. By now, nine Member States have introduced, or will introduce, internal border checks for various reasons. But Schengen is not under threat because of the decision of the Member States, because of the reasons to take that decision. The rise of migrant smuggling, organised crime groups exploiting the Schengen Area, terrorist threats we are facing, the challenging geopolitical landscape and the security picture: it is a call to action.

And this leads me to the third day. It is a day to restart and to continue the work of strengthening Schengen: by working and delivering on a true European strategy for internal security; by implementing and further developing the Asylum and Migration Pact in all its facets; by taking up the fight against organised crime; by boosting the capacities and the competences of our security agencies, both at national and European level; by allowing them and finally trusting them to do their work, especially in these times; by finally understanding customs as part of our security architecture; and by preparing ourselves for a dynamic and rapidly changing security landscape.

That will require strength. It will require capabilities and flexibility. But it's time that we deliver on a European Union internal security strategy that is worth its name. So we celebrate, but we also roll up our sleeves. It's time for more Europe, not less.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脨聺脨掳脨麓脩聫脨虏脨掳脨录 脩聛脨碌, 脩聡脨碌 脨陆脨掳 12 脨麓脨碌脨潞脨碌脨录脨虏脩聙脨赂 脩聣脨碌 脨赂脨录脨掳脨录脨碌 脨戮脨潞脨戮脨陆脩聡脨掳脩聜脨碌脨禄脨陆脨戮 脩聙脨碌脩聢脨碌脨陆脨赂脨碌 脨卤脨碌脨路 脨麓脨戮脨驴脩聤脨禄脨陆脨赂脩聜脨碌脨禄脨陆脨赂 脩聝脩聛脨禄脨戮脨虏脨赂脩聫. 脨聵 脩聜脨掳脨潞脨掳, 脨麓脩聤脨禄脨鲁脨戮脨鲁脨戮脨麓脨赂脩聢脨陆脨掳脩聜脨掳 脩聛脨掳脨驴脩聝脨陆脨碌脨陆脨掳 脨戮脨驴脨碌脩聙脨掳 芒聙聻脨聭脩聤脨禄脨鲁脨掳脩聙脨赂脩聫 脨赂 脨聽脩聝脨录脩聤脨陆脨赂脩聫 脨虏 脨篓脨碌脨陆脨鲁脨碌脨陆芒聙聹 脩聣脨碌 脨路脨掳脨虏脩聤脩聙脩聢脨赂 脩聛 脩聟脨碌脨驴脨赂脨碌脨陆脨麓.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Fabrice Leggeri, au nom du groupe PfE. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, la Bulgarie et la Roumanie s'appr脙陋tent 脙聽 int脙漏grer pleinement l'espace Schengen, ouvrant leurs fronti脙篓res terrestres int脙漏rieures 脙聽 la libre circulation. C'est une bombe migratoire pour l'Europe de l'Ouest. Les fronti脙篓res ext脙漏rieures ne seront pas pr脙陋tes si Bruxelles persiste dans sa fuite en avant.

La nouvelle Commission europ脙漏enne, sans Mme脗聽Johansson, devra renforcer la fronti脙篓re terrestre entre la Bulgarie et la Turquie, ainsi qu'entre la Gr脙篓ce et la Turquie, y compris en finan脙搂ant des murs. Tout franchissement ill脙漏gal doit 脙陋tre stopp脙漏. Pas de discours, pas de demi-mesure. Ce sont nos nations qui sont en jeu.

Au lieu d'aider les ONG complices du chaos migratoire, l'Union europ脙漏enne doit aider la Bulgarie et la Roumanie 脙聽 payer des salaires dignes 脙聽 leurs gardes-fronti脙篓res, car, avec Schengen, ils deviennent nos gardes-fronti脙篓res. Frontex doit prot脙漏ger l'Europe et ne plus faire la propagande des ONG.

Enfin, anticipons de futurs flux migratoires 脙聽 travers la Bulgarie et la Roumanie vers l'ouest de l'Europe. Laissez faire, et ce sera l'effondrement de Schengen. Il est temps que l'Europe d脙漏fende ses fronti脙篓res ou elle dispara脙庐tra.

脗聽脗聽Claudiu-Richard T脙垄rziu, 脙庐n numele grupului ECR. 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, stima脠聸i colegi, Rom脙垄nia 脙庐ndepline脠聶te de mul脠聸i ani toate condi脠聸iile pentru aderare complet脛聝 la spa脠聸iul Schengen. Este un adev脛聝r pe care nu-l poate contesta nimeni. Cu toate acestea, am fost 脠聸inu脠聸i 脙庐ntr-o prea lung脛聝 脠聶i umilitoare a脠聶teptare la u脠聶a Schengen, f脛聝r脛聝 prea multe explica脠聸ii. P脙垄n脛聝 la urm脛聝, dup脛聝 negocieri, am脙垄n脛聝ri 脠聶i concesii foarte serioase f脛聝cute de c脛聝tre Rom脙垄nia, ni s-a recunoscut doar o parte din dreptul care ni se cuvine.

脙聨n aceast脛聝 perioad脛聝, economia 脠聶i demnitatea rom脙垄neasc脛聝 au avut mult de suferit. C脙垄teva dintre statele Uniunii Europene 脙庐nc脛聝 blocheaz脛聝 acceptarea 脠聸脛聝rii mele 脙庐n spa脠聸iul Schengen. Este inadmisibil s脛聝 se prelungeasc脛聝 controalele la frontierele terestre pentru 脙庐nc脛聝 vreo c脙垄teva luni. Rom脙垄nia merit脛聝 s脛聝 adere la spa脠聸iul Schengen 脠聶i sunt aici pentru c脛聝 v脛聝 cer sprijinul pentru ca acest obiectiv s脛聝 se realizeze imediat.

脗聽脗聽Fabienne Keller, au nom du groupe Renew. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, Madame la Commissaire, ch脙篓re Ylva脗聽Johansson, c'est une victoire pour les citoyens bulgares et roumains bien s脙禄r, qui vont bient脙麓t b脙漏n脙漏ficier de la libre circulation compl脙篓te au sein de l'Union europ脙漏enne. Apr脙篓s tant d'ann脙漏es d'efforts, la Bulgarie et la Roumanie ont mis les moyens n脙漏cessaires et apport脙漏 toutes les garanties requises en mati脙篓re de contr脙麓le et de s脙漏curit脙漏 aux fronti脙篓res ext脙漏rieures.

C'est une victoire aussi, chers coll脙篓gues, pour le projet europ脙漏en. Un espace Schengen qui se renforce, c'est une Union qui grandit. Plusieurs d脙漏cennies apr脙篓s sa cr脙漏ation, l'espace de libre circulation des personnes, des biens et des capitaux est un succ脙篓s incontestable au service de la prosp脙漏rit脙漏 de notre continent. Rappelons-nous que ce projet est n脙漏 d'une poign脙漏e de dirigeants qui croyaient dans la coop脙漏ration et dans l'unit脙漏 des pays europ脙漏ens; un h脙漏ritage politique qui nous est cher et que nous continuerons de porter.

Madame la Commissaire, je voudrais saluer votre engagement r脙漏solu, tout au long de votre mandat, pour l'ouverture de l'espace Schengen 脙聽 la Roumanie et 脙聽 la Bulgarie. Avec mon groupe Renew Europe, Madame la Commissaire, nous vous en remercions sinc脙篓rement.

脗聽脗聽Nicolae 脠聵tef脛聝nu脠聸脛聝, 脙庐n numele grupului Verts/ALE. 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, doamn脛聝 comisar脛聝, trebuie s脛聝 recunosc c脛聝 am emo脠聸ii 脠聶i c脛聝 m-a emo脠聸ionat discursul dumneavoastr脛聝 pentru c脛聝 am sim脠聸it c脛聝 vine din suflet. Croa脠聸ia, Rom脙垄nia, Bulgaria 芒聙聯 nu e pu脠聸in pentru un comisar, a脠聶a c脛聝 simt c脛聝 a脠聸i f脛聝cut tot ce a脠聸i putut 脠聶i vreau s脛聝 v脛聝 mul脠聸umesc personal pentru asta 脠聶i pentru milioanele de rom脙垄ni, pentru c脛聝 13 ani de a脠聶teptare au 脙庐nsemnat pentru rom脙垄ni suferin脠聸脛聝, costuri foarte multe, miliarde, miliarde de euro care au plecat din buzunarul rom脙垄nilor, poluare, emisii de dioxid de carbon, dar au mai 脙庐nsemnat 脠聶i un sentiment par脠聶iv de cet脛聝脠聸ean de m脙垄na a doua.

Mul脠聸i au luat acest sentiment cu durere, pentru c脛聝 ei iubesc Europa. Mai mult, a alimentat discursul extremei drepte, care acum a 脙庐mpins un candidat care este prorus 脙庐n runda a doua a preziden脠聸ialelor. Este trist, dar s脛聝 ne bucur脛聝m totu脠聶i ast脛聝zi c脛聝 auzim aceste mesaje 脙庐ncurajatoare. S脛聝 vedem Rom脙垄nia 脠聶i Bulgaria 脙庐n ianuarie 脙庐n tot Schengenul, inclusiv terestru, maritim 脠聶i aerian. Simt c脛聝 pleac脛聝 ast脛聝zi un european convins de la c脙垄rma Europei. Noi r脛聝m脙垄nem aici s脛聝 ducem mai departe spiritul acesta european. Tr脛聝iasc脛聝 Rom脙垄nia 脠聶i Bulgaria 脙庐n Schengen!

脗聽脗聽Pernando Barrena Arza, en nombre del Grupo The Left. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤or脙颅as, voy directamente al grano para referirme al tema que nos ocupa y unirme al llamamiento a los Estados miembros y al Consejo para que admitan a Ruman脙颅a y Bulgaria como miembros de pleno derecho del espacio Schengen, tambi脙漏n en las fronteras terrestres.

Queremos, adem脙隆s, aprovechar esta intervenci脙鲁n para denunciar el uso abusivo del C脙鲁digo de Fronteras Schengen que algunos Estados miembros est脙隆n cometiendo, encadenando peticiones extraordinarias a la Comisi脙鲁n cada seis meses. Alemania ha establecido controles fronterizos en septiembre, Francia los ha vuelto a instalar hace un mes, Austria los tiene con sus vecinos del este y del sur, y Eslovenia, Suecia, Italia y Dinamarca los utilizan de manera espor脙隆dica.

Quiero subrayar que esta situaci脙鲁n provoca un gran malestar en las comunidades transfronterizas, amenaza el derecho a la libre circulaci脙鲁n de personas en Europa 芒聙聰recuerden que estamos hablando de un derecho fundamental de la Uni脙鲁n芒聙聰 y, adem脙隆s, causa serios problemas de seguridad en las rutas migratorias, como en el caso del r脙颅o Bidasoa en el Pa脙颅s Vasco donde, debido a los controles fronterizos franceses, diez脗聽migrantes han perdido la vida en los 脙潞ltimos a脙卤os.

Europa necesita volver a sus or脙颅genes humanistas y de defensa genuina de los derechos humanos. Aqu脙颅 es donde se juega, especialmente, la credibilidad y el futuro del proyecto europeo.

脗聽脗聽Mary Khan, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, sehr geehrte Damen und Herren! Letzte Woche war ich in Sofia bei unseren bulgarischen Freunden von der Partei Wasraschdane zu Gast 芒聙聯 einer Partei, die 脙录brigens immer mehr im Aufwind ist und vor allem Unterst脙录tzung von den B脙录rgern gewinnt. Warum? Weil sich in ganz Europa vor allem patriotische und europafreundliche Kr脙陇fte erheben.

Und was nicht europafreundlich ist, ist Ihre Politik der Massenmigration. Seit fast zehn Jahren sorgt diese daf脙录r, dass die Sicherheit auf unserem Kontinent gef脙陇hrdet wird und der Geist von Schengen regelrecht zerst脙露rt wird. Nat脙录rlich w脙录nschen wir uns in einer idealen Welt einen starken und vor allem funktionierenden Schengen-Raum. Doch wie so viele gute Ideen wurde auch dieses Vorhaben von Ihrer linksgr脙录nen Politik ruiniert. Solange die Au脙聼engrenzen der EU nicht sicher sind, ist es unverantwortlich, die Binnengrenzen dort abzuschaffen.

Sie streichen 1079 Kilometer Grenzkontrollen und 脙露ffnen damit die T脙录r f脙录r noch mehr illegale Migration nach Deutschland. Der Schengen-Raum wurde nie daf脙录r geschaffen, illegale Fluchtrouten noch weiter auszubauen. Die Menschen in Europa fordern Grenzkontrollen. Sie tun hier das Gegenteil. Sie 脙露ffnen kriminellen Netzwerken und Menschenh脙陇ndlern T脙录r und Tor. Das ist nicht europ脙陇isch, das ist realit脙陇tsfern. Unsere bulgarischen Partner sehen das 脙录brigens genauso. Sie wollen keine unkontrollierte Migration. Und wenn Bulgarien in den Schengen-Raum aufgenommen wird, wird die Route 脙录ber diese L脙陇nder noch attraktiver f脙录r Wirtschaftsfl脙录chtlinge.

脗聽脗聽Siegfried Mure脜聼an (PPE). 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, stima脠聸i colegi, la finalul lunii martie a acestui an, Rom脙垄nia 脠聶i Bulgaria au aderat la spa脠聸iul Schengen cu frontiera aerian脛聝 脠聶i cu frontiera maritim脛聝 脠聶i am v脛聝zut cu to脠聸ii cum aderarea Rom脙垄niei 脠聶i a Bulgariei la spa脠聸iul Schengen cu frontiera aerian脛聝 脠聶i maritim脛聝 a f脛聝cut spa脠聸iul Schengen mai sigur. Autorit脛聝脠聸ile din Rom脙垄nia 脠聶i din Bulgaria au cooperat cu autorit脛聝脠聸ile din toate celelalte state membre ale Uniunii Europene, cu institu脠聸iile Uniunii Europene, pentru a combate migra脠聸ia ilegal脛聝, pentru a proteja frontiera extern脛聝 a Uniunii Europene 脠聶i am v脛聝zut c脛聝 spa脠聸iul Schengen este mai puternic cu Bulgaria 脠聶i Rom脙垄nia 脙庐n interiorul s脛聝u.

Pe baza acestor evolu脠聸ii pozitive 脠聶i 脠聸in脙垄nd cont de faptul c脛聝 Rom脙垄nia 脠聶i Bulgaria deja de mul脠聸i ani 脙庐ndeplinesc toate criteriile pentru aderarea la spa脠聸iul Schengen, acum, aderarea se va finaliza 脠聶i cu frontiera terestr脛聝. Sunt convins c脛聝 acest lucru va fi un beneficiu pentru cet脛聝脠聸enii Rom脙垄niei 脠聶i ai Bulgariei, pentru 脙庐ntreprinz脛聝torii din 脠聸脛聝rile noastre, dar va fi un c脙垄脠聶tig pentru toat脛聝 Uniunea European脛聝. Este 脠聶i un semnal foarte clar pe care 脙庐l trimitem extremi脠聶tilor 脠聶i populi脠聶tilor: Uniunea European脛聝 este unit脛聝, Uniunea European脛聝 livreaz脛聝. A脠聶tept脛聝m implementarea acestei decizii 脙庐n urm脛聝toarele s脛聝pt脛聝m脙垄ni.

脗聽脗聽Andr脙隆s L脙隆szl脙鲁 (PfE). 芒聙聯 Tisztelt Eln脙露k 脙職r! A magyar EU-eln脙露ks脙漏g egy t脙露rt脙漏nelmi meg脙隆llapod脙隆s k脙录sz脙露b脙漏n 脙隆ll. 2007-es EU-s csatlakoz脙隆suk ut脙隆n janu脙隆r 1-j脙漏t脜聭l Bulg脙隆ria 脙漏s Rom脙隆nia a schengeni 脙露vezet teljes jog脙潞 tagjaiv脙隆 v脙隆lhatnak. 脗聽脗聽Dan Barna (Renew). 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, 脙庐n acest Parlament s-a spus de nenum脛聝rate ori: Rom脙垄nia a 脙庐ndeplinit cu r脛聝bdare fiecare cerin脠聸脛聝 de aderare la Schengen. Cu toate acestea, locul nostru de drept 脙庐n Schengen ne-a fost refuzat timp de 13脗聽ani, cel mai recent prin vetoul injust al Austriei. Aceast脛聝 obstruc脠聸ionare cinic-politic脛聝 contrazice principiile Uniunii Europene de unitate, solidaritate 脠聶i 脙庐ncredere, ignor脛聝 angajamentul nostru ferm fa脠聸脛聝 de responsabilitatea comun脛聝 de a asigura frontierele externe ale Europei, submineaz脛聝 exact poten脠聸ialul economic al regiunii noastre 脠聶i desconsider脛聝 dreptul la liber脛聝 circula脠聸ie al cet脛聝脠聸enilor rom脙垄ni 脠聶i bulgari.

Rom脙垄nia a fost 脠聶i este 脙庐n tot acest timp un partener de 脙庐ncredere, un ap脛聝r脛聝tor ferm al valorilor europene 脠聶i un contributor la securitatea 脠聶i prosperitatea Uniunii. Ianuarie 2025 nu mai este o aspira脠聸ie, este un imperativ. Noi 脙庐nt脙垄rzieri sunt inacceptabile. Cerem ca acest termen s脛聝 fie respectat nu ca o concesie amabil脛聝, ci ca o recunoa脠聶tere a locului nostru de drept 脙庐n spa脠聸iul Schengen. 脙聨n plus, v脛聝 rog s脛聝 v脛聝 脙庐ntreba脠聸i c脙垄nd constat脛聝m valul de populism 脠聶i extremism din Europa dac脛聝 refuzul acestui drept nu a fost, poate, una dintre cauze.

脗聽脗聽Erik Marquardt (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, Frau Kommissarin! Zuallererst m脙露chte ich Ihnen danken f脙录r die Arbeit, die Sie geleistet haben in den letzten f脙录nf Jahren. Ich glaube, dass Sie in einem wirklich schwierigen politischen Umfeld das Bestm脙露gliche geschafft haben, was Sie schaffen konnten. Und auf der anderen Seite wissen Sie auch: Es sind noch einige Aufgaben 脙录brig, uns wird also nicht langweilig. Danke daf脙录r, dass Sie nicht alles erledigt haben und wir hier weiterarbeiten k脙露nnen.

Ich glaube, was wir, wenn es um Schengen geht, verstehen m脙录ssen, ist, dass erst einmal der Beitritt von Rum脙陇nien und Bulgarien nicht nur ein politischer Erfolg ist, sondern ein ganz konkreter Gewinn f脙录r脗聽脙录ber 25 Millionen Menschen in Bulgarien und Rum脙陇nien, die jetzt endlich auch vollst脙陇ndiger Teil des Raums der Sicherheit, der Freiheit und des Rechts werden. Ich denke, was wir in diesem Zusammenhang verstehen m脙录ssen, ist, dass wir in schwierigen geopolitischen Zeiten am st脙陇rksten sind, wenn wir als Europa zusammenhalten, wenn wir auf der einen Seite f脙录r die Sicherheit vor organisierter Kriminalit脙陇t sorgen, f脙录r die Freiheit脗聽芒聙聯 zum Beispiel die Reisefreiheit 芒聙聯, wenn wir aber auch daf脙录r sorgen, dass das Recht an den Grenzen von Schengen gilt, dass wir die St脙陇rke des Rechts an unseren Grenzen hervorheben, das als St脙陇rke begreifen, nicht das Recht des St脙陇rkeren durchsetzen, wie wir es an einigen Au脙聼engrenzen immer wieder sehen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nora Junco Garc脙颅a (NI). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤or presidente, se脙卤ora comisaria, la plena integraci脙鲁n de Ruman脙颅a y Bulgaria en el espacio Schengen es incuestionable. 脗驴Saben ustedes a los desaf脙颅os que se enfrentan los transportistas rumanos y b脙潞lgaros cada vez que tienen que trabajar en la Uni脙鲁n Europea? 脗驴Saben de qu脙漏 manera esta exclusi脙鲁n afecta a la igualdad de derechos y oportunidades en comparaci脙鲁n con otros ciudadanos de la Uni脙鲁n Europea?

En un momento en el que hablamos permanentemente de unidad no tiene sentido mantener las barreras internas entre europeos. Tanto Ruman脙颅a como Bulgaria cumplen con todos los criterios t脙漏cnicos desde hace m脙隆s de una d脙漏cada. Entonces 脗驴cu脙隆l es la justificaci脙鲁n para seguir bloqueando su acceso completo? 脗驴No estamos fallando a los valores que tan repetidamente dicen que compartimos?

Integrar a Ruman脙颅a y a Bulgaria plenamente no es solo una cuesti脙鲁n t脙漏cnica o econ脙鲁mica, es un reconocimiento a su papel como aliados clave en esta estabilidad del este de Europa. Y por eso les pido que apoyen esta medida y doten de ayudas a estos pa脙颅ses para reforzar sus fronteras exteriores.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Victor Negrescu (S&D). 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, Rom脙垄nia va adera complet la spa脠聸iul Schengen 脙庐ncep脙垄nd cu anul viitor. Dup脛聝 13 ani de a脠聶teptare, aceast脛聝 nedreptate poate fi 脙庐n sf脙垄r脠聶it corectat脛聝. Rom脙垄nii vor face parte din Europa f脛聝r脛聝 frontiere dac脛聝 pe 13 decembrie mini脠聶trii de interne vor valida decizia privind integrarea deplin脛聝 a 脠聸脛聝rii noastre 脙庐n spa脠聸iul Schengen. Ast脛聝zi sper c脛聝 este ultima dezbatere de acest fel aici, 脙庐n plenul Parlamentului European.

脙聨n to脠聸i ace脠聶ti ani de a脠聶teptare am ac脠聸ionat 脙庐n mod constant, am ini脠聸iat peti脠聸ii, am discutat 脠聶i convins oficiali europeni 脠聶i am modificat chiar 脠聶i legisla脠聸ia european脛聝. 脙聨n ultimii trei ani am ob脠聸inut zeci de milioane de euro, fonduri europene pentru aderarea Rom脙垄niei 脠聶i Bulgariei la spa脠聸iul Schengen. De altfel, 脙庐n bugetul pentru anul viitor am inclus o alocare suplimentar脛聝 de 10 milioane de euro 脙庐n acest sens. Am fost implicat direct 脙庐n negocierile care s-au purtat. 脙聨i mul脠聸umesc doamnei comisare pentru efortul 脠聶i implicarea domniei sale 脠聶i da, Rom脙垄nia a demonstrat c脛聝 poate proteja frontierele externe ale Uniunii Europene. Sper ca antieuropenii s脛聝 nu pun脛聝 脙庐n pericol acest obiectiv. Le mul脠聸umesc rom脙垄nilor c脛聝 au crezut 脙庐n noi 脠聶i sunt convins c脛聝 p脙垄n脛聝 la finalul anului acest obiectiv va fi realizat.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Petra Steger (PfE). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, sehr geehrte Damen und Herren! Schengen ist gescheitert 芒聙聯 gescheitert durch die z脙录gellose Masseneinwanderung und gescheitert wegen Ihnen, wegen Ihrer Unf脙陇higkeit und Unwilligkeit, diese Massenzuwanderung zu bek脙陇mpfen und endlich die Au脙聼engrenzen zu sch脙录tzen. Von Schengen profitieren heute nur noch die Wirtschaftsmigranten und ihre Schlepper 芒聙聯 anstatt die B脙录rger. Es ist daher nicht verantwortungsvoll, zum jetzigen Zeitpunkt den Beitritt von Rum脙陇nien und Bulgarien zum Schengen-Raum weiter voranzutreiben, w脙陇hrend die EU芒聙聭Spitzen vollkommen versagen, einen funktionierenden Au脙聼engrenzschutz endlich sicherzustellen.

Das ist kein Vorwurf an Bulgarien oder Rum脙陇nien, sondern an die Versager in der Europ脙陇ischen Union. Sogar innerhalb des Schengen-Raums m脙录ssen immer mehr L脙陇nder wieder Grenzkontrollen einf脙录hren, um die Asyltouristen an der Einreise zu hindern 芒聙聯 ein Armutszeugnis f脙录r die Europ脙陇ische Union und die von ihr versprochene Reisefreiheit.

Doch anstatt die Probleme zu l脙露sen, sehen wir, wie die EU einen politischen Kuhhandel eingeht. 脙聳sterreich ist nun offenbar bereit, das Veto gegen die Erweiterung des Schengen-Raums aufzugeben. Und wof脙录r? Offensichtlich daf脙录r, dass die abgew脙陇hlte 脙聳VP den fachlich vollkommen inkompetenten Magnus Brunner als EU芒聙聭Migrationskommissar bekommt. Nicht nur ein fauler Deal auf dem R脙录cken der 脙聳sterreicher, sondern insgesamt ein schlechtes Zeichen f脙录r die Zukunft.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Alessandro Ciriani (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signor Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, l'area Schengen 脙篓 un pilastro dell'Unione Europea: Bulgaria e Romania aspirano da tempo per completare il percorso di adesione e noi non possiamo non apprezzare il lungo lavoro svolto dai due Paesi per soddisfare i criteri tecnici richiesti ed esprimere il nostro convinto sostegno.

Resta tuttavia un dato, un dato che non possiamo ignorare: nello stesso momento in cui parliamo dell'abolizione dei controlli alle frontiere per loro, altri Paesi li ripristinano a causa di un'immigrazione incontrollata che spaventa, che impedisce qualsiasi razionale politica di inclusione e che, al contrario, alimenta la povert脙聽 economica, quella culturale e la criminalit脙聽.

Se vogliamo che l'area Schengen torni a rappresentare un cardine europeo, occorre creare le condizioni di base con un deciso e realistico cambio di rotta, puntando sul controllo delle frontiere esterne, sull'accordo con i Paesi terzi, sull'apertura di hotspot fuori dai confini europei, seguendo la strada aperta in Italia dal governo Meloni.

脗聽脗聽Marta Wcis脜聜o (PPE). 芒聙聯 Panie Przewodnicz脛聟cy! Szanowni Pa脜聞stwo! Koledzy i Kole脜录anki! W czasach mi脛聶dzynarodowego kryzysu my jako Unia Europejska musimy sta脛聡 si脛聶 tak bliscy i solidarni jak nigdy. Sytuacja, w kt脙鲁rej niekt脙鲁re pa脜聞stwa cz脜聜onkowskie wci脛聟脜录 walcz脛聟 o prawo do bycia cz脛聶脜聸ci脛聟 wsp脙鲁lnoty Schengen, jak Bu脜聜garia i Rumunia, inne lekcewa脜录脛聟 t脛聟 fundamentaln脛聟 warto脜聸脛聡 i pr脙鲁buj脛聟 ponownie wprowadza脛聡 granice wewn脛聟trz wsp脙鲁lnoty.

Jako Parlament Europejski musimy wszystkich cz脜聜onk脙鲁w traktowa脛聡 uczciwie, z szacunkiem, jako r脙鲁wnych partner脙鲁w, a warto脜聸ci swobodnego przep脜聜ywu os脙鲁b i towar脙鲁w powinny by脛聡 w sercu Unii Europejskiej. Dlatego wspieramy Rumuni脛聶 i Bu脜聜gari脛聶 w d脛聟脜录eniu do integracji ze stref脛聟 Schengen. Przep脜聜yw towar脙鲁w, jak i os脙鲁b spoza Unii Europejskiej nie mo脜录e by脛聡 zagro脜录eniem bezpiecze脜聞stwa gospodarczego i militarnego kraj脙鲁w, tak jak ma to miejsce w Europie Wschodniej. Dlatego zewn脛聶trzne granice Unii Europejskiej musz脛聟 by脛聡 szczelne, aby wewn脛聟trz Unii nie robi脛聡 sztucznych podzia脜聜脙鲁w.

脗聽脗聽Birgit Sippel (S&D). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident! Leider haben die letzten Jahre gezeigt: Mitgliedstaaten benutzten Debatten zum Schengen-Raum als innenpolitischen Spielball. Dabei ist das grenzfreie Reisen wichtig f脙录r den Binnenmarkt und f脙录r immer mehr B脙录rgerinnen und B脙录rger selbstverst脙陇ndlicher Alltag.

F脙录r Rum脙陇nien und Bulgarien ist der grenz脙录berschreitende Alltag jedoch nicht selbstverst脙陇ndlich. Immer wieder wurde ihnen unter fadenscheinigen Begr脙录ndungen der umfassende Beitritt zum Schengen-Raum verwehrt. Nachdem die Kommission vor 13脗聽Jahren ihr gr脙录nes Licht gegeben hatte, scheinen nun endlich Blockaden einzelner Mitgliedstaaten zu enden. Ich erwarte jetzt, dass der Rat den vollen Beitritt beider L脙陇nder unmittelbar beschlie脙聼t, ohne weitere Verz脙露gerungen. Doch zugleich m脙录ssen wir das grenzfreie Europa sch脙录tzen, insbesondere durch die Aufhebung der zahllosen Binnengrenzkontrollen. Denn der Schengen-Raum ist wichtig f脙录r Wirtschaft und Arbeitspl脙陇tze, aber insbesondere f脙录r die Menschen und f脙录r unseren europ脙陇ischen Zusammenhalt.

脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marieke Ehlers (PfE). 芒聙聯 Voorzitter, we staan aan de vooravond van de volledige toetreding van Bulgarije en Roemeni脙芦 tot het Schengengebied. De Schengenketting wordt daardoor nog iets langer, maar zoals we weten, is een ketting zo sterk als de zwakste schakel.

Recent heeft de linkse regering in Spanje ervoor gekozen om de immigratieregels te versoepelen. Zelfs afgewezen asielzoekers, die dus illegaal in Spanje verblijven, komen in aanmerking voor een verblijfsvergunning.

Dankzij het vrije verkeer binnen Schengen kunnen deze gelukszoekers, eenmaal in het bezit van een verblijfsvergunning, ongehinderd naar Nederland doorreizen. En hoewel we ieder jaar wel heel blij zijn met de komst van Sinterklaas en zijn Pieten uit Spanje, keren zij tenminste wel weer terug. Illegale migranten doen dat niet, want ons terugkeerbeleid faalt keer op keer.

Schengen is simpelweg niet bestand tegen de enorme toestroom van migranten. Zolang we de buitengrenzen niet goed kunnen beschermen, is het onze plicht de binnengrenzen te controleren.

脗聽脗聽脜聻erban Dimitrie Sturdza (ECR). 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, doamn脛聝 comisar脛聝, dragi colegi, este binevenit 脠聶i necesar s脛聝 ne amintim c脛聝 intrarea Rom脙垄niei 脙庐n spa脠聸iul Schengen are loc dup脛聝 nu mai pu脠聸in de 13 ani de umilin脠聸e 脠聶i am脙垄n脛聝ri nejustificate. Intrarea noastr脛聝 脙庐n Schengen nu este o favoare, ci un drept ce ne-a fost prea mult timp refuzat. Aceast脛聝 脙庐nf脛聝ptuire vine sub presiunea unei frustr脛聝ri na脠聸ionale de propor脠聸ii 脠聶i a unor calcule politice meschine, nu din respect pentru rom脙垄ni sau pentru regulile europene.

Intr脛聝m 脙庐n Schengen, dar cu condi脠聸ionalit脛聝脠聸i absurde. Controalele la grani脠聸ele dintre Rom脙垄nia 脠聶i Bulgaria 脠聶i dintre Rom脙垄nia 脠聶i Ungaria vor fi men脠聸inute 脙庐nc脛聝 脠聶ase luni, 脙庐n timp ce nou脛聝 state din spa脠聸iul Schengen au reintrodus controalele interne la frontierele lor. Cum vom salva dreptul la liber脛聝 circula脠聸ie? Spa脠聸iul Schengen trebuie reformat, dar nu prin excludere sau discriminare, ci prin solidaritate 脠聶i respect. Rom脙垄nia 脠聶i Bulgaria au demonstrat deja c脛聝 sunt parte a solu脠聸iei 脠聶i nu a problemei.

脗聽脗聽Vasile D脙庐ncu (S&D). 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, doamn脛聝 comisar脛聝, aderarea la spa脠聸iul Schengen este o obliga脠聸ie a comunit脛聝脠聸ii europene pentru Rom脙垄nia, iar accesul deplin la spa脠聸iul Schengen este o necesitate urgent脛聝 pentru 脙庐nt脛聝rirea unit脛聝脠聸ii 脠聶i a securit脛聝脠聸ii Uniunii Europene. At脙垄t Rom脙垄nia, c脙垄t 脠聶i Bulgaria, s-a spus aici de at脙垄tea ori, 脙庐ndeplinesc de 20 de ani toate criteriile tehnice necesare ader脛聝rii, iar aderarea la spa脠聸iul Schengen este un semnal puternic al solidarit脛聝脠聸ii europene 脠聶i ar confirma c脛聝 respectarea criteriilor 脠聶i a angajamentelor asumate este o valoare respectat脛聝 脠聶i de institu脠聸iile europene. Nu vrem un cadou 芒聙聯 vrem ceea ce suntem obliga脠聸i s脛聝 avem 脠聶i, mai mult, suntem 脙庐ndrept脛聝脠聸i脠聸i s脛聝 ob脠聸inem.

Am avertizat de la aceast脛聝 tribun脛聝 c脛聝 acest lucru va afecta electoral Rom脙垄nia. C脛聝lin Georgescu, candidatul populist care a c脙垄脠聶tigat primul tur al alegerilor preziden脠聸iale 脙庐n Rom脙垄nia, spunea despre Schengen: 芒聙聻Europa este o temni脠聸脛聝. M脛聝 bucur c脛聝 nu am intrat.芒聙聺 Vrem pe 12 decembrie ca Uniunea European脛聝 s脛聝 ac脠聸ioneze 脙庐n mod just 脠聶i decisiv. Doamn脛聝 comisar脛聝, v脛聝 mul脠聸umim foarte mult pentru lupta dumneavoastr脛聝 de c脙垄脠聸iva ani, pentru dreptate 脠聶i pentru libera circula脠聸ie.

脗聽脗聽Ant脙鲁nio T脙垄nger Corr脙陋a (PfE). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, Senhora Comiss脙隆ria, 脙漏 da mais elementar justi脙搂a o que hoje se tem dito aqui em rela脙搂脙拢o 脙聽 ades脙拢o a Schengen da Bulg脙隆ria e da Rom脙漏nia, ambos membros da Uni脙拢o Europeia desde 2007, membros da NATO, parceiros de confian脙搂a. E que menos sentido faz ainda quando n脙鲁s compararmos com o caso da Alb脙垄nia e do Kosovo, que usufruem da isen脙搂脙拢o de vistos desde 2010 e 2024. E querem comparar a Bulg脙隆ria e a Rom脙漏nia com esses pa脙颅ses? N脙拢o, meus amigos.

Nas m脙拢os da Rom脙漏nia e da Bulg脙隆ria, as fronteiras externas da Uni脙拢o ficar脙拢o mais fortes, mais resistentes, porque o que 脙漏 importante 脙漏 que Schengen funcione. E estes dois pa脙颅ses est脙拢o dispostos a que Schengen funcione, o que n脙拢o acontece em muitos pa脙颅ses, como no meu pa脙颅s, por exemplo, onde assistimos a uma imigra脙搂脙拢o sem quaisquer regras, sem qualquer respeito pelo pa脙颅s e pelos seus cidad脙拢os. Parab脙漏ns 脙聽 Bulg脙隆ria. Parab脙漏ns 脙聽 Rom脙漏nia. Sejam bem-vindos!

脗聽脗聽Lukas Mandl (PPE). 芒聙聯 Herr Pr脙陇sident, liebe Kolleginnen und Kollegen! Ich habe immer und immer wieder in jedem einschl脙陇gigen Interview gesagt: Ich werde der Erste sein, der feiert, wenn endlich Bulgarien und Rum脙陇nien in den Schengen-Raum kommen k脙露nnen, wenn der Schengen-Raum erweitert werden kann. Und das ist jetzt脗聽芒聙聯 in greifbarer N脙陇he脗聽芒聙聯 beinahe schon der Fall, weil die Kriterien erf脙录llt sind. Es war nie so, dass jemand auf Dauer Bulgarien und Rum脙陇nien aus Schengen fernhalten wollte. Vielmehr war es so, dass es die Zahlen an illegalen Grenz脙录bertritten eben nicht erlaubt haben, dass das fr脙录her geschehen konnte.

Es waren nicht Bulgarien und Rum脙陇nien, die verantwortlich daf脙录r waren, dass die Zahlen schlecht waren. Es war die europ脙陇ische Ebene gemeinsam, die sich anstrengen musste, um die Zahlen zu reduzieren. Gemeinsam mit der europ脙陇ischen Ebene konnten die Zahlen reduziert werden. Innenminister Gerhard Karner hat es in 脙聳sterreich m脙露glich gemacht, ma脙聼geblich die Zahlen an der 脙露sterreichischen Ostgrenze zu reduzieren. Generationen vor uns haben das vereinte Europa aufgebaut f脙录r offene Grenzen. Und wir k脙露nnen jetzt verantwortungsvoll diesen Schritt machen. Es gibt da und dort noch Binnengrenzkontrollen, wo sie n脙露tig sind. Aber die Au脙聼engrenzen zu sch脙录tzen, das ist die gemeinsame Aufgabe.

脗聽脗聽Sun脛聧ana Glavak (PPE). 芒聙聯 Po脜隆tovani predsjedavaju脛聡i, po脜隆tovana povjerenice, kolegice i kolege, puno pristupanje Bugarske i Rumunjske schengenskom prostoru pitanje je pravednosti i od strate脜隆ke je va脜戮nosti za Europsku uniju. Jo脜隆 2011. ispunjeni su tehni脛聧ki uvjeti. Va脜隆 je put bio dug, pun izazova, pa i onih politi脛聧kih.

Kao Hrvatica, dobro razumijem 脜隆to 脛聡e to zna脛聧iti za va脜隆e gra脛聭ane. 脛聦lanstvo u Schengenu ja脛聧a i sigurnosni okvir Europske unije, bez obzira na sve ove skepse koje dolaze s jedne ili druge, desno-lijeve ili desno-desne strane, rekla bih. Unato脛聧 izazovima, Schengen poti脛聧e sigurnosnu suradnju i ja脛聧anje zajedni脛聧kih mehanizama. Imperativ je da tim pitanjima pristupimo osiguravaju脛聡i da sigurnosne mjere ne naru脜隆avaju slobode koje pru脜戮a Schengen.

Za Bugarsku i Rumunjsku ulazak u Schengen zna脛聧i, u punom smislu, europsku pripadnost i jednakost. Na kraju, ja sam prije dvije godine do脛聧ekala Novu godinu di脜戮u脛聡i rampu koja vi脜隆e nikada nije postojala na mojoj granici. To 脜戮elim i vama. 脛聦estitam.

脗聽脗聽Adina V脛聝lean (PPE). 芒聙聯 Domnule pre脠聶edinte, doamn脛聝 comisar脛聝, doamnelor 脠聶i domnilor, noua mantr脛聝 european脛聝 este competitivitatea economic脛聝, dar competitivitatea nu poate exista f脛聝r脛聝 o pia脠聸脛聝 intern脛聝 complet脛聝 脠聶i func脠聸ional脛聝. Spa脠聸iul Schengen este esen脠聸ial pentru o economie puternic脛聝, f脛聝r脛聝 bariere, iar Rom脙垄nia 脠聶i Bulgaria trebuie s脛聝 fac脛聝 parte din el. Pia脠聸a intern脛聝 nu se poate opri la frontiere. Studiile arat脛聝 c脛聝 fiecare grani脠聸脛聝 genereaz脛聝 pre脠聸uri mai mari la m脛聝rfuri, o pia脠聸脛聝 intern脛聝 fragmentat脛聝 pentru transporturi 脠聶i emisii de carbon inutile.

O Europ脛聝 cu Rom脙垄nia 脠聶i Bulgaria 脙庐n Schengen 脙庐nseamn脛聝 mai mult脛聝 prosperitate nu doar pentru cet脛聝脠聸enii acestor 脠聸脛聝ri, ci pentru to脠聸i cet脛聝脠聸enii europeni. Mul脠聸umesc Comisiei Europene 脠聶i 脙庐n special comisarei Ylva Johansson pentru eforturile constante depuse, eforturi care sper c脛聝 脙庐n cele din urm脛聝 vor fi r脛聝spl脛聝tite cu o decizie pozitiv脛聝 la reuniunea din decembrie.

We will toast for you when opening the champagne.

脗聽脗聽Juan Fernando L脙鲁pez Aguilar (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤ora comisaria, este Parlamento Europeo se ha pronunciado insistentemente, a lo largo de a脙卤os, a favor de que Bulgaria y Ruman脙颅a finalmente entrasen en el espacio Schengen. Si lo hemos conseguido despu脙漏s de haber superado las fronteras mar脙颅timas y a脙漏reas, ahora con el compromiso del levantamiento de las fronteras interiores, tambi脙漏n terrestres, en buena medida es por la insistencia de este Parlamento Europeo 芒聙聰y por su trabajo, comisaria Johansson, que le reconocemos y agradecemos芒聙聰.

Este no es el fin de la historia, porque entrar en Schengen supone, es verdad, disfrutar finalmente, en igualdad, de un derecho fundamental muy preciado de la ciudadan脙颅a europea, como es la libre circulaci脙鲁n, pero tambi脙漏n el cumplimiento de un derecho europeo legislado que aprobamos la legislatura anterior, el C脙鲁digo de Fronteras Schengen. Este hace que el restablecimiento de fronteras interiores sea un 脙潞ltimo recurso sujeto al control de la Comisi脙鲁n y, en su caso, a la revisi脙鲁n del Tribunal de Justicia de que las razones sean de orden p脙潞blico o de seguridad interior, y estrictamente acotadas en el tiempo, y, por tanto, con compromiso del restablecimiento de la libre circulaci脙鲁n tan pronto como sea posible.

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, It脙陇valta on luopumassa pitk脙陇aikaisesta linjastaan vastustaa Bulgarian ja Romanian t脙陇ysimittaista liittymist脙陇 vapaan liikkuvuuden Schengen-alueeseen. T脙陇m脙陇n my脙露t脙陇 rajatarkastukset ovat poistumassa nyt kyseisten maiden maarajoilta.

Hyv脙陇t kollegat, vapaa liikkuvuus on hyv脙陇 ja kannatettava asia, mutta emme saa olla sinisilm脙陇isi脙陇. J脙陇senvaltioilla on oltava oikeus ja keinot puolustautua kohtaamiltaan uhilta, kuten laittomalta maahanmuutolta, Ven脙陇j脙陇n vakoilulta ja j脙陇rjest脙陇ytyneelt脙陇 rikollisuudelta. Juuri t脙陇st脙陇 syyst脙陇 Saksa ja monet muut maat ovat ottaneet sis脙陇rajatarkastukset k脙陇ytt脙露脙露n takaisin.

Rajatarkastukset eiv脙陇t ole huono asia, p脙陇invastoin niill脙陇 edistet脙陇脙陇n kaikkien EU-maiden turvallisuutta. Jos joku pit脙陇脙陇 passin n脙陇ytt脙陇mist脙陇 rajaa ylitt脙陇ess脙陇脙陇n vaivalloisena, on hyv脙陇 kysy脙陇: kenell脙陇 oikeasti on syyt脙陇 huoleen, tavallisella kansalaisella vai rikollisilla ja laittomasti maassa olevilla?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI). 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, our lands where once the borders stood, now flows the breath of brotherhood. No sentry halls, no gate delays, the open roads greet sunlit days. Through valleys deep or hills that climb, a fearless union breaks through times. In market hum and hearts alight, the ties of Schengen bind the night. Bulgaria's plains, Romania's streams, now wave into our shared dreams. From east to west, from sea to sea, the pulse of Europe beats so free. What walls we raised, our hearts unbind. Through Schengen doors we seek, we find no greater gift, no finer art, than Europe's soul in every heart. So travel on, let journeys weave, the thoughts of borders we shall leave. For here in freedom's vast embrace, the union spirit finds its place.

脗聽脗聽Matej Tonin (PPE). 芒聙聯 Gospa predsednica. Schengen je bilo nekaj najlep脜隆ega, kar se je zgodilo moji generaciji. Jaz se 脜隆e spominjam, kako smo ure in ure 脛聧akali na mejnih prehodih in, hvala bogu, je Slovenija tista dr脜戮ava, ki je danes obdana z vsemi schengenski dr脜戮avami. Ampak glej ga, zlomka, kljub temu, da naj bi bil Schengen okrog in okrog Slovenije, imamo vsaj na treh mejah ponovno vzpostavljene mejne kontrole.

In prav Schengen je bil tista najbolj otipljiva stvar, tista stvar, ki so jo ljudje najbolj 脛聧utili kot enega izmed pozitivnih sadov na脜隆e skupne Evropske unije. In prav ta sad je ponovno ogro脜戮en. In veste zakaj? Zaradi slabega upravljanja migracij. Ker se slabo upravlja z migracijami, so se ponovno vzpostavile mejne kontrole.

Zato pri脛聧akujem od Komisije, ki bo jutri potrjena, da vzpostavi red in da se ponovno vzpostavi Schengen, kakr脜隆nega si 脜戮elimo. In seveda Romunija in Bolgarija si zaslu脜戮ita mesto v Schengnu.

脗聽脗聽Luis-Vicen脠聸iu Lazarus (NI). 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, voiam s脛聝 v脛聝 脙庐ntreb dac脛聝 脠聶ti脠聸i cumva cam c脙垄脠聸i bani a pierdut Rom脙垄nia 脙庐n fiecare an prin neaccederea 脙庐n Schengen. V脛聝 spun eu: 10 miliarde de euro. Dac脛聝 discut脛聝m doar de 13 ani, de脠聶i Rom脙垄nia era preg脛聝tit脛聝 脙庐nainte de 13 ani, discut脛聝m de cel pu脠聸in 130 de miliarde, poate 150 de miliarde de euro. Cine va da Rom脙垄niei ace脠聶ti bani care au fost pierdu脠聸i? B脛聝nuiesc c脛聝 nimeni, nu? Este pur 脠聶i simplu o pierdere. Dar acum aud c脛聝, iat脛聝, nu este de fapt o accedere la 1 ianuarie, ci va trebui s脛聝 mai a脠聶tept脛聝m 6 luni 脠聶i c脛聝, pe de alt脛聝 parte, alte nou脛聝 state au introdus controale. Suntem noi cumva de vin脛聝? Nu. 脠聵i ce o s脛聝 facem, s脛聝 mai pierdem 脠聶i al脠聸i bani?

A脠聶 recomanda Comisiei, poate, ca 脙庐n viitor s脛聝 aib脛聝 脙庐n vedere ca atunci c脙垄nd cineva arunc脛聝 un veto pe mas脛聝 脠聶i nu mai prime脠聶te o 脠聸ar脛聝 脙庐n Schengen sau pentru cu totul alte motive s脛聝 fie penalizat. A脠聶adar, propun ca Austria, de exemplu, s脛聝 ne dea vreo 50 de miliarde de euro pentru c脛聝 s-a opus cu veto intr脛聝rii Rom脙垄niei 脠聶i a Bulgariei 脙庐n Schengen.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Daniel Buda (PPE). 芒聙聯 Doamn脛聝 pre脠聶edint脛聝, doamnelor 脠聶i domnilor colegi, doamn脛聝 comisar脛聝, Rom脙垄nia 脠聶i Bulgaria au fost furnizori de securitate 脠聶i stabilitate la grani脠聸a Uniunii Europene de peste 11 ani. Dup脛聝 mul脠聸i ani de a脠聶teptare, iat脛聝 c脛聝, 脙庐n sf脙垄r脠聶it, acestea vor adera 脙庐n ianuarie 2025 la spa脠聸iul Schengen 芒聙聯 un moment istoric pentru ambele 脠聸脛聝ri, dar 脠聶i pentru 脙庐ntreaga Uniune European脛聝. Drumul p脙垄n脛聝 aici a fost lung, am avut perioade de incertitudine, de descurajare, care au alimentat, din p脛聝cate, un curent antieuropean printre cet脛聝脠聸enii no脠聶tri, dar iat脛聝 c脛聝 ast脛聝zi putem spune c脛聝 am reu脠聶it.

Acest succes nu este doar o realizare pentru Rom脙垄nia, ci 脠聶i un pas semnificativ pentru 脙庐ntreaga Uniune European脛聝, consolid脙垄ndu-ne unitatea 脠聶i valorile fundamentale. 脙聨ntr-o perioad脛聝 脙庐n care curentele extremiste se r脛聝sp脙垄ndesc tot mai mult, aderarea noastr脛聝 la spa脠聸iul Schengen transmite un mesaj puternic despre angajamentul nostru comun fa脠聸脛聝 de democra脠聸ie, stabilitate 脠聶i cooperare. Cet脛聝脠聸enii Rom脙垄niei 脠聶i ai Bulgariei vor putea c脛聝l脛聝tori liber, iar beneficiile apartenen脠聸ei la Uniunea European脛聝 vor fi resim脠聸ite cu adev脛聝rat, de asemenea, 脠聶i de mediul economic 脠聶i de afaceri 脠聶i noi cu siguran脠聸脛聝 vom avea o Uniune European脛聝 mai puternic脛聝.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(Koniec zg脜聜osze脜聞 z sali)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ylva Johansson, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, thank you for putting this debate on the agenda. Thank you for reminding us again that Bulgaria and Romania belong in Schengen, fully and unreservedly. We are now very close to achieving that goal, and I am very happy, on a personal note, that one of my final debates in this House is dedicated to fully welcoming Bulgaria and Romania, because this has been an important goal of mine during my mandate. And I know that today I am among friends. The European Parliament has always been a strong supporter of Schengen membership for Bulgaria and Romania.

Soon it will be goodbye from me, but I am glad that the much too long waiting will soon be ended. We will be able to say 'welcome' to the citizens of Bulgaria and Romania to fully benefit from the Schengen freedoms. It is a big win for the whole of Schengen. It is a big win for all EU citizens. It is a big win for our internal market, for our businesses, for our economy 芒聙聯 finally!

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Georgiana Teodorescu (ECR), 脙庐n scris. 芒聙聯 Sunt mai bine de 13 ani de c脙垄nd Rom脙垄nia 脙庐ndepline脠聶te toate condi脠聸iile pentru a fi 脙庐n spa脠聸iul Schengen!

13 ani de c脙垄nd toate am脙垄n脛聝rile 脠聶i refuzurile au avut la baz脛聝 doar calcule politice, de oportunitate economic脛聝, 脠聶i nicidecum motive de nelegalitate, de nerespectare a cerin脠聸elor!

13 ani de c脙垄nd Rom脙垄nia ajut脛聝 Europa (脠聶i nu numai) 脙庐n mod continuu 脠聶i dovede脠聶te c脛聝 este un partener de 脙庐ncredere, f脛聝r脛聝 a fi tratat脛聝 astfel!

13 ani de c脙垄nd cet脛聝脠聸enii no脠聶tri stau ore 脙庐n 脠聶ir, la cozi kilometrice, 脙庐n punctele de trecere a frontierei pentru a pleca sau a reveni 脙庐n 脠聸ar脛聝!

Suntem sau nu egali to脠聸i cet脛聝脠聸enii europeni? Sunt toate statele membre egale 脙庐n cadrul UE? Condi脠聸iile pentru aderarea la spa脠聸iul Schengen sunt acelea脠聶i pentru toat脛聝 lumea?脗聽Dac脛聝 da, atunci nu exist脛聝 niciun motiv pentru ca Rom脙垄nia s脛聝 nu fie admis脛聝 urgent 脙庐n Schengen 脠聶i s脛聝 se spele, astfel, ru脠聶inea 脠聶i umilin脠聸a celor 13 ani de refuzuri nejustificate!

脗聽脗聽Micha脜聜 Wawrykiewicz (PPE), na pi脜聸mie. 芒聙聯 Strefa Schengen to fundament Unii Europejskiej umo脜录liwiaj脛聟cy swobodne przemieszczanie si脛聶 os脙鲁b pomi脛聶dzy pa脜聞stwami cz脜聜onkowskimi bez kontroli na wewn脛聶trznych granicach. To nie tylko praktyczna korzy脜聸脛聡, ale tak脜录e symbol to脜录samo脜聸ci UE, kt脙鲁ry sprzyja integracji gospodarczej i spo脜聜ecznej.

Swoboda przemieszczania si脛聶 nie powinna by脛聡 ograniczana bez uzasadnionych przyczyn. Dowolne interpretowanie tej zasady podwa脜录a fundamenty Unii i os脜聜abia zaufanie do jej instytucji. Kontrole graniczne wewn脛聟trz Schengen musz脛聟 pozosta脛聡 wyj脛聟tkiem, nie regu脜聜脛聟.

Bu脜聜garia i Rumunia dowiod脜聜y swojego zaanga脜录owania w zabezpieczanie zewn脛聶trznych granic Unii, spe脜聜niaj脛聟c wszystkie kryteria niezb脛聶dne do pe脜聜nego przyst脛聟pienia do Strefy Schengen. Rozszerzenie Schengen o te kraje nie tylko wzmocni stabilno脜聸脛聡 i bezpiecze脜聞stwo Unii w regionie po脜聜udniowo-wschodniej Europy, ale tak脜录e wy脜聸le jasny sygna脜聜, 脜录e inkluzywno脜聸脛聡 i solidarno脜聸脛聡 s脛聟 podstawowymi warto脜聸ciami UE.

Wzywam do pilnego podj脛聶cia decyzji o zniesieniu kontroli na wewn脛聶trznych granicach l脛聟dowych z Bu脜聜gari脛聟 i Rumuni脛聟 dla dobra nas wszystkich.

脗聽脗聽Iuliu Winkler (PPE), 脙庐n scris. 芒聙聯 Dup脛聝 13 ani de am脙垄n脛聝ri nejustificate, s脛聝pt脛聝m脙垄na trecut脛聝, la Budapesta, s-a luat o decizie important脛聝 pentru intrarea Rom脙垄niei 脠聶i Bulgariei 脙庐n spa脠聸iul Schengen 脠聶i cu frontierele terestre de la 1 ianuarie 2025.

脙聨n ace脠聶ti ultimi ani, pentru europarlamentarii UDMR, aderarea 脠聸脛聝rii noastre la Schengen a fost o prioritate, iar decizia luat脛聝 sub pre脠聶edin脠聸ia ungar脛聝 a UE este salutar脛聝 脠聶i 脙庐ncununeaz脛聝 脠聶i eforturile 脠聶i munca noastr脛聝 din PE. Sper c脛聝 脙庐n cele din urm脛聝 se va face dreptate, iar cet脛聝脠聸enii no脠聶tri vor putea c脛聝l脛聝tori cu adev脛聝rat liber 脙庐n UE f脛聝r脛聝 controale la frontierele terestre. Pe l脙垄ng脛聝 circula脠聸ia liber脛聝 a cet脛聝脠聸enilor, aderarea la spa脠聸iul Schengen aduce 脠聶i libera circula脠聸ie a m脛聝rfurilor. Acesta este un obiectiv economic extrem de important pentru economia noastr脛聝, pentru antreprenorii no脠聶tri, pentru produc脛聝torii no脠聶tri agricoli 脠聶i nu 脙庐n cele din urm脛聝, pentru comunit脛聝脠聸ile din Banat 脠聶i Partium, unde dimensiunea economic脛聝 脠聶i comercial脛聝 a cooper脛聝rii transfrontaliere poate s脛聝 primeasc脛聝 un nou impuls.

Aderarea la spa脠聸iul Schengen este un obiectiv extrem de important pentru comunitatea noastr脛聝, la fel cum este pentru 脙庐ntreaga 脠聸ar脛聝. Am speran脠聸a c脛聝 am dep脛聝脠聶it piedicile politicianiste 脠聶i meschine 脠聶i c脛聝 vom reu脠聶i imediat s脛聝 elimin脛聝m dublul standard care ne 脙庐mpov脛聝reaz脛聝 din punct de vedere al dezvolt脛聝rii economice.

脗聽

16. Prison conditions in the EU (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Przewodnicz脛聟ca. 芒聙聯 Kolejnym punktem porz脛聟dku dziennego jest o脜聸wiadczenie Komisji w sprawie warunk脙鲁w w wi脛聶zieniach w UE. (2024/2944(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Helena Dalli, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, honourable Members, I thank this House for putting the situation of prisons in the EU on the agenda of this plenary. As you know, whereas detention issues are mainly a competence and responsibility of the Member States, the Charter of Fundamental Rights requires that, within the scope of EU law, detention conditions do not lead to violations of fundamental rights. Moreover, all EU Member States have committed themselves to respect the standards drafted by the Council of Europe on this matter, such as the 2006 European Prison Rules.

At EU level, the Commission adopted in 2022 a recommendation on the procedural rights of suspects and accused in pre-trial detention and on material detention conditions. The recommendation describes minimum standards, a number of which are already present in different international instruments, such as using pre-trial detention as a measure of last resort and introducing periodic reviews where its use is justified. The recommendation also provides minimum standards for cell sizes, outdoor time, nutrition and healthcare conditions, as well as initiatives with a view to regeneration and social rehabilitation.

Given the vast number of international standards in this area, it is useful to systematise the relevant existing international standards which are most relevant for judicial cooperation between Member States. This aims to facilitate the work of judicial authorities, who have to take rapid decisions in the context of the European arrest warrant, and to lead to a more uniform approach in the EU.

In order to monitor the follow芒聙聭up given to the recommendation, the Commission has developed a questionnaire in close cooperation with experts in the field of detention. Member States were expected to inform the Commission on their follow芒聙聭up to this recommendation by 1脗聽October, and we are still waiting for the replies of some of them. Once all Member States have submitted their replies, the Commission will assess the measures taken at national level and submit a report in the course of next year.

The issue of detention was also one of the priorities of the Belgian Presidency of the Council, and during the Justice Council in June, conclusions on small芒聙聭scale detention were approved. These conclusions focus on the advantages of so-called small scale detention facilities in terms of resocialisation and rehabilitation, and call upon Member States and the Commission to take further action to promote this concept.

The Commission also continues to work on the EU framework decisions that are relevant in the field of detention, such as the framework decisions on the transfer of prisoners on probation and alternative sanctions, and on the European Supervision Order. It has turned out that the latter two framework decisions are scarcely used in practice. To better understand the functioning of these instruments, and to examine whether and how best these pre芒聙聭Lisbon instruments could be modernised, a study has been launched this year and it should be completed by autumn of next year.

Organisations that receive operating grants from the Commission, such as EuroPris and the Confederation of European Probation, have created specific expert groups on the framework decisions relating to detention, which meet on a yearly basis. Numerous action grants funded by the Commission have also been dedicated to awareness芒聙聭raising and support in the practical application of the EU instruments which I have just mentioned.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Salvatore De Meo, a nome del gruppo PPE. 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signora Commissaria, i dati frammentati sulle condizioni carcerarie e quelli sul sovraffollamento impongono da subito l'adozione di interventi coordinati e soluzioni condivise a livello europeo per affrontare questa emergenza.

Mi soffermo sul dato del carcere sovraffollato, che peggiora le condizioni di vita dei detenuti e la qualit脙聽 di servizio degli operatori, facendo aumentare le tensioni e compromettendo l'efficacia dei programmi di riabilitazione e di reinserimento. A ci脙虏 si aggiunga anche il delicato tema dei suicidi sia tra i detenuti che tra gli addetti al controllo.

Voglio segnalare l'esperienza del mio partito, Forza Italia, che durante l'estate, su iniziativa del ministro Tajani, ha visitato le carceri italiane e avanzato alcune proposte, alcune delle quali sono al vaglio anche della Commissione europea per migliorare le condizioni carcerarie, tra cui il trasferimento dei detenuti tossicodipendenti nelle comunit脙聽 di recupero e la riduzione dei tempi di attesa per il carcere preventivo, considerando che oltre il 50% dei detenuti molto spesso viene assolto dopo o durante il giudizio.

Queste sono solo alcune delle misure su cui ragionare per definire subito una strategia europea necessaria, non solo a ridurre il sovraffollamento, ma a restituire umanit脙聽 al sistema penitenziario, trasformandolo da un semplice strumento punitivo a mezzo per la riabilitazione. Perch脙漏, cari colleghi, non dimentichiamo che la reclusione non deve essere una privazione della libert脙聽 ma soprattutto non deve essere una privazione della dignit脙聽.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sandro Ruotolo, a nome del gruppo S&D. 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signora Commissaria, il grado di civilt脙聽 di un Paese si pu脙虏 giudicare entrando nelle sue carceri.

A livello di Unione europea, secondo Eurostat, nel 2022 c'era un detenuto ogni 924 abitanti e undici Stati membri su 27 soffrivano di sovraffollamento, che 脙篓 la maggiore causa di suicidio, su cui ancora non esistono statistiche a livello dell'Unione.

In Italia dall'inizio del 2024 ad oggi ci sono stati 81 suicidi, uno ogni quattro giorni. Sia chiaro: siamo contro l'impunit脙聽 ma certamente ci piacerebbe vedere pagare il conto con la giustizia anche ai colletti bianchi, cosa che succede raramente.

Ci dobbiamo per脙虏 chiedere come prevenire e come recuperare: non 脙篓 accettabile, per esempio, che nel carcere di Napoli oggi ci siano 2.103 detenuti invece dei 1.400 previsti.

Vi sembra civile che detenuti e agenti di custodia vivano in quelle condizioni? 脙聢 troppo facile chiudere a chiave la cella e dimenticarci di loro.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Matthieu Valet, au nom du groupe PfE. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, une fois n'est pas coutume, n'en d脙漏plaise aux gauchos, la prison, ce n'est pas le Club Med. Son premier r脙麓le, en France comme en Europe, n'est pas de r脙漏ins脙漏rer le d脙漏tenu, mais d'abord et avant tout de prot脙漏ger la soci脙漏t脙漏. En effet, tant que ces d脙漏tenus sont dedans, ils ne font pas de victimes dehors. Donc construisez des prisons, vous sauverez des victimes.

N'en d脙漏plaise 脙聽 nos amis de l'Observatoire international des prisons, entre autres maux de notre syst脙篓me carc脙漏ral, il est 脙聽 bout de souffle, par manque de places de prisons dans tous les pays d'Europe. Pas besoin d'脙陋tre polytechnicien pour comprendre que de nouvelles places garantiront un encellulement individuel, des conditions de s脙漏curit脙漏 pour les agents p脙漏nitentiaires et, surtout, l'effectivit脙漏 de la peine.

Surtout, les juges europ脙漏ens, au lieu de d脙漏fendre en permanence les d脙漏tenus, devraient prot脙漏ger les agents p脙漏nitentiaires en r脙漏autorisant les fouilles syst脙漏matiques apr脙篓s les parloirs, qui sont de v脙漏ritables incubateurs 脙聽 objets prohib脙漏s, interdits et dangereux pour nos agents p脙漏nitentiaires.

En prison, les d脙漏tenus doivent aussi travailler pour se r脙漏ins脙漏rer, mais surtout pour indemniser les victimes.

Face 脙聽 l'urgence de la surpopulation carc脙漏rale, expulsion des d脙漏tenus de nationalit脙漏 脙漏trang脙篓re: un quart des d脙漏tenus en France脗聽芒聙聯 les prisons fran脙搂aises sont surpeupl脙漏es, vous le savez脗聽芒聙聯 sont de nationalit脙漏 脙漏trang脙篓re.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Michael McNamara, on behalf of the Renew Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, the Council of Europe's 2023 annual report on prison populations noted that Cyprus, Romania, France, Belgium, Hungary, Italy and Slovenia are all grappling with severe overcrowding, with the prison density of more than 105脗聽inmates per 100脗聽places available, with Ireland, Portugal, Finland and Denmark not far behind.

Cork prison, in the constituency that I represent, is already at 120脗聽% capacity, with 60 of its 355脗聽inmates sleeping on the floor. Of course, those levels of overcrowding make the prisons extremely dangerous 芒聙聯 not just for inmates, but also for those who work with those inmates. Yet we hear our government parties, as they campaign, talking about increasing minimum sentencing.

I mean, the question arises: where are you going to put these inmates? Who is going to be Barabbas? Who is going to be released instead? Instead of just talking about more prison spaces, which, yes, are needed, we also need to ensure that there are fewer people serving short-term sentences, and prisons don't become a place where we are housing people with addiction problems and mental health issues. Fundamental reform is needed of our prisons across Europe.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Saskia Bricmont, au nom du groupe Verts/ALE. 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, catastrophique, c'est l'脙漏tat de nos prisons en Europe: 100脗聽% ou plus de taux d'occupation dans douze pays sur 27, jusqu'脙聽 200脗聽% dans certaines prisons. Des personnes d脙漏tenues partagent des matelas dans des cellules surpeupl脙漏es et v脙漏tustes. En cons脙漏quence: probl脙篓mes d'hygi脙篓ne, de dignit脙漏, d'acc脙篓s aux soins, au droit et 脙聽 la r脙漏habilitation, pourtant indispensable pour pr脙漏venir la r脙漏cidive et la radicalisation.

Au manque de places, la r脙漏ponse de nos autorit脙漏s est g脙漏n脙漏ralement la construction de nouvelles prisons. Plus de places entra脙庐nent pourtant plus de d脙漏tenus. La m脙漏ga-prison de Haren en Belgique n'en est qu'un exemple flagrant parmi d'autres. La gr脙篓ve du personnel en t脙漏moigne: les conditions de d脙漏tention d脙漏plorables entra脙庐nent aussi des conditions de travail catastrophiques.

La question fondamentale est celle de la vision de la soci脙漏t脙漏 et du mod脙篓le p脙漏nal que nous souhaitons. Depuis cinq脗聽ans, je demande que l'Union europ脙漏enne adopte un cadre contraignant commun sur les conditions de d脙漏tention en Europe. Et c'est grave que nos gouvernements aient rejet脙漏 un tel cadre commun en脗聽2022: grave pour les droits fondamentaux des personnes d脙漏tenues et des travailleurs, grave pour la coop脙漏ration judiciaire en Europe, grave pour l'脙聣tat de droit, dont l'Union europ脙漏enne doit 脙陋tre la garante.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ilaria Salis, a nome del gruppo The Left. 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, Benito, Mussa, nome sconosciuto, Vincenzo, Federico, Giuseppe, Pasquale, nome sconosciuto, nome sconosciuto, Salvatore, John, nome sconosciuto, nome sconosciuto, Salvatore, Abdel Jalil, Atef, nome sconosciuto, nome sconosciuto, Luca, nome sconosciuto, nome sconosciuto, nome sconosciuto, Giuseppe, Lulsim, Alessandro Patrizio, nome sconosciuto, Fabiano, Fabrizio, Vincenzo, Fedi, Youssef, nome sconosciuto, Giuseppe, Majon, nome sconosciuto, Francesco, Al脙卢, nome sconosciuto, Giuseppe, Alin Vasili, nome sconosciuto.

Un minuto non 脙篓 sufficiente per nominare tutte le persone che nel 2024 si sono tolte la vita nelle carceri italiane.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Paulo Do Nascimento Cabral (PPE). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, Senhora Comiss脙隆ria, as condi脙搂脙碌es dos estabelecimentos prisionais s脙拢o tamb脙漏m uma preocupa脙搂脙拢o com a dignidade da condi脙搂脙拢o humana. Em Portugal, a realidade que o atual Governo encontrou foi metade dos reclusos em cadeias sobrelotadas, reduzidas 脙隆reas de alojamento por recluso e infraestruturas obsoletas e degradadas, acesso inadequado a cuidados de sa脙潞de e guardas prisionais abandonados.脗聽Rapidamente chegou a acordo com os guardas prisionais para uma progress脙拢o na carreira mais r脙隆pida, est脙隆 a acelerar a entrada de novos profissionais e a desenvolver experi脙陋ncias-piloto com recurso a novas tecnologias.

Mas destaco a cadeia de Ponta Delgada, na Regi脙拢o Aut脙鲁noma dos A脙搂ores, com uma taxa efetiva de ocupa脙搂脙拢o superior a 120脗聽%, com perfil de idade jovem, em condi脙搂脙碌es sub-humanas - e temos, por exemplo, 47脗聽pessoas no armaz脙漏m transformado em sala - e com a desloca脙搂脙拢o de centenas de reclusos para as cadeias de outras ilhas e continente portugu脙陋s, passando estes a cumprir uma dupla pena tamb脙漏m pelo isolamento, quer para os pr脙鲁prios, quer para as suas fam脙颅lias, dificultando o processo de reabilita脙搂脙拢o, que 脙漏 o objetivo 脙潞ltimo desta priva脙搂脙拢o de liberdade.

E isto tem de acabar. As promessas t脙陋m mais de duas d脙漏cadas e nada foi feito. Sa脙潞do por isto o atual Governo pelo compromisso com o in脙颅cio da constru脙搂脙拢o de uma nova cadeia j脙隆 em 2027.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Juan Fernando L脙鲁pez Aguilar (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤ora comisaria Dalli, durante la primera legislatura en la que tuve el honor de presidir la Comisi脙鲁n de Libertades Civiles, Justicia y Asuntos de Interior, presid脙颅 tambi脙漏n una misi脙鲁n del Parlamento Europeo de supervisi脙鲁n del sistema de prisiones europeo solo para constatar, de primera mano, las enormes variaciones entre los Estados miembros y algunas terribles deficiencias, incompatibles, para empezar, con el art脙颅culo 3 del Convenio Europeo de Derechos Humanos, que proh脙颅be la tortura y los tratos inhumanos y degradantes, y con el art脙颅culo 4 de la Carta de los Derechos Fundamentales de la Uni脙鲁n Europea, que dice lo mismo.

Y es por eso que, en esa legislatura, aprobamos normas procesales que garantizan medidas m脙颅nimas de convergencia europea en relaci脙鲁n no solo con el acusado, sino tambi脙漏n con las condiciones de detenci脙鲁n.

Pero el trabajo no estar脙隆 completo hasta que no cumplamos los objetivos de la Resoluci脙鲁n de este Parlamento Europeo sobre las condiciones de la prisi脙鲁n preventiva en la Uni脙鲁n Europea, para establecer no solamente un c脙鲁digo de duraci脙鲁n m脙颅nima y m脙隆xima de la prisi脙鲁n preventiva, sino tambi脙漏n de duraci脙鲁n media de la prisi脙鲁n preventiva y de garant脙颅as de dignidad que sean compatibles con la Carta de los Derechos Fundamentales de la Uni脙鲁n Europea para las personas detenidas y en prisi脙鲁n preventiva en la Uni脙鲁n Europea.

脗聽脗聽Juan Carlos Girauta Vidal (PfE). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, la condici脙鲁n preliminar que deber脙颅a garantizarse en cualquier prisi脙鲁n de la Uni脙鲁n Europea es que los funcionarios que trabajan en ellas est脙漏n seguros. A d脙颅a de hoy, en Europa, las c脙隆rceles tienen presos cada vez m脙隆s peligrosos.

Con demasiada frecuencia hemos de escuchar noticias de ataques a los trabajadores y de c脙鲁mo, muchas veces, las prisiones act脙潞an tambi脙漏n como centro de radicalizaci脙鲁n de los internos.

Por eso, es tan inaudito como intolerable que quienes ejercen claramente una profesi脙鲁n de riesgo 芒聙聰como los funcionarios de prisiones芒聙聰 no dispongan de los medios adecuados para su trabajo. Esto incluye su reconocimiento como agentes de la autoridad, que se les provea de todos los recursos materiales, humanos y jur脙颅dicos necesarios para salvaguardar su integridad f脙颅sica en el desempe脙卤o de sus funciones, as脙颅 como su dignidad en el trabajo. La seguridad de toda la sociedad comienza garantizando la de aquellos que vigilan a los que la perturban.

脗聽脗聽Friedrich P脙录rner (NI). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, Frau Kommissarin! Viele Jahre habe ich in Bayern als Gef脙陇ngnisarzt in einer Krankenabteilung gearbeitet. Die aktuellen Foltervorw脙录rfe gegen das bayerische Gef脙陇ngnis Augsburg芒聙聭Gablingen schockieren mich. Von unmenschlicher Unterbringung und Misshandlung ist hier die Rede.

In Deutschland sind die Bundesl脙陇nder f脙录r den Strafvollzug zust脙陇ndig. In Bayern scheint es hier ein massives Problem zu geben. Ja, es braucht mehr Transparenz in den Gef脙陇ngnissen. Bei Fixierungen oder Unterbringungen in besonders gesicherten Haftr脙陇umen m脙录ssen unbedingt bessere Kontrollen durch Gef脙陇ngnis脙陇rzte erfolgen. Auch k脙露nnte man f脙录r diese Gefangenen ein Antragsrecht, um einen Anwalt zu konsultieren, regeln. Strafvollzugsbeauftragte, die als neutrale und unabh脙陇ngige Ansprechpartner f脙录r die Justizverwaltungen zur Verf脙录gung stehen, k脙露nnen dem entgegensteuern. Ich erinnere ausdr脙录cklich an das Subsidiarit脙陇tsprinzip. Deutschland kann das ohne die EU regeln, und deshalb rufe ich nach Bayern: Machen Sie bitte Ihre Arbeit! Dann braucht es keine EU-Regelung.

脗聽脗聽Cecilia Strada (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signora Commissaria, nonostante le sentenze della Corte europea dei diritti dell'uomo, gli appelli delle ONG e le risoluzioni del Parlamento, il sovraffollamento delle carceri e il trattamento degradante dei detenuti rimangono problemi gravissimi diffusi in tutta l'Unione.

In Italia, il mio Paese, il 110% dei posti in carcere 脙篓 occupato ben oltre la capienza massima e i suicidi nel 2024 sono stati 81, quasi due alla settimana. Il sovraffollamento carcerario, lo sappiamo, pu脙虏 costituire, come gi脙聽 accaduto in diversi casi, una violazione del divieto di tortura sancito dalla CEDU e dalla Carta di Nizza e porta all'aumento della violenza e porta al peggioramento delle relazioni tra detenuti e tra detenuti e personale. Il sovraffollamento limita l'accesso alle cure, alla formazione professionale e ai necessari percorsi di reintegrazione.

Le soluzioni ci sarebbero: dobbiamo occuparci dell'applicazione di misure alternative al carcere per i reati minori; dobbiamo dedicare particolare attenzione ai progetti di reinserimento per giovani detenuti e minorenni, fornendo loro gli strumenti per non tornare in galera da adulti; dobbiamo necessariamente concordare standard europei per il rispetto dei diritti fondamentali dei detenuti e delle detenute. Facciamolo.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Julien Sanchez (PfE). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, ce d脙漏bat sur les conditions de d脙漏tention en Europe est int脙漏ressant. Alors que, dans de nombreux pays du monde, il y a encore une vision saine du bien et du mal, la prison y 脙漏tant une punition, un endroit aust脙篓re o脙鹿 l'on est admis parce qu'on s'est mal conduit et o脙鹿 on est donc trait脙漏 en cons脙漏quence comme un coupable, de nombreux pays europ脙漏ens ont, h脙漏las, plus de consid脙漏ration pour les coupables que pour les victimes.

En France, on peut narguer ses victimes depuis la prison. On se souvient, parmi d'autres, de l'organisation d'une sorte de jeu Koh-Lanta avec des d脙漏tenus faisant du karting dans la cour de la prison de Fresnes. En France, certains d脙漏tenus introduisent objets, armes et t脙漏l脙漏phones, et continuent 脙聽 organiser les trafics depuis la prison, sans oublier la radicalisation islamique.

Si la surpopulation carc脙漏rale est une r脙漏alit脙漏, elle a deux causes principales: les promesses non tenues des gouvernements successifs en mati脙篓re de cr脙漏ation de places de prison et l'immigration massive soutenue par l'Union europ脙漏enne, qui est un grand fournisseur de d脙漏linquants et criminels qui peuplent nos prisons.

Il est temps de r脙漏instaurer en France la double peine, les peines planchers, de stopper l'immigration massive et de renvoyer dans leur pays de mani脙篓re effective les personnes frapp脙漏es d'OQTF. De faire en sorte que nos prisons ne soient plus des foutoirs. D'aider les surveillants p脙漏nitentiaires, qui font un m脙漏tier difficile et sont totalement oubli脙漏s, en leur donnant enfin moyens et soutien. D'imposer la discipline et d'arr脙陋ter d'脙陋tre plus attentionn脙漏 envers les d脙漏tenus qu'envers les victimes.

脗聽脗聽Alessandra Moretti (S&D). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, carcere di Regina Coeli a Roma: 1.100 detenuti per 627 posti; carcere di Agrigento: il bidet viene usato sia per l'igiene che per pulire i piatti; San Vittore, Milano: il tasso di affollamento 脙篓 del 227%. In Italia sono pi脙鹿 di 80 i detenuti che si sono suicidati solo nel 2024.

Questi sono soltanto alcuni dati che raccontano le gravi violazioni dei diritti umani nelle carceri italiane. I detenuti, nella maggior parte dei casi, non hanno nemmeno lo spazio vitale minimo di tre metri quadrati a persona, stabilito dalla Convenzione europea dei diritti dell'uomo.

L'Unione europea definisce giustamente la superficie minima per garantire il benessere di un maiale in allevamento ma non ha norme altrettanto chiare per garantire condizioni dignitose ai detenuti. Cos脙卢 il carcere diventa una pentola a pressione in cui cresce la rabbia.

Serve porre fine a questa vergogna e garantire che il trattamento delle persone detenute non violi i principi fondamentali sui quali si fonda la nostra Unione.

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, Suomessa on yhdet Euroopan mukavimmista vankilaoloista. Ne ovat niin mukavat, ett脙陇 er脙陇s avovanki jopa luonnehti asiaa mediahaastattelussa n脙陇in: "Rautaa ei ole miss脙陇脙陇n, saa saunoa ja sy脙露d脙陇 kolme kertaa p脙陇iv脙陇ss脙陇 firman laskuun, se oli hienoa aikaa".

T脙陇st脙陇 huolimatta sek脙陇 Euroopan neuvoston ett脙陇 YK:n kidutuksenvastaiset komiteat ovat pit脙陇neet Suomea hampaissaan. Ne ovat kertoneet meille, ett脙陇 tutkintavankeja ei saisi s脙陇ilytt脙陇脙陇 edes lyhytaikaisesti poliisin tiloissa. Nyt jotain rajaa! S脙陇ilytett脙陇vi脙陇 tutkintavankeja ep脙陇ill脙陇脙陇n vakavista rikoksista, jolloin on teht脙陇v脙陇 kaikki sen eteen, ett脙陇 tutkinta saadaan suoritettua onnistuneesti ja syylliset vastuuseen. Jos tutkintavanki viett脙陇脙陇 muutaman p脙陇iv脙陇n poliisin tiloissa vailla yhteyksi脙陇 ulkomaailmaan, kyse ei ole kidutuksesta. Kyse on normaalista rikostutkinnasta.

脗聽脗聽Anthony Smith (The Left). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, oui, le syst脙篓me carc脙漏ral fonctionne mal, mais l'extr脙陋me droite s'obstine 脙聽 d脙漏fendre un mod脙篓le archa脙炉que et autoritaire.

N'en d脙漏plaise aux fachos, les faits vous donnent tort: en France, par exemple, le taux de r脙漏cidive des d脙漏tenus en sortie s脙篓che est de l'ordre de 65脗聽%, alors qu'il n'est que de 40脗聽% pour ceux qui b脙漏n脙漏ficient d'un accompagnement social, comme par exemple une fin de peine en milieu ouvert.

脗聽脗聽Lukas Sieper (NI). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, liebe Menschen Europas! In dieser Debatte 脙录ber die Haftbedingungen innerhalb der Europ脙陇ischen Union haben wir viel Verbesserungsbedarf im Hinblick auf unsere Gef脙陇ngnisse feststellen m脙录ssen. Ich m脙露chte aber vielmehr auf eine Aussage eingehen, die ein Kollege dort unten in der Mitte getroffen hat. Er sagte: Wir m脙录ssen die Menschen in die Gef脙陇ngnisse sperren, weil wir die Opfer sch脙录tzen wollen, aber keinesfalls die Insassen. Nat脙录rlich sperren wir Leute in Gef脙陇ngnisse, um die Opfer von potenziellen Straftaten zu sch脙录tzen, um diese Leute f脙录r das, was sie den Opfern ihrer Straftaten getan haben, zu bestrafen.

Aber solange wir in einem Rechtsstaat leben, sch脙录tzen wir immer auch die Gefangenen. Ein Staat, der nicht in der Lage ist, einen Menschen unter menschenw脙录rdigen Bedingungen einzusperren, ist nicht besser als die unz脙陇hligen Diktaturen, die wir zu unserer Schande auf unserem Kontinent bereits erleben m脙录ssen. Deswegen m脙露chte ich, auch wenn sie es an anderer Stelle nicht verdient haben, hier eine Lanze f脙录r die T脙陇ter brechen. Auch T脙陇ter haben eine menschenw脙录rdige Haft verdient.

脗聽脗聽Diego Solier (NI). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, 脗驴qu脙漏 puede ser m脙隆s peligroso que tratar diariamente con asesinos, violadores y maltratadores? Pues eso mismo pienso yo. 脗驴C脙鲁mo es posible que la profesi脙鲁n de funcionario de prisiones no est脙漏 catalogada como profesi脙鲁n de riesgo? 脗驴C脙鲁mo es posible que ni est脙漏n considerados como agentes de la autoridad en Espa脙卤a? Cada diecis脙漏is脗聽horas un funcionario de prisiones en Espa脙卤a es agredido, pero no es profesi脙鲁n de riesgo. Y estos trabajadores deben esperar hasta los sesenta y cinco脗聽a脙卤os para poder jubilarse con todos los derechos. Y, al igual que pasa con la polic脙颅a, dependiendo de d脙鲁nde ejerzan su trabajo, su remuneraci脙鲁n es diferente, por el mismo trabajo. 脗驴D脙鲁nde est脙隆 la igualdad? Y lo mejor de todo, que desde el Gobierno se legisla para proteger a los presos y desproteger a los funcionarios. Vamos, el mundo al rev脙漏s.

脗聽脗聽

(Koniec zg脜聜osze脜聞 z sali)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Helena Dalli, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam President, thank you for this debate. The Commission wants to underscore the need to improve the situation in prisons within the EU and 芒聙聯 since poor prison conditions risk violating fundamental rights of citizens and seriously affect the proper operation of judicial cooperation 芒聙聯 instruments such as the European arrest warrant. I recall that, as part of the political priorities of the next Commission, President von der Leyen announced work to strengthen the European arrest warrant and to step up cooperation between Eurojust and Europol.

The report that the Commission will publish in the course of 2025, assessing compliance with its recommendation on detention, will provide more transparency on the situation in the Member States. The Commission will also continue to develop synergies with the work done by the Council of Europe in this area, by providing funding to the European network of national bodies that monitor detention conditions in the Member States. In close cooperation with the Council of Europe, guidance and training for judicial professionals will also be developed in order to raise awareness of the recommendation on detention.

脗聽

脗聽脗聽Przewodnicz脛聟ca. 芒聙聯 Zamykam debat脛聶.
