脗聽脗聽Enik脜聭 Gy脜聭ri (PfE), 脙颅r脙隆sban. 芒聙聯 K脙漏t 脙漏ve 脙露t t脙隆mad脙隆st hajtott v脙漏gre egy k脙录lf脙露ldi 脙隆llampolg脙隆rs脙隆g脙潞 tagokb脙鲁l 脙隆ll脙鲁 csoport Magyarorsz脙隆gon. H脙隆tulr脙鲁l rontottak r脙隆 olyan magyar 脙漏s k脙录lf脙露ldi j脙隆r脙鲁kel脜聭kre, akiket 脜聭k egy fasiszta rendezv脙漏ny r脙漏sztvev脜聭inek v脙漏ltek. Magyarorsz脙隆gon ugyanis nincsenek fasiszta felvonul脙隆sok, t脙露rv脙漏ny tiltja ezeket, a rend脜聭rs脙漏g fell脙漏p ellen脙录k.

Az 脙隆ldozatokat k脙录l脙露nf脙漏le eszk脙露z脙露kkel, viper脙隆val 脙漏s gumikalap脙隆ccsal, brut脙隆lisan b脙隆ntalmazt脙隆k. A megt脙隆madott kilenc ember k脙露z脙录l n脙漏gyen s脙潞lyos, 脙露ten k脙露nny脜卤 s脙漏r脙录l脙漏seket szenvedtek.

Ilaria Salis, aki a v脙隆d szerint r脙漏szt vett a t脙隆mad脙隆sok kitervel脙漏s脙漏ben 脙漏s esetenk脙漏nt a v脙漏grehajt脙隆sukban is, az Eur脙鲁pai Parlamentben 脙录l. K脙漏rdezem, az 脙隆ldozatok hangj脙隆t ki hallja meg?

Salis az eur脙鲁pai parlamenti k脙漏pvisel脜聭s脙漏g脙漏vel j脙隆r脙鲁 mentelmi jog脙隆ra hivatkozva akarja meg脙潞szni az igazs脙隆gszolg脙隆ltat脙隆st. B脙颅zom benne, hogy az EP ebben nem lesz partner, 脙颅gy nem tud majd kib脙潞jni a felel脜聭ss脙漏grevon脙隆s al脙鲁l. Ne felejts脙漏k el: k脙露z脙露ns脙漏ges er脜聭szakos b脜卤ncselekm脙漏nnyel v脙隆dolj脙隆k, semmi politikai ind脙颅ttat脙隆s nincs az 脙录gyben.

A Patri脙鲁t脙隆k Eur脙鲁p脙隆脙漏rt k脙漏pvisel脜聭csoport mindent meg fog tenni annak 脙漏rdek脙漏ben, hogy ilyen vissza脙漏l脙漏s a mentelmi joggal ne t脙露rt脙漏nhessen a j脙露v脜聭ben. K脙露zt脙露rv脙漏nyes 脙漏s er脜聭szakos b脜卤n脙露z脜聭knek nincs hely脙录k ebben a H脙隆zban!

脗聽

17. Urgent need to tackle the gender pay gap (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Przewodnicz脛聟ca. 芒聙聯 Kolejnym punktem porz脛聟dku obrad jest o脜聸wiadczenie Komisji pt. 芒聙聻Pilna potrzeba zaradzenia zr脙鲁脜录nicowaniu wynagrodze脜聞 ze wzgl脛聶du na p脜聜e脛聡芒聙聺 (2024/2945(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Helena Dalli, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Madam脗聽President, honourable Members, two weeks ago we marked the European Equal Pay Day. This day quantifies the number of extra days women must work to earn men's take-home income. This year, women must shoulder 46脗聽extra days of work to match men's pay, which is about twice the number of vacation days the average European worker gets a year. During this Commission's mandate, we reduced the gender pay gap by the equivalent of five days, and I augur that during the upcoming mandate it will be shrunk further.

Yet, despite the gender pay gap's decrease, the drop I am reporting is, of course, modest. Women's gross hourly earnings remain, on average, 12.7脗聽% below those of men. At the current rate of progress, we would need another 28脗聽years before achieving pay equality for women and men in the EU.

The gender pay gap is caused by various factors, among them discrimination and biases between women and men in pay systems. The new European legislation on pay transparency was developed to usher a new era of fairness. It is expected to empower workers to claim their rights by finally giving them access to the information they need and alleviating administrative obstacles. It will also trigger action by companies to address the systemic undervaluation of women's work, and address structural discrimination in pay. Effective implementation is now key.

I stress that equal pay is not only about equal pay for men and women carrying out the same job. It is also about equal pay for work that appears to be different, but in fact requires comparable skills, efforts and responsibilities, and is therefore worth the same.

We will put extra effort into supporting the proper application of the principle of equal pay between men and women for work of equal value in the Member States. We have had the principle of equal pay in our Treaty for 67脗聽years, but it proved not effective in practice, as you know.脗聽The Pay Transparency Directive changes this, as it puts forward tools, mechanisms and obligations to address lingering discrepancies.

We are supporting the development of tools and methodologies for employers to detect and correct any unjustified gender pay differences. A solid methodology to compare jobs of equal value will be essential.脗聽To support this development, the European Institute for Gender Equality is developing a step-by-step toolkit on gender-neutral job evaluation and classification systems, which will be accessible online at the beginning of 2026.

But putting in place pay transparency is, of course, not the end of the story. The European Equal Pay Day reminds us of how much work must be done to address the other causes of the gender pay gap. Among these other causes are the underrepresentation of women in decision-making positions, gender stereotypes about the roles of women and men in society, unequal sharing of domestic and care responsibilities, and the perpetuation of inequalities in pension systems.

I recall that during this Commission's mandate, we addressed several of these other causes, as you know. We ensured other achievements: the adoption of the Directive on gender balance on corporate boards in 2022, and the increase of female representation on the boards of the EU's largest listed companies, will give many qualified women a real chance to reach a top position.

It is, however, not enough to have women in top positions or equally represented. Women must have a safe and supportive environment to thrive professionally and take脗聽on leadership positions within their organisations. The support can include mentoring programs, inclusive and family-friendly work arrangements, and the assurance of a safe work environment free from internal and external harassment.

This goes hand in hand with fighting deeply ingrained stereotypes, which are often used to question women's skills and competencies. Such hostile environments cause many women to leave public office.

We have also put substantial efforts into fighting harmful gender stereotypes. We have developed an EU-wide communication campaign. The campaign tackles gender stereotypes in all areas of life: career choices, sharing of care responsibilities and decision-making. We invite you all to share the campaign material with the hashtag #EndGenderStereotypes.

But if we want to give women an equal opportunity to participate in the labour market, and value our contribution in achieving a competitive European economy, we must create the adequate structures for care responsibilities to be used equally by women and men.

Women continue to bear the brunt of household and caring responsibilities, making them leave the labour market or work less hours. Currently, 7.7脗聽million women are unable to join the labour market due to a lack of available care services, even though they are qualified and even though it is not their choice to not be in the labour market.脗聽But because of these care responsibilities and the lack of care services, they are not in the labour market.

In 2003, the employment rate for men stood at 80.4脗聽% and for women at 70.2脗聽%. The gender employment gap is wider when considering full-time equivalent employment. The share of women in part-time work is much higher: 27.9脗聽% compared to 7.7脗聽% for men in 2023; and even wider among employed women with children: 31.8脗聽%, compared to 5脗聽% in 2023.

This matter is addressed in the Work-Life Balance Directive and the Care Strategy. The work-life balance rules improve the conditions to balance work and family choices to achieve a better sharing of domestic and caring responsibilities between women and men, and to create the conditions for women to enter or remain in the labour market. It does so by designing family leave and flexible working arrangements. We are pursuing work on enforcement to ensure the correct implementation of this Directive in all Member States.

Through the European Care Strategy, we also urge Member States to invest in high芒聙聭quality, affordable and accessible care. Available care services allow both women and men to engage in paid work. Strong public care services not only enhance women's participation in the labour market, but also provide employment.

Finally, we must keep in mind that all the inequalities women face during their working life culminate at old age. In 2023, the gender gap in pensions stood at over 25.4脗聽%. One of the objectives of the Gender Equality Strategy is that women receive fair pensions. The Commission included the gender dimension in our triennial Pension Adequacy Report, prepared together with the Social Protection Committee. These efforts will need to be stepped up and sustained during the coming years.

My mind is at rest looking at this House this evening where I can see many of you who were a great support to the Commission's work. I really thank the Parliament for pushing us harder so that we could achieve what we have so far achieved. I am sure, seeing your faces here, familiar faces, that you will continue doing this, so that we can achieve more and more. Thank you so much for your work.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Hel脙漏ne Fritzon, f脙露r S&D gruppen. 芒聙聯 Fru talman! Tack kommission脙陇r Dalli f脙露r allt det arbete ni har gjort f脙露r j脙陇mst脙陇lldheten under de h脙陇r 脙楼ren.

Hur l脙陇nge ska kvinnor v脙陇nta? Det 脙陇r tredje 脙楼ret i rad som kvinnor i EU tj脙陇nar 脙露ver 12脗聽% mindre 脙陇n m脙陇n, och det motsvarar en och en halv m脙楼nadsl脙露n per 脙楼r. Detta 脙陇r helt oacceptabelt.脗聽Kvinnor dominerar l脙楼gl脙露neyrken och de tar dessutom ofta huvudansvaret f脙露r hem, familj och omsorg. Priset de betalar 脙陇r l脙陇gre l脙露ner och l脙陇gre pensioner.

Det handlar inte om ett val, utan det handlar om strukturella or脙陇ttvisor.脗聽Flickor 脙陇r ofta duktiga i skolan. Kvinnor har h脙露g kompetens som beh脙露vs i b脙楼de samh脙陇lle och f脙露retag. Om vi forts脙陇tter i samma takt som i脗聽dag s脙楼 kommer det att dr脙露ja n脙陇stan en livstid innan vi uppn脙楼r j脙陇mst脙陇lldhet i EU, och det kan vi aldrig acceptera.脗聽Ett av mina barnbarn 脙陇r tio脗聽脙楼r, en flicka som g脙楼r i skolan. Ska jag ber脙陇tta f脙露r henne att det kommer att ta 60脗聽脙楼r innan hon f脙楼r leva i ett j脙陇mst脙陇llt Europa? D脙楼 脙陇r hon pension脙陇r, och s脙楼 l脙陇nge t脙陇nker inte jag v脙陇nta. Vi ska inte acceptera n脙楼got annat 脙陇n att v脙楼ra barn och barnbarn f脙露rtj脙陇nar ett Europa d脙陇r lika arbete ger lika l脙露n.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Margarita de la Pisa Carri脙鲁n, en nombre del Grupo PfE. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidente, se脙卤or脙颅as, nuestras constituciones, as脙颅 como la Carta de los Derechos Fundamentales de la Uni脙鲁n Europea, ya garantizan la igualdad entre hombres y mujeres en materia de empleo, trabajo y retribuci脙鲁n. Ahora, la diferencia de salario entre personas es natural y debe existir, y tiene, casi siempre, una justificaci脙鲁n en el nivel de formaci脙鲁n, experiencia previa, tipo de horario, sector...

Los datos solo confirman la existencia de una importante brecha salarial que se suele olvidar: la de las madres trabajadoras. La Directiva de transparencia retributiva se presenta aqu脙颅 como una herramienta para la igualdad, pero, en realidad, es una herramienta para ahogar en m脙隆s burocracia e inseguridad jur脙颅dica a las empresas y a los emprendedores. Esto se traduce en menos oportunidades laborales para todos, tambi脙漏n para las mujeres.

Lo que es urgente es generar un entorno de prosperidad. Dejen de poner palos en la rueda a las empresas, poniendo a las mujeres como excusa. Si les import脙隆ramos de verdad, dejar脙颅an de hablar tanto por nosotras y querr脙颅an escucharnos.

脗聽脗聽Marlena Mal脛聟g, w imieniu grupy ECR. 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Pani Komisarz! Szanowni Pa脜聞stwo! Dyskryminacja ekonomiczna to temat, kt脙鲁ry ma dwa wymiary 芒聙聯 realny i ideologiczny. Parlament jest miejscem, w kt脙鲁rym wygrywa czasami ta druga opcja. Te same osoby pot脛聶piaj脛聟 dyskryminacj脛聶 zawodow脛聟 kobiet, ale jednocze脜聸nie zmuszaj脛聟 je, na przyk脜聜ad w sporcie, do rywalizacji z osobami transp脜聜ciowymi. Gdzie tu r脙鲁wno脜聸脛聡? Odstawmy ideologi脛聶 na bok. Sp脙鲁jrzmy na problem realnie. Pog脜聜脛聶bianie si脛聶 luki p脜聜acowej to problem systemowy. Nie rozwi脛聟脜录emy go jedn脛聟 czy drug脛聟 regulacj脛聟, kt脙鲁ra zrobi wi脛聶cej krzywdy ni脜录 po脜录ytku.

Czasami tutaj, w Parlamencie, s脜聜yszymy s脜聜owa o losie kobiet w moim kraju. A sp脙鲁jrzmy na fakty. 脜職rednia luka p脜聜acowa w Unii Europejskiej to 12,7, a w Polsce 芒聙聯 7,8, jedna z najni脜录szych. Budow脛聶 tego systemu nale脜录y rozpocz脛聟脛聡 od podstaw, przede wszystkim od zapewnienia elastycznych form pracy, dost脛聶pu do opieki nad dzie脛聡mi, do wzmocnienia regulacji prawnych chroni脛聟cych rodzic脙鲁w, ich r脙鲁wnych praw i obowi脛聟zk脙鲁w, od podnoszenia 脜聸wiadomo脜聸ci spo脜聜ecznej, a tak脜录e jawnego raportowania r脙鲁脜录nic wynagrodze脜聞. Tylko zintegrowane, zdecydowane dzia脜聜ania pozwol脛聟 nam stworzy脛聡 bardziej sprawiedliwy i r脙鲁wny rynek pracy. Szukajmy realnych rozwi脛聟za脜聞. Budujmy uczciwy, sprawiedliwy system oparty na jasnych zasadach.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jana Toom, on behalf of the Renew Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, Commissioner, dear colleagues, the question around the gender pay gap is sometimes the same: how can we encourage women to take part in the labour market? All I hear is 'how much more juice can be squeezed out of these lemons?'.

All across the EU, women shoulder the majority of unpaid work such as cleaning, cooking and caring. Women spend weekly on average 13 hours more than men in unpaid work, while spending less time in paid work. Annually, women work the equivalent of eight full-time weeks more, while earning less.

The situation becomes even worse with an increasing ageing population. This is a societal problem reflected in outdated policies in many countries where there is no early childcare available because it is the norm that mothers stay at home. In many countries, there is limited elderly care and even a legal obligation for the family to take care of the elderly. It is women who pay the price.

What we need is the Member States to invest in care and make it available so that women can have a break and finally work the same amount as their male counterparts and have the same amount of income.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Kira Marie Peter-Hansen, on behalf of the Verts/ALE Group. 芒聙聯 Madam President, I get a sense of joy and nostalgia being in this room today脗聽with all of you thinking of a Parliament that definitely was, but maybe still is. But now I will turn to my speech.

Fru formand! Snart f脙楼r vi en ny Kommission, og selv om ansigterne m脙楼ske er nye, s脙楼 er mange af vores udfordringer de samme. Europas kvinder tjener stadig i gennemsnit 12,7 procent mindre end m脙娄nd. Deres pensioner er i gennemsnit 29 procent mindre, og kvinderne har markant f脙娄rre penge investeret i aktier. Det er uretf脙娄rdigt, og vi skal g脙赂re mere for at sikre 脙赂konomisk ligestilling. Heldigvis har vi vist, at vi kan skabe resultater sammen. Vi har forhandlet Europas ligel脙赂nslov om l脙赂n脙楼benhed. Det betyder, at vi f脙楼r l脙赂ngabet frem i lyset og kan bek脙娄mpe l脙赂ndiskrimination, men samtidig er det ogs脙楼 klart, at det ikke kan l脙赂se alverdens ligestillingsproblemer. Vi skal nemlig finde m脙楼der at lukke pensionsgabet p脙楼 og sikre, at vores formuer bliver mere ligeligt fordelt. I SF er vi klar til at k脙娄mpe den kamp for at sikre mere ligestilling i Europa.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Estrella Gal脙隆n, en nombre del Grupo The Left. 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤or脙颅as, 脗驴saben que la brecha salarial implica que, desde el 15 de noviembre hasta fin de a脙卤o, la inmensa mayor脙颅a de las mujeres est脙隆 trabajando gratis? Significa m脙隆s de un mes y medio sin cobrar cada a脙卤o, un 13脗聽% menos que los hombres que hacen el mismo trabajo. Significa que el patriarcado sigue presente. Significa que seguimos infrarrepresentadas en los puestos de poder. Y significa que seguimos asumiendo los cuidados.

Lo que esconden los datos son mujeres agotadas, discriminadas, incluso explotadas, por un trabajo injustamente remunerado. Mujeres que sienten que no les da la vida, que no les llega el dinero a fin de mes, aunque se dejan la piel cada d脙颅a trabajando y cuidando a los dem脙隆s.

Europa debe liderar una respuesta, un cambio a esta injusta situaci脙鲁n. Y el primer paso tiene que ser el incremento del salario m脙颅nimo, porque reduce la brecha salarial, como ya hemos demostrado en Espa脙卤a, pero tambi脙漏n es necesario acabar con un sistema patriarcal que normaliza esa diferencia salarial, as脙颅 como que las mujeres tengamos que asumir los trabajos m脙隆s precarios y la carga de los cuidados.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Irmhild Bo脙聼dorf, im Namen der ESN-Fraktion. 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, sehr geehrte Damen und Herren! Noch immer脗聽芒聙聯 so sollen Statistiken uns glauben machen脗聽芒聙聯 gibt es einen signifikanten Unterschied zwischen der Bezahlung von Frauen und M脙陇nnern 芒聙聯 das Gender-Pay-Gap. Regelm脙陇脙聼ig wird uns erz脙陇hlt, Frauen seien benachteiligt, weil sie weniger verdienten als M脙陇nner. Schauen wir uns diesen Lohnunterschied einmal genauer an. EU芒聙聭weit verdienen Frauen fast 13脗聽Prozent weniger als M脙陇nner, in Deutschland immerhin noch 6脗聽Prozent weniger.

Warum脗聽芒聙聯 diese Frage stellt sich nat脙录rlich脗聽芒聙聯 ist das so? Frauen arbeiten h脙陇ufiger in Teilzeit als M脙陇nner. Frauen sind weniger risikobereit, sie tummeln sich weniger auf Fort芒聙聭 und Weiterbildungen und in Netzwerken. Tats脙陇chlich bringen sie famili脙陇ren Werten eine h脙露here Wertsch脙陇tzung entgegen, und sie lassen sich nicht f脙录r alles, was sie tun, bezahlen. Wenn wir die viele Zeit, die M脙陇nner auf ihr Weiterkommen verwenden, und die vielen Arbeitsstunden, die sie leisten, zusammenz脙陇hlen w脙录rden, w脙录rden wir vermutlich zu dem Schluss kommen, dass das Gender-Pay-Gap eher in die umgekehrte Richtung besteht und M脙陇nner ein viel h脙露heres Einkommen haben m脙录ssten, als es ihnen tats脙陇chlich gezahlt wird. Wir k脙露nnten den Spie脙聼 aber auch umdrehen und endlich die vielen unbezahlten Stunden, die Frauen f脙录r ihre sogenannte Care芒聙聭Arbeit, in denen sie ihre Kinder und ihre Familie umsorgen, angemessen bezahlen. Ich verspreche Ihnen, das Gender-Pay-Gap w脙陇re dann ganz schnell geschlossen.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽L脙颅dia Pereira (PPE). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, apesar de d脙漏cadas de progresso na Europa, as mulheres ganham hoje, em m脙漏dia, menos 13脗聽% do que os homens. Esta disparidade atinge valores de 19脗聽% na 脙聛ustria ou 18脗聽% na Alemanha. S脙拢o valores chocantes.

Em Portugal, uma mulher ganha menos 12脗聽% do que um homem. Estamos melhor do que a m脙漏dia? Sim. Mas n脙拢o podemos ficar satisfeitos, nem satisfeitas. Pagar menos a uma mulher 脙漏 negar-lhe o que 脙漏 seu por direito, o reconhecimento e o valor do seu trabalho. A luta pela igualdade salarial 脙漏, por isso, uma luta pela dignidade humana, pela independ脙陋ncia econ脙鲁mica e pela justi脙搂a social.

Nat脙隆lia Correia, uma antiga deputada do meu partido, do PSD, e defensora dos direitos das mulheres, escreveu um dia: 脗芦Uma mulher tem de deslumbrar, n脙拢o com conquistas pequenas ou vit脙鲁rias adiadas, mas com um brilho que desafia as normas, que sacode a indiferen脙搂a e transforma o mundo. Porque quando uma mulher brilha, ela n脙拢o ilumina apenas a sua vida. Ela ilumina a de todos 脙聽 sua volta脗禄.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Estelle Ceulemans (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madame la Pr脙漏sidente, chers coll脙篓gues, ce 25脗聽novembre 脙漏tait la Journ脙漏e internationale contre la violence 脙聽 l'脙漏gard des femmes.脗聽Avec les violences physiques, sexuelles, sociales et institutionnelles, l'in脙漏galit脙漏 salariale constitue une autre forme de violence, 脙漏conomique et financi脙篓re celle-l脙聽. Cette m脙陋me violence 脙漏conomique est incontestablement li脙漏e aux violences physiques et sexuelles.

En effet, une femme qui d脙漏pend financi脙篓rement de son partenaire court plus de risques de subir des violences physiques et sexuelles au sein de son couple. L'in脙漏galit脙漏 salariale est donc une discrimination et une violence faite aux femmes, inacceptable en脗聽2024. Elle est 脙漏valu脙漏e 脙聽 seulement 13脗聽% de diff脙漏rence de salaire horaire moyen en Europe. Mais ce chiffre cache des r脙漏alit脙漏s tr脙篓s diff脙漏rentes entre 脙聣tats membres ou entre secteurs professionnels, et les femmes restent tr脙篓s majoritaires dans les emplois 脙聽 bas salaire.

Sans parler de la diff脙漏rence de revenu annuel, cons脙漏quence directe du travail 脙聽 temps partiel, qui concerne essentiellement les femmes et a des cons脙漏quences n脙漏gatives non seulement sur les salaires, mais aussi sur les droits sociaux, comme les pensions, et sur le niveau de carri脙篓re. Tout cela est 脙漏videmment la cons脙漏quence notamment de l'in脙漏galit脙漏 dans la r脙漏partition des charges domestiques. Soyons donc vraiment clairs: ces 13脗聽%, aujourd'hui, sont sous-estim脙漏s par rapport 脙聽 la r脙漏alit脙漏 des femmes et aux vraies in脙漏galit脙漏s salariales.

Il est donc temps d'agir, de garantir un droit soi-disant 脗芦acquis脗禄 depuis le trait脙漏 de Rome de脗聽1958 et de s'attaquer r脙漏ellement 脙聽 cette in脙漏galit脙漏 et 脙聽 cette discrimination envers les femmes.

脗聽脗聽P脙隆l Szekeres (PfE). 芒聙聯 Tisztelt Eln脙露k Asszony! Els脜聭 f脙漏rfi hozz脙隆sz脙鲁l脙鲁k脙漏nt hadd jegyezzem meg, hogy a h脙露lgyeknek igaza van. Sok esetben m脙隆r el is hangzott kor脙隆bban, hogy a k脙录l脙露nbs脙漏g oka az is, hogy a n脜聭k k脙露z脙录l sokan r脙漏szmunkaid脜聭ben dolgoznak, mert a csal脙隆dban v脙漏geznek t脙露bb munk脙隆t, a gyerekeket 脙漏s az id脜聭sek gondoz脙隆s脙隆val is foglalkoznak, ami脙漏rt nem j脙隆r b脙漏rez脙漏s.

Szeretn脙漏k mondani erre egy j脙鲁 magyar p脙漏ld脙隆t. N脙隆lunk van f脜聭脙隆ll脙隆s脙潞 anyas脙隆g, teh脙隆t aki azt v脙隆lasztja, hogy a munka vil脙隆ga helyett 脜聭 ink脙隆bb csal脙隆danyak脙漏nt szeretne 脙漏rv脙漏nyes脙录lni, akkor ez脙漏rt 脜聭 fizet脙漏st kap, ez脙漏rt a nyugd脙颅jalapja is n脜聭. 脗聽脗聽Mariateresa Vivaldini (ECR). 芒聙聯 Signora Presidente, onorevoli colleghi, signora Commissaria, essere donna non pu脙虏 e non deve essere un motivo di discriminazione. Eppure ancora oggi siamo ben lungi da una parit脙聽 sostanziale nei diritti fra uomini e donne.

Il principio di parit脙聽 retributiva 脙篓 sancito dai trattati: ma le donne continuano a guadagnare mediamente il 14% in meno rispetto agli uomini. A maggior ragione oggi, che ci troviamo a vivere un inverno demografico, non possiamo rinunciare alla met脙聽 della nostra forza lavoro.

Le interruzioni di carriera sono dovute a maternit脙聽 e responsabilit脙聽 familiari: tutto ci脙虏 non pu脙虏 essere un fattore discriminante. Una donna lavoratrice 脙篓 spesso anche una madre, che dopo il lavoro si occupa della casa, dei figli, talvolta sola. E va sostenuta anzich脙漏 penalizzata, sia sul fronte della carriera sia per gli aspetti salariali.

Dobbiamo creare regole di partenza che siano uguali per tutti. Le statistiche dicono che le donne hanno in media un'istruzione superiore agli uomini, anche nelle materie STEM ed un'aspettativa di vita pi脙鹿 lunga.

Mi auguro che questo Parlamento, insieme alle altre istituzioni, possa responsabilmente offrire soluzioni concrete per colmare un divario retributivo oggettivamente iniquo e ingiustificato.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Lucia Yar (Renew). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear colleagues, who among us would willingly accept being paid less than another colleague for doing the same work? Of course, no one. Yet, it is an acceptable notion in Europe. For women across the EU, this is everyday reality. They are paid 13脗聽% less than men, on average, for work of the same value. And even though we are hearing that it is a made芒聙聭up problem, the numbers speak for themselves. This difference persists even after accounting for feminisation of some sectors or for working hours. There is only one explanation: the 13脗聽% is the value of discrimination.

We try to address it through legislation. The Pay Transparency Directive is the right way to go 芒聙聯 we need to implement it. But real change will require action from every single one of us. Let's question disparities. Let's demand fairness. And to my fellow women, insist on what is rightfully yours, because equality is a fundamental right and it applies to your paycheque as well.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Katrin Langensiepen (Verts/ALE). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin! Wir Frauen, wir m脙录ssen immer f脙录r unsere Rechte k脙陇mpfen. Es waren unsere Gro脙聼m脙录tter, es waren unsere M脙录tter, jetzt sind es wir. Wenn man sich auf Social Media umschaut, was wir wieder f脙录r Trends haben: Da gibt es die sogenannten脗聽tradwives, wo einem in bunten Farben dargestellt wird, was das Hausfrauendasein doch alles bringt. Da sind 脙录berbezahlte Influencerinnen, die dann darstellen, wie toll Hausarbeit ist.

Das ist aber nicht die Realit脙陇t. Wenn wir 脙录ber den Gendergap in der Bezahlung sprechen, m脙录ssen wir auch 脙录ber die unbezahlte Care-Arbeit sprechen. Dann haben wir vielleicht den Kindergarten, wo ich dann das Kind hinbringen kann. Aber wer organisiert das denn? Wer hat dann am Ende des Tages die Orga-Last, den mental load, zu sagen: 芒聙聻Du musst als Vater dich drum k脙录mmern, und ich bin raus, dass ich hier in diesem Haus parlamentarische Arbeit machen kann芒聙聹. Das muss bezahlt werden. Daf脙录r m脙录ssen wir in der n脙陇chsten Kommission k脙陇mpfen, in der hoffentlich starken Care芒聙聭Strategie.

脗聽脗聽Petras Gra脜戮ulis (ESN). 芒聙聯 Gerbiama Pirmininke, a脜隆 manau, kad moteris turi b脜芦ti gerbtina, gerbiama ir mylima. 脜陆i脜芦r脛聴kit, 9 valandos nakties. 脛聦ia Europos Parlamente nakt脛炉 i脜隆naudojamos moterys. 脗聽脗聽El脜录bieta Katarzyna 脜聛ukacijewska (PPE). 芒聙聯 Pani Przewodnicz脛聟ca! Pani Komisarz! I chc脛聶 powiedzie脛聡 te脜录 z tego miejsca, 脜录e to jest wstyd, 脜录e w XXI wieku kobiety zarabiaj脛聟 blisko 13% mniej ni脜录 m脛聶脜录czy脜潞ni.

Gdy popatrzymy na stanowiska kierownicze, r脙鲁脜录nica p脜聜acowa si脛聶ga nawet 23%. I to pokazuje jasno, 脜录e kobiety wci脛聟脜录 zderzaj脛聟 si脛聶 ze szklanym sufitem, kt脙鲁ry ogranicza ich potencja脜聜 i mo脜录liwo脜聸ci awansu. R脙鲁wno脜聸脛聡 p脜聜ac, prosz脛聶 Pa脜聞stwa, to nie tylko kwestia sprawiedliwo脜聸ci. To inwestycja w lepsz脛聟 przysz脜聜o脜聸脛聡. Badania jasno pokazuj脛聟, 脜录e zmniejszenie r脙鲁脜录nic p脜聜acowych wzmacnia gospodark脛聶, buduje zr脙鲁wnowa脜录ony rynek pracy i podnosi jako脜聸脛聡 脜录ycia ca脜聜ego spo脜聜ecze脜聞stwa. Nie mo脜录emy czeka脛聡 kolejnych dziesi脛聶cioleci, by zobaczy脛聡 zmiany. Kobiety w Europie zas脜聜uguj脛聟 na r脙鲁wne szanse ju脜录 dzisiaj. 脗聽脗聽Lina G脙隆lvez (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, se脙卤ora comisaria, en Europa ahora mismo hay m脙隆s mujeres que obtienen diplomas universitarios que hombres. En algunos pa脙颅ses, desde hace ya dos o tres d脙漏cadas, hay m脙隆s mujeres que hombres con estudios superiores entre la poblaci脙鲁n con edad de trabajar y, a pesar de eso, la brecha salarial es del 12,7脗聽%, favorable no a las mujeres que tienen m脙隆s formaci脙鲁n, sino a los hombres. Y eso solo se refiere al salario por hora. As脙颅 que no hay nada de natural en esto.

Si miramos, adem脙隆s, la brecha de ingresos anuales aumenta y aumenta tambi脙漏n a lo largo de la vida. Y hay estimaciones de que la brecha en las pensiones es del 40脗聽%, algo, por cierto, muy relacionado con la feminizaci脙鲁n de la pobreza.

Las y los socialistas hemos promovido iniciativas como la Directiva sobre transparencia retributiva, que ahora queremos que se aplique a todas las empresas 芒聙聰y no solo a las grandes芒聙聰 y que aborde la minusvaloraci脙鲁n de los sectores feminizados, incluyendo el principio de 脗芦igual salario por trabajo de igual valor脗禄. Pero eso tendr脙隆 solo un alcance limitado si no abordamos tambi脙漏n un reparto equitativo del trabajo dom脙漏stico y de los cuidados no remunerados, porque las mujeres no tenemos ning脙潞n gen de la plancha ni ning脙潞n gen de la escoba, y si garantizamos una red de calidad y accesible de servicios de cuidados y de servicios p脙潞blicos, de educaci脙鲁n y sanidad. Y, para ello, promoveremos el pacto de cuidados que Europa necesita para que abordar la crisis demogr脙隆fica no recaiga exclusivamente en los hombros de las mujeres.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Maria Walsh (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, we speak tonight on the urgent need to address the gender pay gap, and time and time again, discussions about equality seem to echo late at night in an empty Chamber, leaving me with a great sense of frustration. There are 20 colleagues here tonight, representing over 51脗聽% of our population in the EU.

Last mandate we delivered binding pay transparency measures through the directive on pay transparency, but gender equality isn't just about pay, it's about securing women's future. Take pensions for example. Women on average retire with significantly lower pensions compared to men, a direct consequence of the pay disparity faced throughout their careers.

Let's not forget pink tax too. Women pay a higher price for products simply because they are marketed for us as female 芒聙聯 from toiletries to clothing to services like dry cleaning, thousands of euros are hidden in tax that hits the wallets of women across the EU, further widening the financial gap.

We desperately must build on the directive for the thousands of women who are currently setting up their businesses and can't access capital, or the many who are experiencing physical, mental and financial abuse in partnerships but stay to protect their families.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marc Angel (S&D). 芒聙聯 Madam President, dear Commissioner Dalli, a 13脗聽% gender pay gap in our Union is a scandal and is a slap in the face of our core values. And while I welcome EU legislation and in particular the Pay Transparency Directive, we must face the facts: these initiatives will only be successful if there is real political will for change. And I can hear those in this House who will claim that the administrative burden of this legislation is too high 芒聙聯 that's not true 芒聙聯 and it's the EU and not we are responsible for that 芒聙聯 it's the exact opposite.

It is up to our Member States, up to our companies to guarantee gender equality and to ensure the principle of equal pay for equal work. If they had respected our treaties and the Commission recommendations, Europe would not have had to legislate. We all have a responsibility in transforming patriarchy into a Union of equality.

And to those who want to portray this fight as an invention of what they call gender ideologists: equal pay is enshrined in our treaties since 1957. Therefore, they must stop blaming the progressives. They should stop making women and workers feel guilty and rather look at their own inability or unwillingness to guarantee equality. Enough is enough. Let us together put an end to the gender pay gap. And thank you very much, Commissioner Dalli, for all the work you have done.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Zolt脙隆n Tarr (PPE). 芒聙聯 Tisztelt Eln脙露k Asszony! S脜聭t, vannak olyan munkak脙露r脙露k, olyan feladatk脙露r脙露k, ahol a n脜聭k fizet脙漏se 20%-kal is kevesebb, mint a f脙漏rfiak脙漏, jellemz脜聭en az oktat脙隆sban 脙漏s az eg脙漏szs脙漏g脙录gyben. A probl脙漏ma gy脙露ker脙漏hez tartozik az is, hogy egyes orsz脙隆gokban nem biztos脙颅tanak egyenl脜聭, megfelel脜聭 k脙漏pviseletet a n脜聭k sz脙隆m脙隆ra, ezzel is azt a meghaladott 脙漏s hamis k脙漏pet er脜聭s脙颅tve, hogy a n脜聭knek nincsen helye vezet脜聭 poz脙颅ci脙鲁kban. Ez脙漏rt is l脙隆tom azt 脙露r脙露mmel, hogy ezt az 脙录l脙漏s脙录nket is egy h脙露lgy vezeti, 脙漏s hogy ennyi h脙露lggyel lehet脙录nk egy脙录tt itt a Parlamentben, ennyi felel脜聭s h脙露lggyel.

V脙隆ls脙隆ghelyzetben a n脜聭i vezet脜聭k a felel脜聭ss脙漏g els脜聭dleges 脙隆ldozatai, ahogy ez n脙隆lunk t脙露rt脙漏nt febru脙隆rban, amikor a kegyelmi 脙录gy kirobbant. Azt gondolom, hogy 芒聙聻gender pay gap芒聙聺-r脜聭l besz脙漏lve 芒聙聻appreciation gap芒聙聺-r脜聭l, vagyis elismer脙漏si hi脙隆nyr脙鲁l is besz脙漏l脙录nk. Ezen kellene v脙隆ltoztatnunk.

脗聽脗聽Evelyn Regner (S&D). 芒聙聯 Frau Pr脙陇sidentin, sehr geehrte Frau Kommissarin! Ja, nat脙录rlich gibt es sie, die Instrumente, damit wir den uns脙陇glichen Gender-Pay-Gap bek脙陇mpfen. Nat脙录rlich gibt es sie. Wir haben ja auch eines dieser Instrumente beschlossen. Ich sage jetzt noch einmal versp脙陇tet 芒聙聻Danke芒聙聹 an all diejenigen, die das so angepackt haben, denn dieses Instrument ist stark. Deshalb hei脙聼t es jetzt: umsetzen, umsetzen, umsetzen! Das, was auf dem Tisch liegt, sorgf脙陇ltig und gut umsetzen, damit auch wirklich alle sp脙录ren, dass wir diesen Gender-Pay-Gap schlie脙聼en k脙露nnen.

Erst vor vier Tagen war in meiner Heimatstadt Wien der Unequal Pay Day 芒聙聯 also schrecklich, dass wir den noch immer haben; absurd, dass das in diesem Jahrhundert ist. Ich kann nur sagen, wenn wir jetzt sorgf脙陇ltig umsetzen, dann k脙露nnen wir auch die Situation von Frauen und damit auch von M脙陇nnern verbessern. Wenn wir anpacken, was jetzt noch zu tun ist 芒聙聯 n脙陇mlich auch gleichen Lohn f脙录r gleichwertige Arbeit in einen Rechtsakt zu gie脙聼en 芒聙聯, k脙露nnen wir etwas tun. Wenn wir die Care-Arbeit anpacken, wenn wir endlich auch das Thema unbezahlte Arbeit zu einem europ脙陇ischen Thema machen, dann k脙露nnen wir etwas tun. Deshalb sage ich: Unser neues EU芒聙聭Lohntransparenzgesetz soll und wird diesen Tag in die Geschichtsb脙录cher verbannen, und wir werden all diese anderen Themen in diesem Haus mit genau diesem Elan angehen.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Nina Carberry (PPE). 芒聙聯 Madam President, the gender pay gap is a pressing issue right across Europe. Women earn on average 13脗聽% less per hour than men, and this inequality has lifelong consequences.脗聽Women's economic empowerment demands urgent action.

It is our role as lawmakers to foster policies that empower women, especially young women, to become the leaders of tomorrow. Advancing women into leadership roles, enforcing pay transparency and fostering workplace cultures that prioritise inclusion are the key to this.

And while this Parliament has taken close steps to close the gender pay gap, we must continue to expand programmes that promote female entrepreneurship, skills development and STEM participation.

We can and we must do more to empower women in all walks and in all stages of life. There is so much more to be achieved if we put our political weight behind the fight against inequality and discrimination.

脗聽脗聽Elena Sancho Murillo (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, todav脙颅a hoy existe una brecha salarial entre hombres y mujeres y, ante discursos y partidos pol脙颅ticos que niegan este hecho, nosotros aportamos datos.

Las mujeres de la Uni脙鲁n Europea en 2024 siguen ganando, de media, un 13脗聽% menos que los hombres que realizan el mismo trabajo y durante las mismas horas. Esto supone que, desde el 15 de noviembre hasta que acabe este a脙卤o, las mujeres de Europa han dejado de ser remuneradas por su empleo y esto les pasa en cada a脙卤o de su vida laboral.

Desde Europa, debemos seguir trabajando por atajar la brecha salarial de g脙漏nero, pero debemos ir m脙隆s all脙隆. Hay que avanzar en medidas de conciliaci脙鲁n real, en brindar oportunidades a las mujeres para cualquier tipo de trabajo y en la Directiva sobre transparencia retributiva, porque solo as脙颅 conseguiremos la igualdad real, en la que cada persona, independientemente de su g脙漏nero, pueda prosperar y avanzar en una Uni脙鲁n Europea justa e igualitaria.

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen (ECR). 芒聙聯 Arvoisa puhemies, nyt puhutaan sukupuolten v脙陇lisist脙陇 tuloeroista, mutta tarkastellaanpa asiaa rehellisesti. Sukupuolten v脙陇lisi脙陇 tuloeroja ei ole olemassa siin脙陇 muodossa kuin t脙陇脙陇ll脙陇kin annetaan ymm脙陇rt脙陇脙陇.

Ero ihmisten palkkapusseissa ei johdu sukupuolesta vaan yksil脙露iden tekemist脙陇 valinnoista. Miehet ja naiset haluavat itse hakeutua erilaisille aloille. T脙陇m脙陇 on aivan luonnollista, mutta t脙陇m脙陇n p脙陇iv脙陇n woke-ilmastossa t脙陇m脙陇n sanominen 脙陇脙陇neen aiheuttaa jo pahennusta.

Mit脙陇 meid脙陇n pit脙陇isi sitten tehd脙陇? Asettaa sukupuolikiinti脙露t kaikkiin ammatteihinko? Min脙陇 puolustan mieluummin miesten, naisten ja yritysten vapautta valita.

脗聽脗聽Jo脙拢o Oliveira (The Left). 芒聙聯 Senhora Presidente, as mulheres ganham, em m脙漏dia, menos 13脗聽% que os homens na Uni脙拢o Europeia.

Essa disparidade 脙漏 o reflexo da crescente explora脙搂脙拢o laboral como causa de fundo das desigualdades entre mulheres e homens. A solu脙搂脙拢o n脙拢o est脙隆, nem em bonitas proclama脙搂脙碌es de pol脙颅ticas de igualdade, nem em diretivas superficiais se elas, afinal, acabam por consagrar na letra da lei disparidades salariais discriminat脙鲁rias das mulheres.

A solu脙搂脙拢o est脙隆 sim, em pol脙颅ticas que concretizem o direito ao trabalho e ao trabalho com direitos; que valorizem os sal脙隆rios, que deem combate efetivo 脙聽s discrimina脙搂脙碌es das mulheres no trabalho e no emprego; uma efetiva e mais abrangente a脙搂脙拢o pol脙颅tica para a igualdade no trabalho; a garantia da aplica脙搂脙拢o do princ脙颅pio de sal脙隆rio igual para trabalho igual; a capacita脙搂脙拢o das autoridades nacionais competentes na fiscaliza脙搂脙拢o e puni脙搂脙拢o das discrimina脙搂脙碌es e viola脙搂脙拢o dos direitos laborais e o refor脙搂o desses direitos, nomeadamente na contrata脙搂脙拢o coletiva.

Essas s脙拢o as pol脙颅ticas que podem, efetivamente, eliminar as disparidades salariais que penalizam as mulheres.

脗聽脗聽Idoia Mendia (S&D). 芒聙聯 Se脙卤ora presidenta, la igualdad plena sigue siendo un reto democr脙隆tico de primer orden porque afecta a la mitad de la poblaci脙鲁n. La brecha salarial se sigue derivando de h脙隆bitos sociales, pero tambi脙漏n afecta a la negociaci脙鲁n colectiva. Por eso, es fundamental la perspectiva de g脙漏nero en la negociaci脙鲁n colectiva y el impulso a las pol脙颅ticas activas de empleo que fomenten la contrataci脙鲁n de las mujeres en sectores masculinizados. Pero es grave que haya en este mismo Parlamento personas que niegan la misma existencia de la brecha salarial. Por eso, es necesario seguir reforzando los espacios de di脙隆logo con propuestas que ayuden a corregir la desigualdad y a sensibilizar a los agentes sociales y a la ciudadan脙颅a.

Hace m脙隆s de un a脙卤o que entr脙鲁 en vigor la Directiva sobre transparencia retributiva, gracias al trabajo y al empe脙卤o de mi grupo, S&D, y fue un gran paso. Ahora, es el momento de pedir a los Estados miembros que den un paso al frente para verificar su compromiso con su aplicaci脙鲁n plena en todos los Estados de la Uni脙鲁n.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(Koniec zg脜聜osze脜聞 z sali)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Helena Dalli, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 (Start of speech off mic) ... But also as a global leader in tackling multiple challenges, such as keeping the EU's economy robust, resilient and sustainable, where all talents and skills will be needed and will need to be valued.

Women's contribution and value is, of course, no less. While we are making progress on the gender pay gap, we are still far from reaching a zero gap. During this mandate, we took many measures from the adoption of the directives on women on boards and pay transparency to the care strategy and combating stereotypes.

Enforcement of the principle of equal pay is long overdue 芒聙聯 over 60 years since it was included in the Treaty. We expect all the actions taken to bring about change, especially the new directive on pay transparency, which should contribute to fair pay. It is a key step towards a union of equality. I count on all of you here and, of course, those who are not here for your continued support and determination to combat this gender pay gap.

Your commitment to gender equality is invaluable in the development of EU policies. I am sure that my successor will be able to count on your support in the implementation and the raising of awareness about the new rules in the Member States. Workers must know about their rights and employers must understand their obligations.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

脗聽脗聽Cristina Maestre (S&D), por escrito. 芒聙聯 La diferencia salarial media entre hombres y mujeres se sit脙潞a en torno al 13脗聽% 芒聙聰concretamente, un 12,7脗聽% de media芒聙聰 en Europa. Por cada euro que gana un hombre, una mujer gana 0,87脗聽EUR. Esta brecha salarial equivale a una diferencia de aproximadamente un mes y medio de salario al a脙卤o, diferencia que se incrementa si hablamos de ingresos anuales. La brecha va aumentando a lo largo de la vida y supera el 30脗聽% de media, lo que explica en parte la feminizaci脙鲁n de la pobreza en Europa, especialmente de las mujeres mayores.

Los y las socialistas consideramos necesario tanto transformar la Estrategia Europea de Cuidados en un Pacto de Cuidados como trabajar en la implantaci脙鲁n efectiva de la legislaci脙鲁n que puede contrarrestarla, que incluye tanto la Directiva de transparencia retributiva como la Directiva sobre el equilibrio de g脙漏nero en los consejos de administraci脙鲁n, la Directiva sobre conciliaci脙鲁n y la Directiva sobre salario m脙颅nimo. Debemos continuar avanzando en la transversalidad, llevando la igualdad a toda la acci脙鲁n pol脙颅tica de la Uni脙鲁n y a sus Estados miembros.

脗聽脗聽Jagna Marczu脜聜ajtis-Walczak (PPE), na pi脜聸mie. 芒聙聯 Szanowny Panie Komisarzu, Szanowny Panie Wiceprzewodnicz脛聟cy, pa脜聞stwa cz脜聜onkowskie maj脛聟 czas do 2026 roku na wdro脜录enie dyrektywy o transparencji p脜聜ac. To dobry krok, by p脜聜ace kobiet i m脛聶脜录czyzn za ten sam zakres dzia脜聜a脜聞 zr脙鲁wna脜聜y si脛聶, ale nie mo脜录emy te脜录 zapomnie脛聡 o luce ze wzgl脛聶du na p脜聜e脛聡, kt脙鲁ra ma miejsce w 脜聸wiadczeniach emerytalnych.

Ze wzgl脛聶d脙鲁w kulturowych to w脜聜a脜聸nie kobiety s脛聟 najcz脛聶脜聸ciej opiekunkami os脙鲁b z niepe脜聜nosprawno脜聸ciami.

By opiekowa脛聡 si脛聶 swoimi dzie脛聡mi, nierzadko rezygnuj脛聟 one z pracy zawodowej i w zwi脛聟zku z tym maj脛聟 mniejsze do脜聸wiadczenie zawodowe, co przek脜聜ada si脛聶 na ni脜录sze emerytury i poczucie zale脜录no脜聸ci od partnera czy m脛聶脜录a.

Niepe脜聜nosprawno脜聸脛聡 dziecka nie musi by脛聡 przeszkoda do kariery zawodowej, naukowej b脛聟d脜潞 sportowej.

Z mojej perspektywy, perspektywy matki osoby z niepe脜聜nosprawno脜聸ci脛聟, kluczowe jest, by zapewni脛聡 taki system asystencji osobistej, dzi脙漏ki czmu opiekunki-matki b脛聶d脛聟 mog脜聜y p脙鲁j脜聸脛聡 do pracy, p脜聜aci脛聡 podatki i sk脜聜adki emerytalne. To jest krok do niezale脜录nego 脜录ycia ich oraz ich dzieci. Tylko pe脜聜ne wdro脜录enie Konwencji ONZ o Prawach Os脙鲁b z Niepe脜聜nosprawno脜聸ciami gwarantuje nam asystencj脛聶 osobist脛聟, mieszkania wspomagane oraz opiek脛聶 wytchnieniow脛聟.

脗聽

18. Political and humanitarian situation in Mozambique (debate)
Video of the speeches
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Priek脜隆s脛聯d脛聯t脛聛js. 芒聙聯 N脛聛kamais darba k脛聛rt脛芦bas punkts ir debates par Komisijas pazi脜聠ojumu par politisko un humanit脛聛ro st脛聛vokli Mozambik脛聛 (2024/2946(RSP)).

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Helena Dalli, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, now from the gender pay gap we go to Mozambique.脗聽Following the elections of 9脗聽October, the situation in Mozambique remains highly volatile and marked by violence and disorder.

Before going deeper into the subject, let me salute the work and the efforts of honourable脗聽Member Ballar脙颅n as Chief Observer of the EU Electoral Observation Mission, of honourable Member脗聽L脙鲁pez-Ist脙潞riz White, and of all the Members that were part of the European Parliament's delegation in the Mission Mozambique.脗聽Your efforts and pertinent observations have been instrumental for us to follow the situation and give us a deeper understanding of the facts.

So, allow me to give a few indications about our general partnership with Mozambique, which is a very positive and far-reaching one, with considerable support when it comes to development, humanitarian aid, peace-building, as well as security to tackle the Cabo脗聽Delgado insurgency.

The EU has contributed to providing security in the north of the country through the EU Military Assistance Mission on the ground, and the European Peace Facility. The support is essential to fight the insurgency and the risks of spill-over effects in the region, and ultimately to create possibilities for the return to a normal life for the local people.

Thanks to our cooperation with Mozambique, we have established close relations with the Mozambican Government. The EU has become a valued partner, which can speak frankly and openly, and this is why, among trusted partners, we cannot remain silent in the current situation.

Since the election of 9脗聽October, we are witnessing a worrying spread of violence, looting, vandalism and desperation. The brutality of the repression from the state apparatus and the police has exacerbated the situation. We deplore the considerable number of fatalities over the past weeks, and we also condemn the assassination of two opposition politicians on 19脗聽October, and we demand clarity on the facts and a full investigation.

In our public communication and numerous statements, the EU's messaging has been very clear. It is imperative for all sides to maintain restraint and orderly conduct. While it is important that everybody's voice is heard, harsh rhetoric and the disproportionate use of force and violence from all sides should be avoided at all costs.

I stress that the EU and the Electoral Observation Mission are not taking any sides, and only act on the basis of principles as an important partner and friend of Mozambique, solely in the interest of the Mozambican people. Their will has to be fully respected.脗聽It is important to reiterate this, as the electoral process is far from finished, as you know. We look forward to the final announcement of election results by the Constitutional Council. Until the final result, the EU will remain vigilant that the people's will is upheld and that human rights are respected.

In the medium term, we will strive for a conducive environment for democratic reform and addressing the Electoral Observation Mission's recommendations. We will work with Mozambique in the most constructive way, and in full respect of the EU's interests and values.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽H脙漏lder Sousa Silva, em nome do Grupo PPE. 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, Senhora Comiss脙隆ria, Caros Colegas, enquanto membro da Miss脙拢o de Observa脙搂脙拢o Eleitoral do Parlamento Europeu 脙聽s elei脙搂脙碌es gerais de Mo脙搂ambique, testemunhei in loco a determina脙搂脙拢o e, eu direi at脙漏, a alegria do povo mo脙搂ambicano em exercer o seu direito democr脙隆tico ao voto. Contudo, 脙漏 com grande preocupa脙搂脙拢o que acompanho os acontecimentos p脙鲁s-eleitorais, marcados por tens脙碌es, confrontos, centenas de feridos, algumas dezenas de mortos e resultados finais ainda n脙拢o validados.

A viol脙陋ncia nega, claramente, os princ脙颅pios e os valores democr脙隆ticos. 脙聣 crucial que todas as partes envolvidas respeitem o Estado de direito. E, congratulo芒聙聭me tamb脙漏m com a disponibilidade da comunidade de pa脙颅ses de l脙颅ngua portuguesa para facilitar o di脙隆logo entre as partes.

A hist脙鲁ria tamb脙漏m nos mostra que a paz 脙漏 poss脙颅vel em Mo脙搂ambique, mesmo em circunst脙垄ncias dif脙颅ceis, como 脙漏 o caso.

Termino desejando que Mo脙搂ambique reencontre o caminho da paz e da democracia que o seu povo anseia e merece.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Francisco Assis, em nome do Grupo S&D. 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, Senhora Comiss脙隆ria, quero come脙搂ar por saudar o povo mo脙搂ambicano. Um povo que praticamente n脙拢o conhece outra realidade que n脙拢o a pobreza e a viol脙陋ncia. A viol脙陋ncia do colonialismo no passado; a viol脙陋ncia das mil脙颅cias isl脙垄micas sobre as popula脙搂脙碌es do norte do pa脙颅s; a viol脙陋ncia do extrativismo e das for脙搂as de seguran脙搂a privatizadas por multinacionais; a viol脙陋ncia policial que reprime manifesta脙搂脙碌es e que por vezes mata manifestantes e at脙漏 opositores pol脙颅ticos; a viol脙陋ncia da fome e da falta de cuidados b脙隆sicos; a viol脙陋ncia de n脙拢o ter um futuro de prosperidade no horizonte, apesar dos seus cobi脙搂ados recursos naturais.

Mo脙搂ambique tem sido um pa脙颅s pouco aud脙颅vel na cena internacional. Apelo, por isso, 脙聽 Uni脙拢o Europeia para olhar mais ami脙潞de para este pa脙颅s. Para organizar uma resposta internacional ainda mais adequada ao terrorismo isl脙垄mico que o assola h脙隆 oito anos. Para exigir investiga脙搂脙碌es independentes 脙聽s viol脙陋ncias de que ele 脙漏 v脙颅tima, doa a quem doer. Para responsabilizar legalmente empresas europeias que, direta ou indiretamente, o violentem. Para exigir o respeito pelo seu voto democr脙隆tico. Para exigir justi脙搂a eleitoral. Para dar alguma esperan脙搂a a este grande povo que 脙漏 o povo mo脙搂ambicano.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Gy脙露rgy H脙露lv脙漏nyi, on behalf of the PfE Group. 芒聙聯 Mr President, dear colleagues, as a member of the European Parliament's election observation delegation, I had the opportunity to see elections in Mozambique were forward芒聙聭looking, with a new generation entering into power. However, the discrepancies in voting registration and tabulation have led to severe demonstrations with really tragic consequences.

The current developments are the result of the lack of security, weak institutions and mistrust. This happens when the country contains only traces of statehood. In a country where near half a century the same party is ruling, this certainly calls for strengthening the democratic transition. The newly elected political leadership has a responsibility to restore the trust, security and strengthen the institutions.

Mozambique has a huge potential for development, with the third largest natural gas resource in Africa. However, without security, the people of the country will not ever benefit from it. The political instability favours only extremists, as we see in Cabo Delgado. Here, the Islamist extremists attacked 27 villages in February. The EU can and must be a partner of finding the solution but cannot lead instead of the Mozambicans.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Sebastian Tynkkynen, on behalf of the ECR Group. 芒聙聯 Mr President, elections were filled with electoral fraud, including ballot tampering, voter suppression and restricting press freedom, all conducted by the ruling party. After the elections, violence has escalated to a point where members of the opposition parties have been murdered. This is the reality of politics in Mozambique.

Meanwhile, the country faces another challenge in the north, where a jihadist insurgent group with links to ISIS is killing and displacing people from their homes. This situation in Mozambique highlights to us what we should fight against globally. We must fight against terrorist organisations like ISIS and Hamas. Where these kind of organisations have set foot, we are not going to see flourishing societies.

Besides this, we must preserve democracy by standing against anti-democratic countries like China, Russia and Iran, which are promoting their ideologies globally. If their systems win, the world loses. Any amount of development aid is not going to help at that point. We would just witness countries ending up like Mozambique 芒聙聯 countries in chaos.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ana Vasconcelos, em nome do Grupo Renew. 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, Comiss脙隆ria, Caros Colegas, nas 脙潞ltimas sete semanas, Mo脙搂ambique tem vivido os momentos mais violentos da sua hist脙鲁ria recente.

Por contestarem a legitimidade das elei脙搂脙碌es de 9 de outubro 芒聙聯 de que h脙隆 ind脙颅cios fundados de graves irregularidades 芒聙聯, os mo脙搂ambicanos t脙陋m sido sujeitos a uma forte repress脙拢o violenta dos seus direitos. Este tipo de conduta 脙漏 inaceit脙隆vel e exige uma resposta firme por parte da comunidade internacional.

N脙拢o basta condenarmos a viol脙陋ncia e apelarmos 脙聽 defesa do Estado de direito, a Uni脙拢o Europeia deve exigir uma investiga脙搂脙拢o independente para apurar viola脙搂脙碌es de direitos humanos, bem como as alegadas irregularidades eleitorais.

N脙拢o podemos ignorar os relatos de homic脙颅dio e agress脙拢o de adultos e crian脙搂as levados a cabo, seja por for脙搂as policiais, seja pelas for脙搂as de resist脙陋ncia pol脙颅tica. E temos de nos certificar que as viola脙搂脙碌es dos mais elementares direitos democr脙隆ticos dos mo脙搂ambicanos, desde o direito ao voto ao direito de protesto, s脙拢o investigados.

Finalmente, a Uni脙拢o Europeia deve tamb脙漏m monitorizar a utiliza脙搂脙拢o dos fundos europeus destinados 脙聽 paz em Mo脙搂ambique. Qualquer suspeita de desvio mina a nossa credibilidade e fortalece os atores pol脙颅ticos que apostam no retrocesso democr脙隆tico nesta regi脙拢o e no resto do mundo, nomeadamente a China e a R脙潞ssia.

脙聣 a democracia mo脙搂ambicana que est脙隆 em jogo, mas n脙拢o s脙鲁. A desestabiliza脙搂脙拢o da regi脙拢o abre portas aos inimigos da democracia liberal europeia...

(O presidente retira a palavra 脙聽 oradora)

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marie Toussaint, au nom du groupe Verts/ALE. 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, chers coll脙篓gues, depuis les 脙漏lections du 9脗聽octobre, le Mozambique conna脙庐t une immense vague de r脙漏pression qui doit 脙陋tre condamn脙漏e.

Une autre vague de violence inou脙炉e a frapp脙漏 le pays et concerne directement l'Union europ脙漏enne. 脙聙 l'脙漏t脙漏脗聽2021, plusieurs centaines de personnes ont 脙漏t脙漏 enferm脙漏es dans des conteneurs, priv脙漏es d'eau, affam脙漏es, battues, asphyxi脙漏es et tortur脙漏es. Des femmes ont 脙漏t脙漏 soumises 脙聽 des agressions sexuelles r脙漏p脙漏t脙漏es, des b脙漏b脙漏s ont 脙漏t脙漏 d脙漏membr脙漏s, des milliers de familles ont 脙漏t脙漏 d脙漏plac脙漏es de force. Ce bain de sang a 脙漏t脙漏 perp脙漏tr脙漏 par l'arm脙漏e mozambicaine op脙漏rant dans un site gazier g脙漏r脙漏 par la plus grande entreprise p脙漏tro-gazi脙篓re europ脙漏enne, j'ai nomm脙漏 Total Energy. Total savait, mais a laiss脙漏 faire.

Alors je vous pose la question: est-ce vraiment cette 脙漏conomie-l脙聽, qui s脙篓me le d脙漏sespoir et le chaos 脙聽 travers le monde, que nous souhaitons laisser prosp脙漏rer depuis l'Europe? Ne c脙漏dons pas 脙聽 Pouyann脙漏 et consorts, qui nous demandent de d脙漏truire nos normes sociales et environnementales. Dans l'urgence, demandons, sur cet 脙漏v脙漏nement tragique, une enqu脙陋te internationale ind脙漏pendante susceptible de mettre au clair les responsabilit脙漏s et de condamner celles et ceux qui ont 脙漏t脙漏 coupables de telles atrocit脙漏s.

脗聽脗聽Catarina Martins, em nome do Grupo The Left. 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, as elei脙搂脙碌es mo脙搂ambicanas de 9 de outubro n脙拢o foram justas, nem livres nem transparentes. A observa脙搂脙拢o eleitoral da Uni脙拢o Europeia relata problemas nos cadernos eleitorais, na campanha e na integridade dos resultados.

Foi precisamente o an脙潞ncio de resultados sem credibilidade que despoletou os protestos populares que temos assistido. Seguiu-se uma repress脙拢o injustific脙隆vel e inaceit脙隆vel. Sessenta e sete pessoas mortas, incluindo dez crian脙搂as, centenas de feridos e mais de 2 000 detidos, incluindo menores.

O presidente de Mo脙搂ambique anunciou conversa脙搂脙碌es para uma sa脙颅da desta crise. A reuni脙拢o de hoje, infelizmente, n脙拢o respondeu 脙聽s expectativas.

Garantir a transpar脙陋ncia eleitoral e parar a repress脙拢o s脙拢o passos essenciais que a Uni脙拢o Europeia deve apoiar no respeito pela autodetermina脙搂脙拢o e soberania mo脙搂ambicanas. Deve tamb脙漏m reconhecer a grave situa脙搂脙拢o humanit脙隆ria 芒聙聯 incluindo a fome 芒聙聯, e refor脙搂ar, assim, o apoio 脙聽 popula脙搂脙拢o mo脙搂ambicana.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Antonio L脙鲁pez-Ist脙潞riz White (PPE). 芒聙聯 Mr President, last month I led the European Parliament electoral observation mission to Mozambique. I would like to thank my colleagues that participated in that delegation and also the Chief of the European Mission, Ms Laura Ballar脙颅n, my colleague, for the very good collaboration that we had there.

We were welcomed by kind people hopeful to overcome a very challenging social and security context. As Europeans, we have responsibility in Mozambique. Unfortunately, today we still do not have the official results of the elections. The irregularities observed during the electoral process and the lack of transparency from the electoral administration, which is putting into question the integrity of the results, is fuelling social unrest, violence and has already caused dozens of deaths. We must keep urging all parties to exercise restraint.

Moreover, the Constitutional Court must fulfil its mandate with responsibility so that the voice of the people will be respected. We have to do this, and it's our responsibility to support the Mozambican people, and especially the young generations that are also speaking in these elections. They voted for that in their aspirations for democracy, safety and prosperity.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Laura Ballar脙颅n Cereza (S&D). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, como chefe da Miss脙拢o de Observa脙搂脙拢o Eleitoral para Mo脙搂ambique, considero fundamental que o resultado das elei脙搂脙碌es de 9 de outubro seja reflexo da vontade expressa nas urnas.

Embora a campanha e o dia eleitoral tenham decorrido de forma relativamente pac脙颅fica, as irregularidades observadas pela nossa miss脙拢o tornaram-se catalisadoras da viol脙陋ncia que o pa脙颅s tem vivido nas 脙潞ltimas semanas com o resultado tr脙隆gico de, pelo menos, 50 mortes.

Infelizmente, o an脙潞ncio dos resultados preliminares pela CNE n脙拢o dissipou as nossas preocupa脙搂脙碌es.

A fim de assegurar a integridade e transpar脙陋ncia no processo eleitoral, devolver a confian脙搂a dos mo脙搂ambicanos nas institui脙搂脙碌es p脙潞blicas e reduzir as tens脙碌es p脙潞blicas no pa脙颅s: 1. Instamos o Conselho Constitucional a analisar os recursos apresentados. 2. Pedimos 脙聽 CNE a publica脙搂脙拢o de resultados desagregados por mesas de voto. 3. Reclamamos que o exerc脙颅cio do direito de manifesta脙搂脙拢o pac脙颅fica seja respeitado, que o uso excessivo da for脙搂a seja evitado e que todas as partes se abstenham de usar ret脙鲁rica inflamat脙鲁ria.

Finalmente, considero essencial que as autoridades investiguem e levem perante a Justi脙搂a os respons脙隆veis pelos assassinatos de Elvino Dias e Paulo Guambe.

脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Tiago Moreira de S脙隆 (PfE). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, Mo脙搂ambique 脙漏 um Estado soberano, cuja independ脙陋ncia deve merecer o maior respeito e apoio de Portugal e da Uni脙拢o Europeia.

O pa脙颅s enfrenta hoje a dura realidade de uma crise humanit脙隆ria e de seguran脙搂a persistente.

Desde 2017, o terrorismo em Cabo Delgado j脙隆 causou mais de 4 000 mortos e for脙搂ou mais de 1 milh脙拢o de pessoas a abandonar as suas casas.

Apesar de melhorias recentes, muitos mo脙搂ambicanos enfrentam a fome e a falta de acesso a cuidados b脙隆sicos, agravados por cortes na ajuda humanit脙隆ria, como os anunciados pelo Programa Mundial de Alimenta脙搂脙拢o. A capital, Maputo, tamb脙漏m sofre com uma onda de tumultos pol脙颅ticos que se juntou 脙聽 criminalidade dirigida, incluindo o rapto de empres脙隆rios, muitos deles cidad脙拢os portugueses.

Este cen脙隆rio refor脙搂a a urg脙陋ncia de apoiar Mo脙搂ambique atrav脙漏s de mais ajuda humanit脙隆ria e com programas de coopera脙搂脙拢o delegada financiados pela Uni脙拢o Europeia. Desenvolvimento e seguran脙搂a andam de m脙拢os dadas.

Recordemos que Mo脙搂ambique 脙漏 mais do que um lugar de dificuldades. 脙聣 um pa脙颅s de resili脙陋ncia e esperan脙搂a. Como escreveu Mia Couto: 脗芦Quero morar numa cidade onde se sonha com chuva. Num mundo onde chover 脙漏 a maior felicidade. E onde todos chovemos.脗禄.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Ana Miguel Pedro (PPE). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, Mo脙搂ambique e Portugal partilham n脙拢o s脙鲁 uma l脙颅ngua, mas tamb脙漏m uma hist脙鲁ria que nos chama 脙聽 responsabilidade de agir em solidariedade.

A crise pol脙颅tica 脙漏 apenas uma pe脙搂a num cen脙隆rio muito mais devastador. Milhares de mo脙搂ambicanos enfrentam uma crise humanit脙隆ria, consequ脙陋ncia de uma combina脙搂脙拢o de fatores naturais 芒聙聯 como ciclones e inunda脙搂脙碌es 芒聙聯, aliados a uma escala de ataques terroristas que espalham inseguran脙搂a pelo territ脙鲁rio.

Desde 2017, o norte de Mo脙搂ambique 脙漏 palco de uma brutalidade de ataques terroristas com liga脙搂脙拢o ao Estado Isl脙垄mico. Mais de meio milh脙拢o de pessoas foram deslocadas, metade delas crian脙搂as. Sabemos que o Mecanismo Europeu de Apoio 脙聽 Paz, originalmente destinado a auxiliar pa脙颅ses africanos, tem sido cada vez mais redirecionado para responder 脙聽 guerra na Ucr脙垄nia. Compreendemos a necessidade, mas rejeitamos crit脙漏rios duplos: se apoiemos 脙聽s for脙搂as armadas ucranianas com equipamento letal para combater uma agress脙拢o injustificada, ent脙拢o devemos ter a mesma determina脙搂脙拢o em capacitar Mo脙搂ambique para enfrentar o terrorismo que devasta comunidades inteiras.

Mo脙搂ambique 脙漏 um pa脙颅s de luta, resili脙陋ncia e esperan脙搂a. Mas at脙漏 mesmo os mais fortes precisam de aliados. Que os mo脙搂ambicanos, ao olharem para n脙鲁s vejam, n脙拢o um continente distante, mas um irm脙拢o solid脙隆rio.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marit Maij (S&D). 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, Commission, despite Mozambique's abundant natural resources, 73脗聽% of the population lives in poverty; 670脗聽000 people are displaced by armed conflict. Young Mozambicans calling for change are met with violence and human rights violations. Forty people have died in the post-election violence. Internet restrictions, including blockades of social media platforms, constitute violations of freedom of speech and access to information, and affects young people disproportionately in their livelihoods.

We need to stand with the young Mozambicans fighting for common values of democracy and prosperity, including the right to protest and to peacefully assemble.

Therefore, I ask the Commission, can you commit to investing in programmes with a main priority to advance the position of citizens? Will you sanction those committing human rights violations under the Global Human Rights Sanctions Regime? Will you refrain from supporting investments in Cabo Delgado, which violate the social and economic rights of the Mozambican population?

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Rody Tolassy (PfE). 芒聙聯 Monsieur le Pr脙漏sident, chers coll脙篓gues, alors que la province de Cabo Delgado au Mozambique sombre dans l'horreur, avec 70脗聽000脗聽d脙漏plac脙漏s en quelques semaines, des villages d脙漏truits et des enfants arrach脙漏s 脙聽 leur avenir, que propose l'Union europ脙漏enne? Rebaptiser une mission et allouer 14脗聽millions脗聽d'euros suppl脙漏mentaires pour des formations militaires d脙漏j脙聽 insuffisantes. Mais o脙鹿 est l'efficacit脙漏? O脙鹿 sont les r脙漏sultats?

Depuis脗聽2021, des millions d'euros ont 脙漏t脙漏 investis et, pour autant, la situation ne cesse de s'aggraver. Pendant ce temps, les besoins humanitaires, eux, restent financ脙漏s 脙聽 hauteur de seulement 17脗聽%. Ce manque de coh脙漏rence entre les discours et les actions est une insulte aux populations locales et un gaspillage des ressources europ脙漏ennes.

Alors que l'aide humanitaire est laiss脙漏e 脙聽 l'abandon, combien de temps encore l'Union continuera-t-elle 脙聽 investir massivement dans des missions militaires sans r脙漏soudre les causes profondes de ces crises? La cr脙漏dibilit脙漏 de l'Europe et la dignit脙漏 des peuples sont en jeu.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽L脙颅dia Pereira (PPE). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, os mo脙搂ambicanos sabem decidir o seu destino. Confiam na for脙搂a do di脙隆logo, tomam a脙搂脙碌es, transformam. S脙拢o um povo irm脙拢o que j脙隆 superou desafios dif脙颅ceis no passado, conquistou a paz ap脙鲁s anos traum脙隆ticos de guerra civil e, estou certa, ser脙隆 capaz de ultrapassar o momento delicado que atravessa.

Na Uni脙拢o Europeia, devemos contribuir para promover o di脙隆logo entre todas as for脙搂as pol脙颅ticas. Somente atrav脙漏s de conversas abertas e construtivas podemos encontrar solu脙搂脙碌es duradouras que beneficiem toda a popula脙搂脙拢o, garantam a democracia e devolvam a Mo脙搂ambique as condi脙搂脙碌es para o seu desenvolvimento.

As elei脙搂脙碌es de 9 de outubro representam um marco importante, mas 脙漏 essencial que os resultados sejam apurados com transpar脙陋ncia e justi脙搂a e que a publica脙搂脙拢o dos resultados desagregados, tal como reclamado pela Uni脙拢o Europeia, seja consagrada.

Devemos empenhar-nos ativamente em contribuir para que, atrav脙漏s do di脙隆logo, seja poss脙颅vel restabelecer um clima de tranquilidade, fundamental para o desenvolvimento e para a prosperidade de Mo脙搂ambique.

Os mo脙搂ambicanos t脙陋m a for脙搂a e a determina脙搂脙拢o para moldar o seu futuro. A sua resili脙陋ncia e esp脙颅rito de uni脙拢o s脙拢o a chave para um Mo脙搂ambique melhor.

脗聽脗聽

Catch-the-eye procedure

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Jo脙拢o Oliveira (The Left). 芒聙聯 Senhor Presidente, os factos confirmam o desprop脙鲁sito do agendamento deste debate.

Segundo diversas entidades, as elei脙搂脙碌es gerais em Mo脙搂ambique decorreram, em geral, de forma calma e pac脙颅fica.

Os resultados anunciados pela Comiss脙拢o Nacional de Elei脙搂脙碌es est脙拢o a ser sujeitos 脙聽 valida脙搂脙拢o e promulga脙搂脙拢o pelo Conselho Constitucional. Apesar disso, um dos candidatos derrotados autoproclamou-se vencedor. Apelou ao confronto e 脙聽 paralisa脙搂脙拢o da atividade econ脙鲁mica no pa脙颅s, rejeitando os apelos ao di脙隆logo e ao recurso 脙聽s inst脙垄ncias pr脙鲁prias para contestar os resultados.

Os sequentes atos de viol脙陋ncia provocaram dezenas de mortos e centenas de feridos, assim como vandalismo, destrui脙搂脙拢o e saque de bens p脙潞blicos e privados, ataques e amea脙搂as a cidad脙拢os.

Associamo-nos 脙聽 ampla condena脙搂脙拢o destes injustific脙隆veis atos, expressa por parte das autoridades, for脙搂as pol脙颅ticas e sociedade mo脙搂ambicana, assim como aos apelos ao di脙隆logo, no respeito do normal funcionamento das institui脙搂脙碌es democr脙隆ticas mo脙搂ambicanas.

Solidarizamo-nos com o povo mo脙搂ambicano, instando ao respeito pela soberania e independ脙陋ncia da Rep脙潞blica de Mo脙搂ambique, rejeitando quaisquer inger脙陋ncias externas nas decis脙碌es que s脙鲁 ao povo mo脙搂ambicano e 脙聽s suas institui脙搂脙碌es democr脙隆ticas cabe tomar.

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽脗聽

(End of catch-the-eye procedure)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽

脗聽脗聽Helena Dalli, Member of the Commission. 芒聙聯 Mr脗聽President, I would like to thank the honourable Members for this very rich conversation tonight. This debate exemplifies the complexity of the current situation in Mozambique.

We will have to wait for the final proceedings of the Constitutional Council and to have a new administration in place in order to be able to assess how to shape the future of our relations. Our long-standing and fruitful relation with Mozambique allows the EU to have a credible voice. We can say to our Mozambican friends very clearly that the EU demands reform, answers to the desire of change by youth, upholding democratic values and human rights, and maintaining the element of trust and good cooperation which is so important for the EU-Mozambique relationship. Our intention is to continue supporting Mozambique and its people. We want to remain good friends and strong partners. We will continue engaging with Mozambique for the good of the Mozambican people, and to strengthen democratic institutions and civil liberties. The EU's intention is also to continue the integrated approach for Cabo Delgado, with development, humanitarian aid, peace-building and security measures and focus on the well-being of the people.

Any kind of recalibrations in our relationship, of course, will have to be assessed in light of the current and future events and it is way too early to say this now, as I think you can all well understand.

Written Statements (Rule 178)

脗聽
脗聽脗聽
脗聽		脗聽

脗聽脗聽Marta Temido (S&D), por escrito. 芒聙聯 Desde o in脙颅cio da crise p脙鲁s-eleitoral, Mo脙搂ambique vive dias de desrespeito pelos direitos c脙颅vicos e pol脙颅ticos, com repress脙拢o policial violenta dos protestos de rua,脗聽deten脙搂脙碌es arbitr脙隆rias de ativistas e jornalistas e limita脙搂脙碌es do acesso 脙聽 Internet. Contam-se dezenas de mortos e milhares de feridos. A crise econ脙鲁mica continua a acentuar-se, num pa脙颅s onde dois ter脙搂os da popula脙搂脙拢o vive abaixo da linha de pobreza. Mais de 30 dias depois das elei脙搂脙碌es, os resultados oficiais ainda n脙拢o s脙拢o conhecidos.

Por isso, neste debate, quero, sobretudo, deixar um apelo. Um apelo para a pacifica脙搂脙拢o pol脙颅tica.

脙聣 urgente que o Servi脙搂o Europeu para a A脙搂脙拢o Externa e os Estados-Membros (incluindo o Governo portugu脙陋s) instem as autoridades nacionais a p脙麓r termo imediato ao uso desproporcional da resposta policial, gerindo os protestos de acordo com as obriga脙搂脙碌es internacionais de Mo脙搂ambique em mat脙漏ria de direitos humanos. Que instem as autoridades nacionais a divulgar, com urg脙陋ncia, os resultados eleitorais e a publicar, transparentemente, os resultados desagregados por mesa de voto. E que instem todos os partidos pol脙颅ticos a abster-se de fomentar a viol脙陋ncia e a procurar a via do di脙隆logo para devolver a Mo脙搂ambique um futuro com esperan脙搂a.
